input: rating 5.0. NIce addition to the train set Right price and on time delivery. The train tracks are a perfect match for the train set and snap together easily. Alignment of the track height at the joints needs to be carefully done to avoid derailing the very light weight rail cars, but once set up properly, they are great.
rating 5.0. Fit and cheaper Fit in with the set that I got from one of the biggest big box and cheaper.  Shipping is fast too.  Same product at better price.

rating 4.0. exactly as expected I have the e-z track already and these extensions worked great.  You don't have to deal with bedrolls and uneven floors as much, the only downside is that the plastic track bed transfers more track noise to the material below, so I still put track roll under the plastic.

rating 5.0. A Kid At Heart I am a retired man recently widowed. While going thru my wife's belongings I came across my HO steam engine and my Bachman Spectrum passenger cars. I got excited and decided to put it under the Christmas tree this year. I tested it to see if it still runs and found that the only track I had was 2 9" straight tracks which was enough to move the engine back and forth. So now I needed track.. I never thought Amazon would sell track and sure enough they do. I liked what I saw in the picture and the reviews were good. I liked the idea about then snapping together and can be used on carpet without the track moving. I took a chance and ordered several packs of the 22" radius curved, some 9" straight and the terminal rerailer. First good part is it was on PRIME and I did receive my order in 2 days. I put them together and was amazed how easily the snapped together and just as easy to take them apart.
I wanted to run my train around the Christmas tree on the carpet. (Every Christmas tree should have a train either under it or beside it.) I ran the train at full speed and so happy to see the track did not move on the carpet, it lays flat on the carpet and the train runs evenly on the track (no wavy up and down ride where the tracks connect together. It is a tight solid fit. The result is the tracks lay on the carpet like they were nailed down onto a platform. Then I was really excited and got the Christmas tree and set it up inside the circle. Then I went and bought more straight track so more or less eliminate a circle. Well, to make a long story short, My tree is up, the train running, and all the holiday decorations are on display - even though Thanksgiving Day 2012 was still 4 days in the future.
If you older and old kids (my age) are going to get your train out and running again and you find that your track is no longer usable, the Bachmann Snap-Fit track is the only way to go. One final plus that I must mention: Amazon, thank you very much for paying for my track with the points on my Amazon credit card and having it here in two days with Prime. You just have to love when Amazon buys nice things for you. To all of you that are reading my review - Have a very Merry and Safe Christmas and remember WHOSE birthday is celebrated on December 25. (( It's ok to sing Happy Birthday, too. You sing it to your kids so why not to the Lord?))

rating 4.0. Team effort assembly The help of my 6 and 8 year old grandchildren was required to put this track together.  They were able to show me how to make this work.  Assembly assisted with my "bonding" efforts a lot.  I'm sure that I could not have put this track together without their engineering skills.  I should have ordered more track and several variations and will do so after Christmas.  Maybe then the grandkids will let me play with the train.

rating 2.0. bumpy tracks Bought for my 5 yr old grandson.  Connecting tracks together is very difficult. The track guides actually move when trying to re-assemble. Needed a plier to pull them back to even, yet, there are stil minor gaps on the track which sometimes derails the train.

rating 1.0. WARNING: CONTAINS LEAD; KEEP OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN In tiny print at the bottom of the package, partially hidden by the product itself is: "WARNING THIS PRODUCT CONTAINS LEAD. MAY BE HARMFUL IF EATEN OR CHEWED. KEEP OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN." The only way to do that is to return it, or better yet don't buy it.

I find it hard to believe they would sell a product that many people purchase for children that contains lead, but this is what it says. If you are concerned about lead and lead dust, I hope this provides the disclose that is lacking from the product description.

rating 2.0. Speed Control failure I bought 2 bachmann train sets for Christmas.  Both speed controllers failed within 1 day.  One was returned to Bachmann for repair with a 4-6 week wait.  With 2 failures, it would appear to be a manufacturing issue.  I don't understand why new controllers could not be sent out immediatley.

rating 3.0. Perfect for a kids starter trainset For the most part, these are very suitable tracks for the purpose we are using them (Kids train set that they put together on occassion, not a permanent display setup).

I was hoping with the more solid nature of the track I would have an easier time allowing the kids to play with the train set on the carpet, we still have severe problems with derailments, and even the engine itself getting stuck.

Setup is quick and easy, but you still need to make sure you take great care in ensuring that the rails line up properly and are linked as well.

These tracks do not look very "realistic" for the avid train enthusiast. But for a kids starter train set, these are perfect and will help younger imaginations with smaller hands be more "hands on" with setup. (My four year old can put them together)

rating 5.0. Bachman Magic [[VIDEOID:7a13c3e6479a283cf5238177fa8e93f3]] There is absolutely nothing to dislike as far as Bachman accessories are concerned including N and HO scale ez tracks and metal wheels plastic
Trucks to hold metal wheels etc. These are the essential components to design your own passenger cars and goods wagon. I have added a video of assembled HO scale tracks with Walters
Canadian Pacific locomotive, Original Bachman passenger cars and my own passenger car design.

rating 5.0. Son is happy My son was thrilled to get these for his birthday. They snap right in with his other pieces and now he can make different shaped tracks instead of just a circle. They work great and I thought the price was good too

rating 3.0. Some Tracks Defective I ordered 8 tracks (2 ea quantity 4 in each pack), of the eight 3 were defective. The ones that were not assembled with no issues and had no other issues. The problem with the three defective tracks was the gap when connected. The metal track bar was not long enough to eliminate gaps when connected. All of you enthusiasts will identify with my meaning. Fortunately I had enough working tracks to complete the track I was designing under my Christmas tree. The tracks are great which is the reason for the 3 star rating. The product works as designed if there are no defects. This is no blame to the vendor. The tracks arrived on time and the cost was competitive! I do recommend.

rating 3.0. COUNTRY LAYOUT, NOT CITY Very easy to assemble.  That being said,  I find that the design limits their use.  The built-in roadbed lifts the track.  I see BNSF tracks near my home like that outside of the city.  In the city they drop down for street crossings and running by the train station.  I found that the model train station and a freight transfer station that I have can't be placed close to this style of track and they sit too low to look correct.

rating 5.0. Nice Add on for a Bachmann Set for Around the Christmas Tree Going into the holidays I had an HO scale backman train with the regula r12 piece circle track. I wanted to extend the track just a little bit to fit comfortably around our 8 foot artifical tree.

These four additional track pieces did just the trick adding a few extra inches to each side.

The track is very easy to put together. Even my three-year-old was capable of assembling the track.

This track also works well on either carpet or hardwood floor. If you are using the old style of track without the plastic bed upgrading to EZ track will save you plenty of headaches.

input: rating 5.0. Black Widows I just received my Black Widow Scorpion pencils . I LOVE them ! They go on very smooth . I already loved the first Black Widows and now I am in love with these . I highly recommend these for anyone who wants a smooth pencil for coloring . It even seems they may have improved the quality if possible . I am very pleased and will order more in the future . I have other inexpensive and expensive pencils but these are my favorite !

rating 5.0. I really like them. They are smooth to use & shade ... I received mine today. I really like them. They are smooth to use & shade beautiful.

rating 5.0. Awesome pencils! I LOVE the Black Widow pencils so I was so excited to see the second edition Scorpion set launch. I immediately ordered them and have to say I am not dissapointed! The colors are vibrant and very smooth! I would definately recommend these pencils.

rating 4.0. LOVE LOVE LOVE I received my pencils today and love them!  I had the black widows but i own so many pencils, that i really didnt use them till today, and what a shame!  The colors are gorgeous and some colors i havent seen before, and they seem to blend and layer fairly well...the only problem i see while working with them is there are still not enough colors!!  Lol...PLEASE PLEASE come out with another 24 set soon!!  And truly, for those of us that like to do really detailed pictures, 48 just isn't enough, need at least a 72 set so i really hope another, at least, 24 set will be in the making soon...i would have given the pencils 5 stars if there were more colors....

rating 5.0. Love these! Love these new pencils! I received my Scorpions yesterday and I love, love, love them! The colors are beautiful, with such names as blood red, pig's ear and pastel purple. They come in a nice tin box another plus over the Black Widow Spider pencils. These will work nicely with them though, I can not wait to use them. Will post a finished picture.

rating 5.0. Love them!! I am so pleased with these pencils! They are awesome and i love the new colors. The scorpions & black widows have become one of my favorite sets and i have alot! I like having the names on the pencils too! One recommendation is to put the color names closer to the base of the pencils so the name will be visible on pencil for as long as possible.I hope you will come out with more colors!
rating 5.0. Five Stars I love love love these! Vibrant colors & so smooth & creamy!

rating 5.0. Great! I love these! The company listened to the customer feedback on the Black Widow pencils and put the name of the colors on the pencils this time. There is also a color chart on the inside cover of the tin. If you are a fan of the Black Widow pencils you need to purchase the new Scorpion pencils which are their newest colors. Just fantastic!

rating 4.0. GREAT PENCILS!! An awesome addition to any set, or perfect beginners set! Aside from the packing of my tin and how they arrived (pictures below, luckily no broken tips/pencils!) I LOVE these pencils! They lay and blend very nicely.....these pencils are great for amateurs & professionals alike!

rating 5.0. Awesome pencils! These are absolutely the ones to buy. Awesome pencils! They lay down the color absolutely beautifully. So very glad I ordered these pencils. They rank up with my Prisma's and Fiber Castells.

rating 5.0. Great supplementary set I say this is a great supplementary set because there is no black or white in this set and that is fine because it is the companion set to the Black Widow set. These pencils are smooth and vibrant,  the hold a good point and they doubt give off too much dust. In this set they changed the writing from red to white most likely to make it easier to read and they included pencil names...definite improvements. I love the colors because there are some shades in this set that I can't recall seeing any where else. I really like these pencils and hope they continue to expand the color selection.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Vibrant colors.. no damage.. fast delivery!

rating 4.0. Great if you do not need a fine point, colors are vivid and spread easily as core is so soft. Very difficult to do a fine line with as the leads are very soft and so obtaining a fine point is next to impossible. Even when just sharpened it doesn't stay fine very long as it is so soft. The colors are very vivid. The names of the different colors are clever, instead of calling it "moss green" they call it "grub green" for example.
I did order the set in a box instead of a tin container which have the same number of colored pencils. I did notice that the ones sold without the tin box do not have the color names on the side of the pencil. The ones in the box are hexagonal shaped instead of round.
I have used these to augment a larger group of multi colored pencils where I want a more vivid shade or where I am using a shading pencil as the color spreads very easily. For that purpose they would be a 5 star rating. I would not recommend these as the sole pencils used for coloring.

rating 5.0. FEEL THE DIFFERENCE!! I purchased the original Black Widow and this new SCORPION Edition set. Both sets have different colors, and together -- WOW!!!
These pencils are SO creamy and so rich, they are a delight to use. I use a many different high end brands of colored pencils from Inktense to Prismacolor -- Black Widow is now my "go to" color pencil. I constantly recommend them to members of our coloring group, people at work, even neighbors. (Need a set for people to try - so they can not only SEE the difference, but FEEL the difference)
I can't wait until they come out with even more beautiful colors.
Perfect product, perfect price. Outstanding company that pays attention to what we artists really want . Can't bet these pencils.
This is a spider I don't mind!!!!!

rating 5.0. Awesome Pencils!! I bought these at the same time as the original set and I  LOVE them! I like that this set has the colors labeled on the pencil, the original does not. I like the unusual color choices also, like the black-green. Please make a larger set!! They are smooth an lay down color easily with very little pressure. They work exactly as they were designed to!!! (if you don't know the genesis of these pencils, look them up - it's a cool story!)
rating 5.0. Lovely Vibrant Colors!! The Black Widow colored pencil set is everything is expected. The colors are lovely and vibrant.

rating 5.0. Black widow colors Great pencils for the price, color is a great blend and very smooth color. Like these very much

rating 5.0. Great pencils, great value. I love these pencils, as add-on to the original Black Widow.  They lay down pigment easily, and blend well, sharpen easily.  I have had only 1 out of both sets that broke; I just put it in a heating pad for about 20 minutes and it was fixed.  These pencils are very affordable and do a really nice job.  I like that this set has the name of the color (and a number) on the pencil.  I wish the original set had that.  And the tin case is great.
rating 5.0. Five Stars These are beyond belief. Soft cores last a while before you need to sharpen and have a rich color.
rating 4.0. Four Stars Very nice colored pencils to use on my various adult coloring books.
rating 5.0. Five Stars love these pencils, go on smooth, vibrant colors

rating 5.0. Terrific art colors. As with the Spider pencils, I enjoy working with these. They lay down color easily and layer well. I also like that they don't just roll off an slightly inclined surface.  These are well worth the money and would buy again if the need arises.  Also, the company they come from, is very customer oriented.  That is always a plus. I hope they'll put out more colors in the future. :)
rating 4.0. Good Black Widow Decent set of colored pencils for 20 bucks. Off the grid colors that compliment other brands that are higher priced. I am totally satisfied.
rating 5.0. Five Stars I love these amazing color nice to color with will order again
rating 5.0. Five Stars Great pencils for a great price!
rating 5.0. Five Stars I am in love with these pencils. The colors are very vivid.
rating 5.0. Nice addition to the black widow pencild Really great pencils, colors are vibrant and blend pretty good.
rating 5.0. Why? What a great set of pencils! Why did I put off buying the for so long???  😭

rating 4.0. Great pencils! Color names on this set! Wonderful pencils to color with. In this set the manufacture did us all a big favor and put the name of the color on the pencil. Thank you!!! Only reason I didn't give it a 5 star is a few of my pencil leads are off center making the sharpening off center as well. This is not the sellers fault. It was the fault of being made.

rating 4.0. I love the colors I have just received my color pencils.  I love the colors, however when I sharpen them, the point breaks in my electric sharpener. May be me or it might be just the one pencil. But it is a dream to color.
rating 5.0. Really like them so far These colors are so intense.  Really like them so far.  Fast shipping.  Good Price.  Will purchase them again when needed.
rating 5.0. Love! Beautiful colors and they are very smooth. I have both the black widow and the scorpion pack and I love them!

rating 3.0. Why do these smell? Pencils are nice, they blend well and the black looks great. Downside is some of the darker colours get lost in the black wood, so hard to see. The worst thing about these pencils is the smell!! WTF?? Opened them and the fumes punched me in the face.  I had to air them out to use them 😐
rating 4.0. Four Stars my wife loves these, she just wished there was greater variety of colors

rating 4.0. These aren't bad pencils. They have corrected problems on the scorpion ... These aren't bad pencils.  They have corrected problems on the scorpion  set, so the company listens to their customers.  There are colors in both sets that nobody else has.  They aren't as soft as prisma, but not as hard as polychromos.  Blending is ok. Can't order individual pencils as there is no open stock, explaining my 3 star rating for the Black Widow set.  I would give the Scorpion set a 4 star because they have names and numbers on them and a metal case, but still no open stock.
rating 4.0. So much sad. 4 stars because there’s no turquoise man!  There’s two blood reds. So much sad.
rating 5.0. Awesome set for the price Awesome set for the price ! Great saturation of color I wish they made more sets with more pinks and purples

rating 4.0. This is a review for both the Scorpion pencils and the Black Widow pencils since they are made by the same people I am going to start out with the good things about these pencils.
They have a brilliant color when you color a solid color with them. I really loved the color Blue Horizon. The greens between both sets of colors worked well together. They handle much better than say Crayola pencils or other budget pencils. The color of the pencils themselves and how they feel when you hold them are great.

Now for the negatives.
The colors didn't blend very well together for me. They created a lot of 'dust' when coloring. They broke far more than my Prismacolors ever have. In fact one of my pencils (SC82 Grass Green) broke in half the first time I took the pencil out of the case. Sharpening them was a pain, far more than sharpening my Prismacolors has ever been. I have numerous types of sharpeners and even my tried and true Derwent hand crank pencil sharpener ate these pencils up.

All in all despite the negatives, these are good pencils for the money. I wish there were far more colors and that I could purchase them open stock. Will I buy them again? I don't know, maybe. Are they good for people who can't quite commit the money to an artist quality pencil? Oh definitely! As I said they are much better than almost all the budget pencils I have come across. But again there are only 48 colors total between the two sets so that can be a deterrent.
rating 5.0. Five Stars wonderful pencils smooth and soft, they blend easy.
rating 5.0. Love Love! I use these more than my steadler or prismacolor... I have both sets. Vibrant Bold colors that pop and blend well! Bought another set for my niece who loves mine!!
rating 5.0. Five Stars the tips break easily and it takes a bit too much to sharpen.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Good set of pencils Nice and vivid colors. One pencil has a broken core.
other_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Disappointed Very disappointed in these German pens. Some of them arrived dry. Even the good ones ruin the paper - I tried regular coloring books and watercolor paper. Does not perform as youtube Stabilo 68 tutorials show.
rating 1.0. Do not recommend Cheap low quality pencils, very little pigments. Too many useless pale colors almost invisible on paper.
rating 4.0. Good pencils Good smooth inexpensive pencils. I subtracted one star because white pencil core was broken, and I use white a lot.
rating 1.0. Cheap pencils Low quality pencils, little pigment, poor metallic effect

rating 1.0. Dead on arrival I never expected this from such a reputable eraser. So disappointed! Inserted the batteries, pressed the button - nothing...The batteries were inserted correctly. Now what? Will they make me pay shipment to exchange or return it?

input:
rating 3.0. Very bright! I’m a little disappointed that the adhesive doesn’t seem to be holding the whole string of lights. I’ve used a couple pieces of double sided sticky tape to hold those weak spots, but even that isn’t holding.
I do love the brightness of them, though, and will continue to use them.

rating 2.0. Adhesiveness is ineffective The brightness is awesome.
However, it will NOT stay attached to the machine.

rating 1.0. Not worth it cleaned and prepped site prior to attaching LED strip. Despite that the strip would not remain in place. It became more of a problem and finally had to be removed. Besides it didn't produce enough light to make any kind of difference
Better answer was to use a strong light close to my machine.
rating 5.0. The light works well. I mounted this light to my Janome sewing machine.  I can now see my sewing much better.  The original light just wasn't strong enough for my aging eyes.
rating 1.0. Doesn't stick well It doesn't stick well at all. The part nearest the neck of the light won't stick to the machine and the light shines in my face, not on my fabric. I would not buy this.

rating 2.0. this light is the worst I have ever used The tape did not stay put and I had to keep putting it back in place.  I finally gave up and will not use it.  I do nor recommend this light to a serious sewer

rating 5.0. Let there be light! Totally changed my sewing game. I have plenty of light in my studio, but my eyes are failing me and get worse every year. Even with the lights on and daylight streaming through the window, this light has made threading the needle, seeing the seam allowance lines, and overall sewing soooo much easier. Totally worth it if you're on the fence!

rating 5.0. Bought it for my girlfriend My girlfriend, who does quilting, always complained about the scarce light coming from her sewing machine. Everything else is great in her baby lock, but not so much the lighting, so I gave it a shot and bought it. I thought, since is an inexpensive item, what do I have to lose? She is very happy with her new accessory. She cut it to the proper length (please as the little manual says, don't do it while powered) applied to the body of the sewing machine with the self-adhesive tabs provided in the kit and connected the power to the USB port coming with the sewing machine. A neat feature is that through the little "remote" (it's part of the power cord) she can change the intensity of lights, which can be even too bright. The power cord is pretty long and the kit comes with Velcro strips in order to stick it to the machine power cord. The kit comes with a wall USB adapter for those machines without an USB port; it's not my gf's case, but it is a nice touch to have it included. She can use it for charging her phone etc. She is extremely happy with this purchase, and if she's happy, I am happy :-) Also the price is absolutely reasonable for the quality of the product. Being LED lights I expect durability as well, though I cannot speak about that, yet. Kudos to Madam Sew!

rating 1.0. Doesn’t work consistently. Doesn’t work consistently. Will work for about 2 minutes then turn off.  When it works, its bright and would be great for me IF IT WORKED every time. Very disappointed.

rating 2.0. Cutting this per instructions ruined this light There are several things I wanted to like about this light. The cons very much outweigh the pros. The directions are simple enough to follow. The adhesion and brightness were great when I tried it out. Then I cut it per the instructions and it went downhill from there. It hasn’t worked at all since the first cut. Directions say cut only on the black line btwn the copper dots. After the cut, not touching a copper dot, it would no longer light. The other thing is the on/off switch. Not even a pic or mention on the instructions. Why are there arrows and why are they needed? Contacted madam sew for replacement and I got the equivalent of third degree. Where did you cut, what did you use to cut it, include pics and explanation of how you cut it. Really? Over a 2 ft piece of light. Although it was expensive, $35, doesn’t merit all I had to do because of a faulty light. Then they said send it back, I pay postage and their “team” will look it over and send me a new one. Well the shipping was $15! So for them to send me another light would cost me a total of $50. They do not reimburse for shipping. Went home, threw the light away, leaving this review and will never order from madam sew again. Ordered another light, from HengBo led sewing machine light for only $9.99. A much shorter light that fits perfectly on my machine and is dimmable and works beautifully.
“Sew”long madam sew: first time buyer was burnt out of $35. I guarantee they’ll never rip me off again. They’ve lost what could have been a life long customer btwn me and my 4 machines.

rating 4.0. Very bright! Light strip is very bright and directs the light right to where it's needed. It was a little tricky getting the strip installed due to the orientation of the cord and control switch. Different machine configurations may be easier or harder based on individual circumstances. Adhesion was good, except for the part closest to the power cord. A little more distance between the last light and the beginning of the power cord would have helped.  Overall, it is very effective a definite improvement over the original light on the machine. Pictures attached are with the light strip alone and no other lighting source.

rating 3.0. Bright light but didn't stick well. Adhesive didn't stick well to my machine. May need to use super glue for permenant staying in place. I'll probably try another product. The extra light is great though.

rating 4.0. I like it but… I find the bright light helpful and would recommend it for that reason. Installing it was challenging and awkward; I needed help. Even though I carefully cleaned the surface and let dry overnight, the strip doesn’t adhere near the base.

rating 5.0. Good light Nice and bright. Very easy to trim and apply. Sticks on great.

I would recommend.
rating 1.0. Light-sewing Could could not get the light to stay on the underside of the sewing machine with my material when I put it through the sewing machine.
rating 2.0. Doesn’t stick It won’t stay. Adhesion is very very poor
rating 1.0. Soooo frustrating I ended up throwing this out. It wouldn’t stick to my machine. I cleaned the machine like it said in the directions but no matter what I did, it wouldn’t stay put.

rating 1.0. Didn't work When I got the light strip, I plugged it in to make sure it worked.  Then I installed it and then it never worked.  I tried different receptacles but no luck.  I had to return it.

rating 1.0. Poor Performer Bought two of these for my sewing machines.  After the first arrived and I installed it on my sewing machine I was  thrilled with the brightness.  Immediately bought a second for my other sewing machine.  Only had them for a very short time when one string started to turn itself off.  Have been unable to sit down and sew for an hour without the off on business.  Have used the second machine very little but do not have hope that it will be much better.  Skip this one and look for a better product.

rating 4.0. It’s good I’m a very part time sewer (mostly hems). But I always added extra light around my machine.  When looking to replace light bulb with a bright LED bulb I came across this. First I want to say the quality of the packaging is awesome. The inside item were packaged just as well.  Clear instructions!! They really care about their product.  I thought $20 was a lil to much for what I needed, but I needed this.  I guess I paid for that fancy/quality packaging.  Bottom line is it lit up my work area of the machine very well.
rating 1.0. Don't buy this The wires broke while I was mounting it to my sewing machine!! I would not buy again!!

rating 5.0. I Can See!  I Can See! Was skeptical that this product would make a huge difference to the lighting on my sewing machine, but since the price was reasonable I decided to go for it. When it arrived I put off installing it until the next day, expecting complicated installation instructions. Turns out, the instructions were clear and concise, installation easy and painless. And the lighting!!! It lit up my whole work area and even after working for several hours, there was no eye strain. I LOVE this product and would highly recommend it. Wish I had found it a long time ago!

rating 2.0. Worked just a few minutes I ordered two light strips. One for a gift and one for me. Due to the holidays, I just installed my light strip this P.M.. I loved it....BUT, It did not work long. After I had sewn a few seams I noticed the strip had not adhered well in a spot and I pressed it in and then ran my had along the entire strip to make sure it was all secure. When I reached the end of the strip where it attached to the  cable, the light went out. I wiggled the cable, turned it on and off several times, unplugged it and plugged it back in and did everything I knew to do. Still no light. I am very disappointed and was so excited to have this item.  I don't want a refund, but a light that works. I contacted Madam Sew and they told me to contact you.

rating 4.0. It is bright and lights up the area I attached the light to my sewing machine it lighted the area up nicely. The tape didnt hold well when I was quilting.it came off when I pushed the rolled part through and it bumped it. It worked great for all the other projects i did. The tail of the light is a little thick it needs a clip or tape to hold it more secure.

rating 5.0. Madame Sew Bright Lighting is Sew Cool! I have always wanted better lighting for sewing and now I have it. This is especially important now that I have to wear reading glasses. Madame Sew Bright light strip is so easy to set up and it really makes sewing more enjoyable. I highly recommend this product.

rating 5.0. Fantastic Sewing Lighting Strip I bought this lighting strip for my wife to use.  She sews for a living and does a lot of intricate work which requires good lighting.  She felt that there were always some sort of shadows no matter what kind of lights that she tried.  Once she got this, she was amazed at how well it light up the entire sewing area.  This item comes in nice packaging with easy to read and understand instructions.  The lighting strip can be trimmed to your required length.  Easy to use and it works fantastic!!  She loves it.  It makes her work a lot easier.  I would highly recommend this product to anyone who uses a sewing machine.
rating 2.0. Non stick stripe Lights up well, however, the adhesive doesn't hold. Stripe falls even while I'm working at machine.

rating 1.0. Doesn’t fit my machine My machine is all curves and the strip was hard to position so it would look straight and stick in place. I had to reposition it several times and it never really laid flat against the surface. Now it comes off repeatedly while I’m sewing. I will have to buy something else. It’s very bright and helpful if it would fit my machine. Wish the off switch really turn the power off; it always has a light glowing as long as it is plugged in.

rating 5.0. Perfect lights for any sewing machine! I bought a sewing machine around this time last year and it took me way too long to find this product. I’ve recently upgraded my sewing machine and the first thing I did was reorders these lights because they are that perfect!! The ability to adjust the brightness is incredibly helpful, but these lights overall are easily one of my favorite purchases! If I ever get another sewing machine, lol, I will most definitely be ordering these again!

rating 1.0. Broke before I sewed a stitch Super bright light but unfortunately it broke during installation and I cannot repair it. Was pressing the LED portion onto my machine and the solder (?) that connects the LED strip to the positive / negative snapped at the black wire I have no tools to fix this. Maybe some can, but I think such a week connection should have had more protection that heat shrink tape. This was kind of spendy for what it is and I would not recommend you buy it because of this design flaw.
rating 2.0. Did not stick After cleaning with alcohol The light is slowly coming off.

rating 5.0. A great light for my task work. The light is so good!  I have no shadows, it's bright and wonderful, easy to maneuver, and I like it so much I may be purchasing a second one for a seamstress friend!

rating 5.0. Easy to install and bright! This was super easy to install. The kit comes with very detailed instructions and anchors to secure the cord to the machine and Velcro to hold together will the machine cord. The power cord is long and reached the outlet with ease. The light is very bright and I love it! Couldn’t be happier with this sewing machine light! I would purchase this again

rating 5.0. Provides extra lighting when I need it. It is easy to attach to the sewing machine , it’s very bright but not so much that it is uncomfortable to your eyes. I also like that there is a turn off switch for when I don’t need extra lighting. The price is very reasonable.
rating 4.0. Lights up my work space Excellent light under my sewing machine. My only complaint is that the lights near the cord shine right in my eyes, but I can get used to it.

rating 3.0. It doesn’t stick very well. It doesn’t stick very well even after cleaning surface with alcohol.

rating 1.0. The Adhesion didn’t work for me. Should have returned. The adhesion is Horrible Horrible. Followed instructions applied to my sewing machine and stripes fell off. Haven’t been able to use. The
rating 2.0. staying in place I used the strip for my sewing machine, however it does not stay in place I cleaned the service as directed  but to no avail.
rating 5.0. Madame Sew Light Strip Loved it!!!!!!! G8 brightness Easy to install Just Wonderful.  Always wanted this I highly recommend it I’m a real person Real Home Sewer
rating 1.0. Light doesn’t work properly.. The light is hard to turn on I have to keep clicking the on button for a while before it turns on..

rating 5.0. Great Light Overall - Love it This light is just great! I bought it for my wife sewing machine but I liked it so much that I just other a second one for my working space. This is really a multi-use light...
Looks well-built, everything looks high quality... The light itself is pretty bright, and have a dimmer which I think was an excellent idea cause the light is really bright and in some cases, you would like less intense light. Great buy overall.

rating 1.0. Only worked twice. Stopped working after 2 uses. Entire strip wasn’t sticky. Prompt refund, though. Thanks.

rating 1.0. Doesn't stick Love the concept of this item only it doesn't stick to my sewing machine at all.  I even used a hot glue gun to try to secure it  and even that failed.  I have it now tied to my machine with cable ties.  Looks like crap but at least it works.

rating 5.0. I love this light I’m getting older and my Janome 4120 just doesn’t have enough light for these old eyes when I’m sewing. This was very easy to install and great when I sew or quilt. My neighbor wants one for Christmas and a coworker wants one for her new sewing machine.
Great price to pay to shine a little light in the subject.

rating 5.0. Love this little light I taped it to my machine so I could fit it the best and mark where I needed to cut the light. Once I had it set where I wanted it I began to peel and stick it starting at my cut end. Slowly peeled the paper back to my first piece of tape then folded back the tape so I could continue to peel. Pressed the tape back as I passed it so I didn't pull the light strip off.
Then I figured out where I wanted the wire and switch. Took a little time but I love the extra light.
The picture is without a flash so everybody can see how lit up my machine is. Second picture is without my machine light on. It's much better.

rating 3.0. The adhesive I like the light and it helps me see my project very well. But the
Adhesive didn’t hold very well. I found some double side adhesive at Walmart and it works very well now

rating 1.0. Works very well...doesn't stick though. UPDATE---- The adhesive is awful and loses its stick quickly. Which really sucks because the light it gives is fantastic.

REALLY like these lights. I saw a similar product at the Sewing and Stitchery Expo in WA but they were asking for $70. I thought I'd give these a try and it's great. I highly recommend for anybody who sews and/or uses an embroidery machine.

current_product_reviews: rating 2.0. This light is not durable I loved this while it worked but today it developed a short so it goes on and off (mostly off) until you manipulate the wires. I cannot figure out how to tape it in the functional position. I will order another, cheaper strip to replace it as I didn’t realize how short lived it could be (purchased July of 21; needs replacing just 6 months later.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Best paper for quilter’s paper piecing This paper works better than expensive brands of specialty paper piecing paper. I like to make my HSTs using triangulation software (you can also sew almost any size of flying geese from this software. Load your inkjet printer with this paper. Print your foundations and follow directions on software. This tears very very easily. So easy to remove when you are ready. Highly recommend!
rating 5.0. Good quality for fair price. Nice variety of sizes; all smooth, sharp, well made. Delivery was prompt.

rating 5.0. Don’t be without one of these. This product was bought to replace an old one. These are invaluable for giving necessary perspective in viewing your quilt as you are making composition decisions. I’m very happy.
rating 5.0. Nice bobbin organizer. These were shipped to me quickly and provide a great method of organizing my bobbins. If they hold up well, they will be perfect. Time will tell.

rating 1.0. Worthless Purchased this for bumper stickers and the magnet is too weak to hold anything onto a metal surface. Completely useless. My husband threw it away.  Why do they even manufacture something like this?  If it won’t cling to a metal surface, then it’s hardly what I’d call magnetic!!!

rating 3.0. Fragile. I was disappointed to learn these are very delicate. I accidentally dropped mine before I had even had a chance to use them and was shocked to discover the tip badly bent from falling just a couple of feet and hitting my floor point end first. I don’t think that would have ruined any other scissors I’ve owned.

rating 3.0. Average quality. These aren’t exactly what I would expect from a German manufactured product, but I guess they weren’t terribly expensive either. Just beware, you will not really be getting any bargain. They are so-so quality for so-so price. That’s my take on it anyway. I bought them to give as a gift so I’m not sure if they will last or not.

rating 5.0. Satisfied with product and delivery This arrived in a timely manner, but I have to admit, I felt foolish paying this much for something so simple that I could have manufactured on my own out of card stock... However, I find this so handy, keep it in a little box of tools that I use most often and when working on multiple sewing machines, it is handy to go from one to another and insure that you create the same seam no matter what machine or foot you are using. It does make adjusting seam allowances a breeze so I do recommend this if consistency is what you're after. Just one more tool in the box to try to create an accurate 1/4" seam.

rating 2.0. Not useful for punching soft materials like wool felt I tried to figure out whether or not this would work to punch out circles of felt, and finally had to just try it out for myself. It doesn't work at all for this purpose, so I now have no practical use for it. If you are a person who does felted wool applique, skip this one; it probably works better for the intended purpose, punching leather, but I can't speak to that.

rating 4.0. A basic Cutting Mat Nothing much to say on this one. This was exactly what I expected, since I've already had these, and they don't vary from one to the next. Shipping was timely. Thank you.
rating 5.0. Lois Ericson is a talented designer. Love this pattern. Beautiful robe pattern with many ideas to customize to your liking. Many options rolled up into one pattern purchase.
rating 5.0. Wonderful thimble. This is a replacement for one I lost. I love these thimbles. So comfortable, and the magnet is very handy!

input:
rating 2.0. Great idea, poor quality I wish I could write a positive review, especially after finding a lovely message from the small business owner in the box. Alas.

Most pens in the set were fine, but In a couple the pain was very thin (or the cartridge inside broken) and they were leaking when my son was drawing. The caps are way too tight for pens, so a 7 y.o. couldn’t take them off. We kept playing the “Mom, please take the cap off” game for three days in the row. And then the disaster happened. I was trying to take off a cap from a while pen and the cartridge inside flew off, splashing everything around us with bleachy white paint. Three of my garments are ruined, and so are a couple of chairs and the carpet. Soap won’t take it off, acetone won’t take it off either :(

It’s a great idea of mess-free painting, but it ended in a total mess for us.
rating 5.0. Very happy Great! Fast delivery and work exactly as described! Very happy!

rating 2.0. Painful ridged caps. Cool colors though. The quality of the actual paint seems ok but the caps have ridges that make it extremely painful and hard to remove. It limits my drawing time bc it roughs up my fingers and palms. One of the pens had a hard dry tip for my second use even though paint pours out when I press the tip. Overall, it’s the painful caps that will keep me from purchasing these again.

rating 5.0. Great value! Very happy with the quality for the price.  These are the 3rd brand of paint pens I’ve attempted to use to draw designs on my husband’s skateboard.  Grip tape is like sandpaper- very destructive to pens in general, but these held up really nice!!  Also very blendable and good range of color.  This was my first day using, so not sure about longevity but I am pleased!

rating 1.0. Do not recommend for rock painting These paint markers bleed into each other. Doesn't matter which color. I've let them dry for a day, put in the oven, etc.. they always bleed. And then the other color gets absorbed into the next colors tip. I was very unimpressed.

rating 2.0. Just ok sadly I purchased these for a Mother’s Day project. When I opened them one of the markers tops was cracked and in turn it had dried out and never worked. At first these seemed great. My dark green pen the started to leak everywhere. The tip got pressed in and didn’t come back out properly so I had to throw that one out. After using them all week to complete my project two or three other pens started to dry out even when shaken up. The paint started to come out watery and one of the yellows had to be thrown out too because the tip got stuck just like my dark green. Thankfully it got me through my project but I don’t recommend purchasing
rating 1.0. Don’t waste your money. Product did not flow smoothly. Leaked and globbed  all over my project causing me to have to redo it.  Not worth the money!

rating 3.0. Fairly good and lots of applicationa They work great, vibrant colors
Unfortunately we had the yellow somehow opened and leaked. Seems to be a problem even with another brand we tried.

rating 5.0. Good quality and value My son really likes this set. The pens work well and the colors look great. He did say the gold is more of a dark yellow but chalk that up to personal preference.  Overall a great purchase.

rating 5.0. Water based ink? So when I first opened this product. On big bold letters on the front of the package it says “water based ink” so I contacted the seller to get a replacement thinking I got the wrong item. I get the same thing a few days later, so I decided to open the package up and the pens say oil based ink and I work as oil based pens. I am new to the art world but I’m not sure they can be both oil based and water based at the same time? So I assume it is just a miss print of sorts. But to anyone else with a similar issue, they are in fact oil based pens. They work amazing and there’s a variety of colors. Some of the lighter colors are sheer but I believe that is common with any lighter color paint pen.

rating 5.0. Great Paint Markers This is my first time using paint markers. They are so easy to use and offer great coverage. The range of colors make this non artistic crafter’s ( me) art work have so much depth.
These are medium point. Getting ready to order the thin point.
Dries quickly so I’ve finished so many projects!
rating 5.0. Great markers! Worked awesome on rocks for the kids. No runs. They don't stink. Great markers!
rating 5.0. Great flow These painting markers are better than most I have used. They cover very well.

rating 3.0. Too much mess The caps were very difficult to remove which often resulted in paint leaking everywhere. To avoid this I had to start using my teeth to take off the caps while twisting righty-tighty to prevent a disastrous mess.
The paint coverage was nice, but I couldn’t add layers until the bottom layer was completely cured or the top layer would cut and scratch into the one below. This slowed down my painting process.
Also, as with most paint pens, you had to pump to pens to refresh the tips, but often if you pumped one too may times, it gushed out.
These pens were helpful in certain applications, but the strain on my hands and the inevitable mess made them less-than-ideal for regular usage.

rating 4.0. Fun machine. This thing works great if you use compatible yarns. It might take a little experimenting to find yarns that work well with it.
I found out the hard way that it doesn't like cotton yarn (too much friction), but it works wonderful with smooth, worsted weight wool. It works best at knitting tubes. It can make flat panels, but knitting them is a bit more complicated. It knits in minutes what normally takes hours. I am very pleased with my purchase.

rating 4.0. You gotta have patience I wish I could love it, I only just like it. I have my own headwear company and I make all of my custom beanies on this. It can be wonderful at times, and absolutely suck at other times. The main problem is dropping stitches, to which I have figured out its the softness of the yarn. Worsted weight (4) is the number to use, but you have to be careful and find soft yarns. Anything relatively rough will leave you wanted to throw the machine across the room. I've made probably about 300 beanies on it in the last 6 months, to which about 50 of them I had to restart or just completely walk away from because of dropped stitches. The other problem I have with it is that larger noggins are a tight fit for the beanies this thing produces. I wish it had just a few more needles in it so I could have a larger customer base. When you get the right yarn though, you can crank out a double sided beanie in a matter of 10 minutes. It's a real love hate.

rating 5.0. Knitting Machine I have broken some of the knitting needles in my machine but my husband has been able to replace them when needed.  The needles are kind of expensive.  I have knitted a large number of hats on this machine and found that a medium thread works best.  You do need to keep an eye on it so you don't drop a stitch.  Over all I love it because it saves a lot of time.  I can made a hat in about 15 mins.  I also have the baby Express and love it for making baby hats and scarves.

rating 5.0. Great Value & Great Results! This is my second addi - purchased the large machine this time. They work very well for me - either with two strands of fingering or one worsted. I haven't tried anything heavier than aran yet.

If you really want to "crank out" some knitting, this is the way to go. Watch videos of comparisons to other lower priced machines, and you'll see the difference! Although these are made from plastic, they still are precision machines.

But, it's important to remember that these are machines that can only do a set number of stitches. They are not like $1,000 sock machines or heavy duty flat bed machines. I think that if you are realistic in your expectations, you'll be very pleased. The prices on Amazon are far below retail - great value!

rating 4.0. One Size Does Not Fit All! While I have not purchased this unit, I have used one to make about two dozen hats. after about 5 hats, the "lightbulb" came on and now I can cast on and cast off with ease. I did watch UTube instructional videos which were helpful.  Then turning the crank to continue to knit is a breeze.  I've used worsted weight and sport weight yarn.  The easiest was Caron's Simply soft. No dropped stitches, no splits in the yarn from the needles.  It appears to be a well made machine the quality you expect from Addi! It is a heavy plastic material.  My only negative and that is why the 3 rating instead of a 5, is that the only way to control the size of the finished product...is through the thickness of the yarn.  Want to make a baby hat, a toddler hat, a young child hat, a very large hat.....then this is not the machine to buy.  This is a lot of money to pay for a one size only. To be honest, I didn't try to knit a flat panel.  If what you want to mass produce knit hats, this machine may work for you.  A hat can be completed in under an hour.  I wish Addi could make this machine somehow to choose different widths of the knit tubes!

rating 5.0. This knits worsted yarn best, though you can use double strands of a ... This thing is SOLID. A bit on the pricey side but most things Addi makes, you are getting a quality tool.

This knits worsted yarn best, though you can use double strands of a lighter weight yarn. It is a tight and even gauge. I did have issues with some dropped stitches when I was trying the flat panel option, but I will chock this up to newby errors and not paying attention to the yarn. One word of warning on knitting flat panels is that you will need to make the yarn fairly taut when knitting flat panels so that the hook catches the first stitch. I ended up knitting long tubes and steeking the tube into a flat panel. There are a handful of tutorials on youtube on how to do ribbing and decreases, so you can make more tailored clothing (it's probably more complicated with this than with normal knitting).

I plan on using this to make sweaters quickly and after 30-45 minutes of cranking, you can make a panel fairly quickly. My chest measurement is 41'' across and knitting 2 panels from the front side is a little too wide. After 3 weekends, I ended up making my first sweater on this machine. There are some finishing touches I need to make and probably more steeking to taper the sleeves but it's a start. Knitting in the round, I had no dropped stitches. I would not suggest knitting flat if you also want to watch Netflix (learn from my misadventures!).

A small gripe is the handle you use to crank. It's really uncomfortable and not very ergonomic. And when you're cranking your right arm gets a good work out.

In conclusion, I would strongly suggest this IF you are planning on making a ton of simple stuff VERY fast. It will pay for itself.

rating 4.0. ... for a couple of months now and it is great! It is made of good quality materials and ... I have had the Addi Express King Size Knitting Machine for a couple of months now and it is great! It is made of good quality materials and with proper use and care it should last me a good while. As with any of these machines, you have to learn its quirks and learn how to use it properly. I have both the small and the large Addi. I have tried several different types of yarns with it and so far my favorite 4-ply yarn to use with it is I Love This Yarn. Red Heart 4-ply acrylic yarn works quite well also. It handles 3-ply yarns very well too. I haven't tried bulky weight yarn with it. I have made some very nice items on the Addi, including hats, scarves, lap blankets, ponchos, socks, and shawls.
PROS - Fast, even knitting, allows you to complete items quickly.
Easy to use, even for beginners
Portable, lightweight. I can move to where I need to in my house.
I like the fact that it doesn't need batteries or electricity to operate it
CONS - It does drop stitches from time to time, but if you watch your your tension it's not such a problem. It also tends to drop stitches on the edges when you're making a flat panel but this can be avoided also if you make sure those end stitches aren't loose. It takes practice. Over time I have dropped fewer and fewer stitches. I know a lot of folks complain about the dropped stitches, but I don't see it as a deal breaker.
The row counter has its moods. Sometimes it jumps numbers, and sometimes it just decides to go back to zero for no apparent reason. I usually just keep track of my counting in my head and I don't depend solely on its accuracy.
The instructions that came with the machine aren't that impressionable. You can learn much more by watching YouTube videos or just by practicing on your own.

Overall, . I am very pleased with my Addi Knitting Machines. I tend to use the large Addi much more than the smaller Addi. Even though you can't make a variety of fancy patterns on it, you can knit wonderful items for yourself, your family and friends, and even for charity. I think it was well worth the money I spent for it. I'm giving it 4 stars instead of 5 because of the inaccuate row counter and because the instructions that come with it aren't very helpful. Trial and error is the best way to learn to use this machine.

rating 5.0. I love my new addi Express King Size Knitting Machine! I just received my new addi Express King Sized knitting machine and I am so happy!
First of all, I did not receive this product or any other compensation for this review. Second, I am a professional Knitter, Crocheter, Designer and just starting looming.

I was initially frustrated as the machine seemed to drop stitches on needles 23 & 25 consistently. After starting and redoing my scrap yarn several times, I found I could control this with tension by holding the yarn in my left hand. Once I figured out this, and the consistent speed (not too fast), I was able to make a reversible hat in about 30 minutes! I am now experimenting with other pattern possibilities and yarns.

This machine is well made, the screws are tight and the stitches are consistent. You have a choice of clamping to the table or bench, or holding in your lap. The loom will also sit flat on a surface without clamping. The counter seems to work correctly, provided that the yarn you are using does not cause too much static electricity. I am going to be saving for the addi Express Professional (smaller sized) soon.
I highly recommend this machine.

rating 2.0. Wait until they fix the counter issues... I have a problem with this item. The counter doesn't work. I sent the following e-mail: "The counter doesn't work correctly. It will frequently add 2 when counting rows. This has messed up several items I have made. I have several hats that are all different sizes. Is there a fix for this? It's ruining this loom for me."
Their reply was this: "Hello Matthew, First reset the counter. The reset button is on the bottom of the counter.
The reason why the counter does not work correct is the electrostatic charging caused by the friction of the wool thread on the yarn gate. This can be fixed by using antistatic spray.
If you have any questions please do not hesitate to contact us. Your satisfaction is very important to us: if you are in any way not entirely satisfied, it is essential that you contact us so that we can sort out any issues.
Have a nice day ahead!
Best regards,
Julia Schmitt
SEW-Happy Shop"

Well, Pressing the recessed reset switch wasn't effective, if I do decide to spray it, WHERE do I spray it? What kind of spray? I can't use the block feature if I always have an odd number of rows. Most complaints I have seen are about the defective counter. I tried to find out what to do, and this was the resolution.

It's okay to make knit caps, but that's certainly not all I wanted.

It often drops stitches, but if you know how to knit you can fix then without losing all your work.

A big part of the draw for me to buy this was the counter.

rating 5.0. The Addi is super easy to use and it comes with a video ... I am new to looming. I ordered the Addi Express King and addi Express Kingsize with 46 needles Pattern Book 892 pattern book. I was a little leery about ordering it not only because of the price but I didn't want to end up with another loom that I can only make scarfs and hats on! The Addi is super easy to use and it comes with a video that shows how to cast on and off and how to operate your Addi. It is very easy to learn how to use and I did "knit a 2 color" hat in less than 1/2. It comes fully assembled with the exception of the legs that you simply screw in by hand like wise with the claps that attach the Addi Express Kingsize to your working surface.

The pattern book is much simpler to read than most of the pattern books I ordered (the books I have ordered with my regular looms seem to be written in some code I can't decipher! ) I get really frustrated because I do not under stand regular pattern book and all of it's abbreviations.

This pattern book is not our typical, scarf and hat book, it goes step by step (You can actually understand what they want you do do) into how to make baby onesies, blankets, shawls, ponchos, sweater for both adult male and female and children scarfs, blankets, wrap around jackets. The book comes with both written instructions in English, beautiful clear color photographs of what your project is suppose to look like, plus a complete pattern illustration where they break spell it out how long each piece is suppose to be and how to put it together. As a beginner this is vital. You can really make professional looking pieces in a short while.

Excellent customer Service. The customer support is phenomenal, friendly and extremely helpful. I contacted them with questions and they were extremely professional and super quick to respond. They even sent out an email letting me know where my order was every step of the way. That made me feel a lot better because it was coming Germany and I had no idea how all of this would of turned out. It turned out wonderful and I am very happy and strongly recommend this item, the book and the seller. This would make a very unique and thoughtful gift for anyone wanting to learn how to loom or has health issues that limit using there hands.

rating 5.0. Fantastic Adhesive I originally purchased this adhesive to fix [[ASIN:B002ER8HZC a sketchbook]]--the cover had completely fallen away from the signatures. Not only did this fit the bill perfectly, I quickly found other uses for it as well. I've since used it on several old books and in some paper crafts.

My sketchbook was repaired a couple months ago and I've put it through quite a bit of abuse since (bending the flaps backward, creasing the spine, &c.) and the adhesive hasn't budged an inch. I'll update this review if anything changes, but I'm definitely impressed so far.

It goes down smoothly, but unless you use a pin or needle to punch a hole in the applicator, you will get a huge line. Basically, the tip isn't very fine at all. It dries clear and strong, yet flexible. And a little goes a *long* way, so I'm certain this 4oz. bottle will last a while (unless you're doing massive scale repairs or paper craft projects).

rating 5.0. Pours well, dries evenly - great for nail art I bought this PVA adhesive after watching a nail tutorial on glitter nails. For years, giltter has been the bane of my existance. So shiny and beautiful, yet truly a pain in the neck to remove. With this new popular method, one simply paints on a layer of PVA, lets it dry, then does a base coat, glitter polish, and topcoat. The PVA is easily removable when soaked in water, and there's no damage to the nail nor irritation of fussing with glitter removal.

This PVA spreads as well as any nail polish I've used in the past. It goes on thin, does not clump, and dries clear and smooth. It took about 10 minutes to fully let it dry, which wasn't an issue... usually when I'm doing my nails I'm watching TV or chatting with a friend. I didn't use anything to aid the dry time. After it was dry, I put on a a China Glaze glitter, and then topped it off with Sally Hansen. My manicure lasted about a week - standard time for me, since I type so much at work. When it was time to remove, I just soaked my hands in warm water for about 30 seconds, and then gently scraped it off with an orangewood stick.

It's seriously a great idea for those who love to do nails. I also craft, so having the bottle around may serve a purpouse there as well. I have no doubts of it's quality, and I love that it dries so clear!

rating 4.0. It smoothly and securely repaired a paperback copy of "Voyager" by Diana Gabaldon (A Big Enormous book) The book had separated from the cover along the entire spine and the pages completely separated into 2 sections.  I had tried other methods and glues to no avail.  How I did it:  (1) I used sandpaper to roughen the existing coat of glue along the spine of the the pages and the inside of the cover spine, (2) stacked the 2 sections of pages together on the cover with edges aligned, (3) spread some glue along the spine of both sections of pages, (4) stuck a strip of cotton cloth in the wet glue to cover the entire spine section of pages, (5) spread some glue on the inside of the cover spine, (6) pressed it all together and held it for 5-10 minutes.  The book went together perfectly and the glue was not messy.  I let it dry for 48 hours before opening the book.  Anyone who reads Diana's books can appreciate how many times "Voyager" has been read and will continue to be re-read!

rating 5.0. works great My daughter's favorite board book had split pages and a torn binding (my son had been experimenting with the tensile strength of binding by making the book do 'the wave' repeatedly, even though that's what tore it LAST time...) This glue fixed it. Great stuff. Easy to use. Being able to repair loose pages is also GREAT. Be a good baby shower gift (along with the Transparent Mending Tissue) for parents.

rating 5.0. Does the job well I've tried PVA, e-poxy and hobby glue - these works. However, PVA is a little weak (requires multiple coating) but dries transparent. E-poxy dries fast but can get quite messy if not careful. It does have a certain strong smell. Hobby glue (almost like PVA) is ok but can be a little costly.

This glue is also PVA based. It dries yellow (as like in many commerical made books) into soft gel and seriously very adhesive. It's even difficult to cut with a blade (in one of my project, I accidentally glued the book press too and had to cut it with a knife).

Highly recommended (actually, I'm making my second purchase).

rating 4.0. Great product, but could use a cap! The glue sets well and binds strong. Used it to repair two books so far with good results. One is a 60-year-old, leather bound, small Bible. The text block had separated from the cover and one signature detached from the rest of the text. The glue set overnight beneath the weight of a few books and now you can't even tell it had ever detached.

Only improvement would be to include a cap for the bottle. Once you poke a hole in the nozzle to dispense the glue, there's nothing included to keep the glue from drying out (or spilling out if it gets squeezed or upended!). I initially left the paper liner in the bottle to serve this purpose, but am now just leaving a pin in the hole in the nozzle.
rating 5.0. Good stuff It is a great glue.  I repaired an old Bible. The pages are very delicate and the glue did the trick.  I like that I can clean the excess glue with ease.  Great product.

rating 5.0. Excellent adhesive PH Neutral -use to repair your books. I was a Children's Librarian in a public library for 23 years and repaired many books.

rating 5.0. I'll be sticking with it Far better than any other glue I’ve used. I had to use this for a semester to do paper projects including cardstock and foam board. For me the biggest problem with glues that are water soluable are they are weak and take a too long to dry. This product eliminates both those issues.

The projects I did I was able to finish in half the time with the use of this and I am happy to know they will last forever with this powerful adhesive. The downside to every water soluable adhesive is that thinner pieces it will warp. I tried using it for gluing construction paper and magazines, they warped as expected. For projects like those, rubber cement will always be the best option.

The product holds up strong, dries quickly as well as drying quite clear as advertised; helpful being I used every colour paper other than white. The only thing I don’t like is the price, only 4 ounces for 9 bucks. I’ll get the bigger bottle next time; but I guess you get what you pay for and in this case, high quality glue.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Product worked very well I used this to repair the spin/binding of a hardcover Barbie book that my wife has. The glue is in a nice squeezable bottle with spout and cap as shown on the Amazon ad. It flows with the consistence of an Elmers glue or wood glue so be careful when applying. After applying I also used the Lineco gummed book repair tape that I ordered through Amazon to repair the inside of the cover along the spine. Once this was complete I put two nylon straps with plastic buckles around the book. Just don't make the straps to tight or they may leave groves or pucker the outside of the binding, since the glue will probably start to seep into and soften the binding. Finally I stood the book straight up on the binding, and supported it on both sides, and left it for 24 hours. Everything worked fine. The binding and cover are fixed and all pages easily open. We're very happy with the results.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Solid Snap Fastener Perfect for Leather I used these snaps on 3mm thick leather strips and was very happy with the results. They hold firm through both sides of the leather strip, and securely snap together. They unsnap with just the right amount of pressure, and look good also. Since they are made of plated steel they do require a firm crimp. I recommend using a heavy duty Snap fastener pair of pliers. I got a pair through Amazon by Dritz. These worked well and held up to the pressure of a firm squeeze, without any bending of the handle. I don't know how well these snaps would work on thinner material. I just know they work well with leather. Hope this helped.

rating 5.0. Good Fastener easy to use I needed a fastener to put in some steel snaps through leather straps about 3mm thick. I read all the reviews and searched through the fasteners listed on Amazon. I only need this for occasional use and didn't want to spend a lot. So, I decided on this one . I must say it worked very well the first time putting in the plated steel snaps through leather. These snaps require a firm squeeze to properly crimp the post. This fasteners held up without any bending in the handles. Pretty good for a first use. Comes with instructions on the back of the card, that may be a little hard to follow unless you've done this before. I just had to make sure which half of the snap goes into the plastic platform, and when to use the red adapter. Like I said I'm a first time user, and I was able to figure it out. Product works, my snaps are all holding, and I'm happy with this fastener.

rating 4.0. Very Good leather hole punch If I could have rated this 4.5 stars I would have. The biggest flaw, and I'm being picky, is that there is slight movement in the rotating punch head. This in my opinion, is do to the punch lock not being strong enough. It allows you to rotate the wheel and select the punch size, but realy doesn't secure and center it over the anvil without movement. This may cause the punch to drift as your going through 3-4mm thick material like leather, which will result in a slightly off center hole and snap. This happened to me, but still it was close enough to center to look pretty good. So, if you are just making holes in leather, and or putting snap fasteners through the holes , then I suggest holding the punch head with one hand when squeezing the handle. The punch heads cut very nice with only moderate squeezing of the large easy grip handles. This is a heavy well made all metal punch that seems to be holding up very well. I bought it for occasional use and didn't want to spend a lot on it. This works well and I'm happy with it.

rating 4.0. well worth the money This was a great value. The cup turner works great and was very easy to assemble. It doesn't have a speed setting which would have been nice but it didn't advertise that it did so no harm there. You get a lot of items in this bundle which are great. I'm especially fond of the stands that come with it, however, there are only two "sticks". One you will need for the turner and the other you can use with the tripod stand but there are actually 3 stands that come with this. I had to go buy some dowels to utilize the other stands that came in this bundle. Also, the epoxy that comes in this kit is not great. I used it on two different cups and got terrible "fish eyes" each time. I did the same method on the same cups with a different brand of epoxy and they came out perfect.

input:
rating 1.0. Not worth the money It's $100 worth of nothing special. The only cut you can make is straight acrossed, and that $100 price tag doesn't help the bottle come apart any better than a $25.00 cutter. You still have to go through the process of heating/cooling in order to get a clean break, and you still have to sand the edges down.

For $100, you would think it would allow alternative cut options (angled or straight), it would come with better directions, and design ideas, and that it would take much less time.

I even got the $35 leveler - which was even a bigger waste of money.

rating 4.0. Purchased for a wedding project. Works like a charm, but don't follow the directions for heating the cut seam with a flame.  The carbon residue is impossible to remove!  We heated a small pot if water then dipped the scored area in the water then into a bowl of cold water. We repeated the hot-cold routine until the bottom of the bottle fell off (usually in the hot bath).  Overheating the water caused uneven edges to form, so don't leave the burner on under the pot of water.  Reheat as needed to regain a higher temperature.

rating 5.0. The creator is stable and scores perfect lines every time I'm so excited to finally cut a bottle straight ! I tried building my own cutter without much luck. The creator is stable and scores perfect lines every time. Pouring boiling hot water and then cold has given me excellent results.
Dawn Johnson

rating 5.0. Easy instructions with no confusion regarding the cutter use or ... This item delivered everything it promised. Scoring the glass bottles was not a problem - breaking the glass was a different story all together.  I would like more videos or instructions regarding the glass bottle breaking process.  The first day of use I destroyed more bottles than I could have imagine.  I am sure that the trick is all in the thermal stressing.

rating 5.0. Works great. My daughter loves it Works great.  My daughter loves it.

rating 5.0. The BEST of three different devices I have purchased! I had bought two cheaper ones earlier in the year, but was not satisfied - not stable, too small, too many adjustment reguirements, etc.  But this one is really stable and the cutting device is really well designed - you just need to learn how to release the spring tension AFTER the bottle is in place so it does not begin the etching as you push the bottle downward into place on the device.  The instructions also warned agains using the tapping method which I think is good advice.  I had used the tapping method on about 30 wine bottles, some so dark I could not see where to tap.  The instructions advise to be patient using the candle heating method and then plunging it into cold water. Sometimes it took two or three tries but the smoothness of the cut using this device and the heat/cold water method is VERY much better than the tapping method.  The other aspect I like is that the device is long enough so that you can deal with the smallest and the largest wine bottles on the market without having to unscrew this or that to cut small or large bottles - all you have to do is move the cutting device and adjust the convenient rollers.  Simple, convenient, and sooooo effective and efficient!

thank you again,

rating 5.0. Does What It Says! Very easy to use with simple directions. Great product!

rating 5.0. Excellent Bottle Cutter! Although I have never used any other bottle cutter, I must say that this one is a great purchase. It is easy to use, though it may take a few bottles to perfect the technique. Watching the video for it helps, but the instructions are clear enough that the videos aren't necessary. Using the temperature shock method to separate the bottle has been the best method for me (so I prepare a container of ice water to dunk the top of the bottle in after each round of heat - it helps to have many bottles already scored). The bottle cutter is sturdy and well-made. Considering the process of scoring and the build quality of this one in comparison to other cutters, I am glad that I didn't waste time and money on a less-expensive one simply because of the price.

rating 5.0. BEST BOTTLE CUTTER EVER This is by far THE BEST bottle cutter I have ever used!  Super happy with this purchase. I was able to try it out before I purchased it (my sister has this cutter) and on my very first try with a beer bottle I got a perfect score line....and 99% of every bottle after that as well.  With my pervious bottle cutters I would MAYBE get 3 out of 20 bottles with a good score line.  --- And just an FYI: I use the cold & hot water process to break the bottles at the score line, seems to work the best for me.  Holding it over a candle for 3+ minutes gets really boring, really quick for me.  :-)

rating 5.0. WOW! I'm Impressed! After many failed attempts with other bottle cutters, I splurged and ordered the Creator's Bottle Cutter. WOW! The first bottle I cut was perfect! I hope it's not a "fluke". I did try another bottle that was very odd-shaped and that one cracked vertically. So, bottom line for me is to stick to round bottles. rating 3.0. ... 2 bottles using multiple scores and have not had good sucess yet Tried it on 2 bottles using multiple scores and have not had good sucess yet. Nothing like the video showed, but I believe that it will work, you just need to work through it to acheive your goal.

rating 5.0. Can't believe how well this works! This bottle cutter is worth every penny!  It is really easy to use.  I do suggest viewing the videos on YouTube to get a clearer idea of how to use it.  The instructions are a little unclear. I was cutting perfect bottles after the third try. I am cutting the tops off of beer bottles to make candles.  DO take the instructions seriously regarding only scoring the bottle once.  DO NOT go over a scored line twice, as this is what will cause the cut to be uneven or even cracking of the bottle.  I did a tea kettle full of boiling water and running cold water at kitchen sink to separate the cut, just pour the boiling water over the scored line and then run under cold tap water, and keep repeating until the top falls off.  Usually after two hot pours and two cold rinses. So simple! As for the reviewer who said that he/she couldn't get an even clean cut...I feel that he/she must have some eye hand coordination problems.  It is truly a wonderfully easy device to use.  AND.....BONUS! It is made in the USA!  Just a little light sanding of the edge with some fine grit sandpaper, and viola!  Nice smooth edges. For the few that had an uneven edge, I sanded down with a Dremmel tool. If you want to cut up bottles for any craft, this tool is AWESOME!

rating 5.0. Perfect Storage for Memories (and whatever else you need stored) The last couple years, my husband and I have gone on two amazing road trips and brought back lots of fun souveniers... the newspaper that was delivered to our room at the cozy bed & breakfast, seashells from the beach, postcards from tourist sites we visited, random plastic/paper bags with the logos of places we shopped... I like to save these items because they jog the fun memories. While I love having them, they were just taking up space in our office forever and I decided enough was enough.

After careful consideration, I went with the Pioneer Jumbo Scrapbook Storage Box because it was the ONLY storage box I could find with these dimensions. Indeed, I made the right decision. I bought 2 boxes, one for each trip, and they are the PERFECT size for holding my random assortment of items. I even fit in the 8x8 photo scrapbook I made with all the other souveniers. The boxes are sturdy, they hold a lot, and they look very nice.

I like that there is a place to label what's in the box, but my one complaint would be that there is no index card or label of any kind to place in the slot. This meant I had to hunt down an index card or two and cut them out carefully to match the little holder's size. I got it done and it works fine, but it would have been nice to have a pre-cut label to put in the label holder.

Even with that one small inconvenience, I'd have to say this is a really great product for storing memories of the assorted variety and I would highly recommend it.
