Since they fit perfectly in my Ikea Expedit bookshelf, I'm buying three more for other papers that need to be kept, but not displayed.

rating 5.0. Well made quality and highly useful!!! Bought this for my wife and her new polymer clay hobby. She needed a clean smooth reliable work surface and this glass surface is perfect for her needs!!!

The glass remains cool while she's working on it. This is important because her work area as it is happens to be where she wants it to be at the time. One day it might be in the living room in front of the heater, the next it might be in the kitchen. If the clay is getting to warm laying it on the glass and letting it cool and rest has it firmed up again in no time. Even when the living room is toasty warm!

Being she's just starting out she lacks a lot of the nicer supplementary tools like a square cookie cutter of various sizes and the grid lines allow her to easily make clean accurate cuts with her tissue knife. Very important since her stash of clay hasn't grown very large yet and she's using partial blocks of clay rather than full blocks while she's learning the techniques of making pleasing Skinner blends etc.

The glass is nice and thick but it's tempered so should it for some unknown reason break if dropped on the floor it won't shatter into a bunch of razor sharp splinters it should just turn to easily swept up chunks. This is of particular importance to us having kids. So we see this as an important safety feature as well!

The four corner covers are easily removable should you not desire them to be there. Personally I like this as it adds to the versatility in use. My wife however hasn't commented on her feelings about them.

Edges are all nicely finished with no sharp places or risks of being cut.

When everything is considered we both feel that ~$20 for this glass work surface is quite a bargain for what you receive!

It's certainly money well spent unlike the first pasta type machine we bought for her!!!

Well there you have it. Perhaps not my best review but truthfully how do you provide an in depth review of a piece of glass no matter how wonderfully it works?

I/we bought her glass surface with our own money for full asking price off Amazon receiving no discounts compensation or sexual favors for the review. It's my intent to provide honest reviews of items I buy and use (wife uses in this case) to help others make better informed buying decisions. Since I've spent the money I try to show the good bad ugly and indifferent as I'm able to do you can see it before you spend your hard earned money. It's my sincerest hope that my reviews help someone out.

If you find my review helpful please let Amazon and others know by clicking yes. If you think on the other hand it's a steaming pile please let them know as well. There's too many junk reviews on Amazon that serve no purpose and I don't want mine too be one!!!

Thanks for reading and I hope this helps someone out!

rating 5.0. Fantastic easy to clean mat with guidelines How have I survived crafting with out this????? I had seen a youtube demo done by Cindy Leitz, so decided it was right up my alley and had to get it. Since my local crafts stores don't have it and I can't use those handy 40% off coupons, I went on Amazon and ordered it (it is not expensive for what you get, totally worth full price).

The glass mat is so handy for measuring, my weak point, and makes cutting and drawing straight lines so much faster and easier. I use it for polymer clay, and all the stuff I use with my polyermer clay (inks, paints, resins, etc) and it is so easy to clean up. I use baby wipes for most cleaning, and sometimes i need to use a razor blade to scrap up paint or a thick glob of ink and mica powder globs that dried on it. Glass is such a wonderful surface to work on, so forgiving and easy to care for.

The size it perfect to work on, and working on glass just offers so much benefit over the silicone mat I had been using: cleans up easier, can make clay stick to it when doing certain techniques where you want the clay to stay put, see measure lines, degree lines, squares, and it leaves no texture to clean up on your clay. The glass mat is actually pretty sturdy as well, it is a thick heavy glass, not something that would make you worry it will break or snap. I think the only thing that could make this mat better is if it had a neutral gray color backing instead of white for color work.

rating 4.0. Sadly Disappointed, Getting Happier My glass mat arrived warped so that it tips back and forth trying to do anything on it, making precision work impossible . It was very poorly packaged in a large box with zero packing material, so maybe that was part of the problem. I have asked for a replacement, hoping this will be a fluke, as I am so looking forward to using this for many, many art and craft purposes, and it is just the right size for my space. If the replacement arrives in usable condition, I will up the stars on my review.

7-29-19 UPDATE: The replacement is an improvement, but does still have some unsteadiness problems. I am finding this fairly easy to correct. Because of the placement of the non-stick feet, the entire glass panel bows towards the center if much pressure is exerted on it. I am finding that strategically placed dense cardboard slid underneath provides stability. It doesn't take much thickness. If you have purchased magnetic vent covers from Home Depot to store your dies, the cardboard that comes in those packages is exactly right for sliding under the mat. It is very dense, and about a mm thick. It covers from the 3/4 inch mark across almost to the 9 inch mark, which means I need another piece cut 3-/4 inches wide to go under the entire mat. I plan to cover these entirely in contact paper so they can get wet without disintegrating, and be washable. The feet are not affected by the cardboard, so continue to do their stellar job of holding the glass firmly in place. I'm pretty happy. So now to using it and becoming happier still.

rating 5.0. Great brush set After 35 years of putting my artistic passions on the back burner, I've decided to let it flow. rating 4.0. I am satisfied with this purchase I am satisfied with this purchase. They come in a nice, sturdy, attractive ( the brushes are attractive as well) carrying case that has a nice sturdy zipper . The case material is easy to clean,  so even if I manage to ruin all of the nice brushes..the case is still handy.. I mistakingly left one of the brushes in water and it was ruined where the wood meets the metal, but that was my fault. All in all you can't go wrong with this brush set.

rating 5.0. Great product!!! This is such a steal for the price! I've been a painter/artist for a while and always struggle to find decent brushes (plus a bonus case!) for less than $50. Based on how often I use brushes and the work that I do, I needed a brand that would last a while but that could be replaced easily without spending a ton of money. This is the perfect product for that. The long handles are perfect for oil painting (and in general) and the brushes themselves have a great shine and snap. Plus, the case that comes with it is super handy. I would definitely recommend this to someone who needs products as frequently as I do or for a beginner that doesn't want to shell out a ton of money!

rating 1.0. and although the package itself was in perfect condition, 4 of the 15 brushes were totally ... I ordered these for my fiance, who recently started painting. As a beginner set, I wasn't expecting much, but based on the previous reviews, and the price, I was expecting more than what I received. The set arrived within a few days, and although the package itself was in perfect condition, 4 of the 15 brushes were totally unusable. Upon opening the pack, which I am glad I did before I gifted them to her, I realized the bristles were crushed, and falling out. I was forced to iron the bristles back into place, trim the stray bristles, and try and doctor the set into as good a shape as possible. These would be fine for a child, but if you're seriously thinking about painting, I would invest in something a little better.

current_product_reviews: rating 1.0. Brushes fell apart upon first use. BEWARE! Sad to say, I found these brushes to be completely useless. Upon the first use, the brushes began shedding fibers; one of them detaching from the ferrule in a clump.  The glowing reviews I see here online greatly surprise me. I advise everyone to steer clear of Mont Marte brushes.  The low price is doesn't matter if the product is unusable.

rating 4.0. Beginner set As a first time user and experimenter of Alcohol Inks, I found the set good for what it was worth and the amount of colors provided. As some mentioned, yes the bottles may squirt all over because of the provided pins, so it is highly recommended to work on a color safe space with gloves, masks and whatever means necessary to keep the permanent color away from ruining precious clothing, nails, etc. On Yupo, the inks worked well with some practice and extra media (alcohol). The colors seem to be somewhat similar and named after fruits and flowers, so color swatching was necessary not to confuse each bottle. Overall, they are a good starter set for a reasonable price. Packaging was lackluster so I placed the paints in a plastic container, upright. The plastic bottles are not sturdy and may spill if the cap isn't ensured tightly. These did the job and open the possibilities of trying other brands and colors. Made in China.

rating 1.0. Not a good product, because of the bottle Oh nooooooooooo.
The negatives first:
The biggest issue I have is contents under pressure with these bottles. Some quality control improvements:
They give you a couple of pins for opening the bottle. Unfortunately, the bottles have so much pressure inside that once opened- it blows up on your hand. Ink all over the fingers, desk and clothes- very messy. It says to use gloves on the package- I can see why... might want to use an apron too.
Solution- it would be better to use glass or a less flexible plastic for these inks with a larger neck- so the ink liquid does not get funneled and stuck at the top. The ink gets stuck in the tight neck of the product and explodes when the pin is inserted.
The positives- the colors are very vibrant, many options to choose from. Cool color names.

input: rating 5.0. awesome adhesive!! This really works great for all my wall decals. #1 trick is to oil your hands with vaseline or shea butter otherwise its hard to wash the adhesive off...lol...know this by experience...It does hold very well even on my textured walls but humidity will affect how long it sticks. I just peel back the decal from the wall where it isn't sticking anymore and spray the back of the decal and the wall letting it dry first on both before pressing it back into place. I love the fact it gives no off-coloring of any kind and is strong when done properly. Taking one's time with application helps.

rating 5.0. Perfect. I absolutely love this stuff. I use it to make my Silhouette Curio mats re stick. It just needs a light mist and it works great. I have accidentally oversprayed once or twice, and my paper ripped. But all I had to do to fix it was basically blot it with paper. Since I use it on such a small area and it only takes literally  one second, I use it inside, and I've never had a problem with fumes, or it sticking to everything. But since I use it inside, I don't usually turn it upside down to clean the valve, and as a result, my cap finally clogged. It's been almost a year since I bought it, and I just ordered 100 NY fat caps so I'll never have this problem again. And I'd guess my can is still approximately 3/4 full.

rating 2.0. CAUTION! EXTREMELY TOXIC - CAUSES CANCER! It sounded like a great product, good reviews. But here is language off the can, "DANGER! EXTREMELY FLAMMABLE - VAPORS MAY CAUSE FLASH FIRES! CONTENTS UNDER PRESSURE. VAPOR HARMFUL. CAUTIONS CONTAINS HEPTANE, BUTANE, ACETONE AND PROPANE. DANGER! EXTREMLY FLAMMABLE. Keep away from heat, sparks and open flame. Vapors will accumulate readily and may ignite explosively. Keep area ventilated during use and until all vapors are gone...VAPOR HARMFUL. May affect the brain or nervous system causing dizziness, headache or nausea. Causes eye, skin, nose and throat irritation. Use with adequate ventilation. Avoid continuos breathing of vapor and spray mist. To avoid breathing vapors or spray mist, open windows and doors or use other means to ensure fresh air during application and drying. If you experience eye watering, headaches, or dizziness, increase fresh air or wear respiratory protection (NOISH approved) or leave the area. Avoid contact with eyes or skin. Wash hands after using...DELAYED EFFECTS FROM LONG TERM OVEREXPOSURE. Contains solvents which can cause permanent brain damage and nervous system damage. Intentional misuse by deliberately concentrating and inhaling contents can be harmful or fatal. WARNING: This product contains chemicals known to the state of California to cause cancer and birth defects or other reproductive harm."

Okay, seriously people. I don't care HOW good it sticks. This is a can of death! No, but seriously. It doesn't say vapor MAY be harmful. It says it IS. Cancer. That is crazy. Why would we so interested to stick something that we risk cancer and spread toxic chemicals into our homes, lives and environment? There must be something less toxic to stick things...

rating 5.0. Very versatile easy to use glue Not even what I bought it for but I found a GREAT use for this. Do you have a suction cup mount for your cell phone or GPS that will not stay stuck to the window? Well do I have a suggestion for you! Clean the cup and windshield then with a little paper, protect the mount and spray the face with a liberal amount of glue. Let it dry, I let mine dry overnight, then attach it to the windshield where you want it. It will hold and hold and hold. You can remove it later with a little effort.

It also worked for what I bought it for. I spray a lite coat on the deck and attach the Tee shirt to hold it for screen printing. Works quite well. As with any glue, be careful of over spray.
rating 5.0. great stuff i was able to wash the stencil between uses, allow to air dry and the tack was ready to use again and again

rating 4.0. Great for die cut machine cutting mats I used this for my Silhouette cutting mat because it was starting to lose it's stickiness. Overall I'm pleased with it but there are a few things I noticed.
1. If you are using this for a die cut mat, it would be smart to tape something over the edges of the mat (the non-sticky edges) before you spray. I didn't do this and now the edges of my mat are sticky and they stick to the rollers of my machine when I am cutting something.
2. It doesn't create exactly the same type of stick that a new mat would have. Sometimes if I'm using normal copy paper, the sticky stuff will come off a little bit onto the paper itself.
3. I noticed I have to be more careful when pealing stuff off of the mat now because the sticky consistency seems to make it more stuck overall. This also makes it harder to get the little pieces off after I'm done cutting and it tends to leave a slight residue of paper fibers on the mat.

Overall, I'm pleased with the life this easy tack brought back to my cutting mat. I will definitely continue using it in the future because it is so much cheaper than buying a new mat. I've also found that cleaning my mat with baby wipes helps to get off the residue.
rating 1.0. Must be something better out there Tried using this product to glue a map to some cork board and it peels right off.  The cork is new, and that could be the problem, but word to the wise.

rating 4.0. SPRAY IT OUTSIDE or IT GOES AFTER YOUR WOOD SURFACES Using this for wall stenciling. There's something in this adhesive's formulation that likes wood. I was in a well-ventilated room as recommended in the can's instructions. I wasn't spraying in the direction of anything except the floor. About an hour later, however, every surface in the room felt like I were touching sand paper. Dresser, nightstand, etc. etc. The spray independently found it's way to the furniture and coated it. Luckily my furniture had some coating that allowed me to use dishwashing liquid and a scrubby sponge to remove most of it. Wasn't easy either.

After the 1st day of stenciling, I opted to apply the adhesive to the stencily outside before bringing it back inside. DOES IT WORK? YEAH. I found I totally didn't need the addition of blue tape. This product makes the mylar stick like one of those vinyl magnets, and seals very well every little nook and cranny. I just wish they'd do something about the airborne gunk. For now, save yourself a headache and only spray this out of doors. There'll be less mess, and you'll get more stenciling done without interruption.
rating 5.0. Does the job Great spray for holding down crafty projects, helping temporarily arrange cards, and keeping the mats for my silhouette cameo sticky.

rating 4.0. Great for Silhouette or Crickut Mat Rejuvination I bought this to spray on my cutting mats in order to extend the life. In addition to following all other posted directions (yes, read them!)  I used painter's tape to mark off the edges and only sprayed a light coat on the mat.  Worked great!  I'm getting significantly more life from my cutting mats so I definitely recommend this product this use.  I honestly cannot think of another reason to use it - but I'm sure there are many.

rating 5.0. Great for stenciling I stenciled my ceiling. Would have never been able to do it without this adhesive spray. It held a 20 x 30 stencil on the ceiling with no problem at all. You do need to let the adhesive dry just a little bit before you attempt to stick it on a wall.

The unfortunate side of using the spray is the tackiness it leaves on the stencil. Really hard to remove. Tried using Goof Off to remove the glue residue, but it's going to take forever. Still very worth the purchase for the difficult project.

rating 2.0. Not for permanent adhesion This is a good product for art or presentation products that 1) stay indoors, and 2) require the ability to reposition the work. If you're looking for permanence, seek another product. We laid down three boards of architectural images with this, and they looked perfect -- right until we took them outdoors for transit to another office. In the air and humidity they bubbled and wrinkled. By the time we got to the clients office and set up, they looked haggard, wrinkled, and just sad. I had to do some explaining. It looked completely unprofessional. This is pretty much the spray-on equivalent of a post-it note.

rating 5.0. Not too sticky This works very well for me. I have to make copies of insurance reports and need to cover up parts of them so only one patient shows up on the copy. I've been using paper clips to fasten blank paper "covers" and it's time consuming. I sprayed this on the paper covers and now it's much faster and easier to get the copies made. I've been using the same covers over and over for a couple weeks now and haven't needed to reapply the adhesive.

rating 5.0. Great product, does what it says, and it allowed me to make a very difficult task, somewhat  easy. Great product for multiple tasks. rating 5.0. Good for people with arthritis “I had purchased a set a while back for myself and am pretty happy with it. I got this set for my friend as a gift and I noticed the needles are longer. I'm happy that the company takes customer comments and suggestions seriously since I had commented about the length of the needles when using thick yarn. Great improvement. Might get another set. My hands have improved tremendously.”

rating 5.0. AWESOME HOOKS!!!! I love these hooks...they are comfortable to use and have the sizes clearly marked on each one...the bright colors are so cheerful...they are perfect...Thank you so much!!!!

rating 4.0. Great hooks for the price with caveats. I bought these hooks because I had carpal tunnel surgery in both of my hands 21 years ago, I liked the fact that I was purchasing from a military mom owned company and that the hooks were clearly marked and color coded with the sizes; the price is quite reasonable for crochet hooks of good quality. However, the crochet hook throat on the larger hooks, sizes I - L is much shorter than that of the smaller sizes. Over all they are shorter than the Boye hooks, and despite the increased width of the grip the place where my grip is comfortable makes them awkward to hold. They are a bit difficult for me to work with because of this, most likely because I have such large hands for a woman. Which means, unfortunately, any of the gentlemen crocheters out there who may be looking at these hooks because of the nicely marked and color coded handles might want to stick with what they learned on. On that note I don't wish to discourage anyone from purchasing these hooks, they are very nice and I haven't seen a size 7 hook in years.

Pros: Reasonable price, the hooks are color coded with the sizes clearly marked and a good range of sizes.
Cons: Short throat on sizes I - L and over all shorter than Boye hooks.

rating 5.0. These have become my go-to hooks! Great price, great hooks! I bought these last month on sale. These are tapered hooks (Boye style) not inline (Susan Bates style. ) The handles are so comfortable that I can crochet for 4± hours without taking a break. A rarely seen 7 hook is included this set. It took me awhile to get used to feel of the handles and I noticed my tension and hand hold has improved. I worked on 2 afghans with the hooks and it was a lot easier. I love how these hooks handle cotton yarns. Cotton tends to be stiff and the yarn almost glided on these. I'm really happy!

rating 5.0. Quite pleased! I am bery pleased with these hooks.  I am currently using the "G" hook with a yarn that is very "splitty" but happily, the hooks are smooth and handles the yarn well.  The rubberized end is very comfortable to the hand.  I haven't had a long session with these hooks but I think that the handle and heft of the hook will be less tiring to use.

rating 1.0. Mixed feelings I like the handles. They’re soft without being too soft. I think they could be just a little bigger for more comfort, but in all they’re good. The shaft and neck of some are horrible. I have some that are rough so yarn catches. I have some with scratches in the neck so yarn really catches and frays a bit. Company is aware and has been in “processing” for 2 weeks. It’s taking longer to process than it did to get to me in the first place. You get what you pay for. I should have gone with clover.

rating 5.0. Exactly what I wanted! I crochet quite a bit. I have a little Etsy business and crocheting is one of my favorite things to do. As I was looking on Amazon I really liked the reviews that were said about these Hooks and so I decided to try them. I was very impressed with the quality of the hooks and I love how they feel while I am using them. These are now my favorite Hooks and I will never use regular hooks again. They are exactly what they said they we're going to be. They are so well made. I would recommend these to anyone who needs a product like this. I'm so glad that I made this purchase.

rating 5.0. Good quality for the value Great hooks for the value. These hooks are fairly inexpensive compared to the ones at the store. I’m not so sure that it’s actually “ergonomic”. It has a nice grip and it is easier than just holding a metal hook. If you’re looking for actual ergonomic you’re better off with a ergonomic crochet hook handle by Boye.

rating 5.0. Great Set of Crochet Hooks This review has been a long time coming.  I was so excited to receive my new crochet hooks that I started crocheting immediately.  I cannot say enough about how these crochet hooks helped to improve my crocheting, and I can crochet for extended periods of time without my hand cramping.  Awesome set of crochet hooks at a great price!!!!

rating 3.0. ... compared to the in-line hooks but they're still very nice. I plan to work with them more and ... I find these a little more difficult to work with compared to  the in-line hooks but they're still very nice.  I plan to work with them more and not give up.

rating 5.0. Excellent set I would definitely recommend I've been crocheting for many years. I've always used the plain metal hooks, but as I've gotten older I've found my hands get cramped up after a while and my wrists start to hurt. I recently found I did not have a size B hook I needed for a project and decided to get an ergonomic handled one, as the smaller the hook, the more my hands cramp. As I researched, I discovered I could get a whole set for the price of about three hooks purchased individually. After more research I decided on this set. I like everything about the hooks. The handles are grippy and have a little give to them. I love the bright colors and how the sizes are molded into the handles. The three hooks I have tried have worked smoothly. The company reached out to make sure I was happy with my purchase. My only complaint (not really a complaint, more a wish) is that I would have liked to buy this set with a nice case to hold them, but Athena's set like that does not have the two smallest hooks.

rating 5.0. So Comfortable I am loving my new Crochet Hooks. I have Traumatic Arthritis and regular ones my hand starts cramping within a hour. I've been crocheting now for over 4 hours and I'm thrilled to say so far no problem with my hands. They are very comfortable to hold and use! I would Highly recommend these Crochet Hooks

rating 5.0. Stitches with many loops on the hook? These are what you want! These are really nice, actually have a long enough working area to deal with multiple loops. Tried them out and they immediately have become my primary hooks. Yarn glides nicely.

rating 5.0. Great Hooks by a Great Company I love these crochet hooks, feel very comfortable in the hand, I do have arthritis but no pain with these hooks.  I love the variety for the price. I did have a hook that wasn't very smooth, not only did I immediately receive a replacement hook in the mail, I received a couple of bonus products.  This company aims to make sure there's customer satisfaction so you can buy with confidence.

rating 5.0. These are perfect! These are exactly what I was looking for. I was worried that they would have a hard plastic handle but the handle is a nice grippy, rubber-type material. And the length of the hooks are perfect.

rating 5.0. Awesome Hooks Love these crochet hooks.  My hands tend to get cramped when holding my old ones.  No cramping with these. They are light weight and comfortable to hold.  I also love the fact that they have both the letter and millimeter number printed on them, the colors are pretty too.

rating 3.0. The shafts feel gritty These hooks do fulfill the promise of a comfortable grip, which was my main goal. A few drawbacks though, that people need to be aware of: the shafts of the common sizes are NOT extra long as claimed. There is no way to fit several stitches of bulky yarn on the shaft of the larger sized hooks. My biggest complaint though is that the shafts are not highly polished. They’re not rough and don’t snag the yarn, but there’s just a grittiness to the feel of them as you crochet. I’ve only ever worked with highly polished hooks, so it’s taking some getting used to. I’ll keep using them, because they do help prevent pain in my hands while crocheting, but I really wish they had a glossier finish.

rating 5.0. This set of high quality, excellent value hooks has inspired me to crochet again These crochet hooks looked kind of cute in the picture and at such an incredibly good price, I decided what the hey. I'll buy the set. Even if I only use then occasionally to pick dropped knitting stitches, they're a good price and I can stash an assortment of sizes in each of my project bags.
Well. My goodness! When they arrived (faster than I expected) I was beyond charmed. They are positively yummy! The handles have a little give to them to make them comfortable to hold but are still quite firm. The texture of the handle could be described as "grippy," almost like a silicone kitchen utensil that's been buffed. The handles will not slip out of your hands like a plastic hook might and they're warm to hold, unlike a metal hook. I haven't crocheted since I was a child, but now that I have this set I am inspired to return to the craft. These hooks are too nice to sit in the bottom of a bag waiting for this knitter to goof.
Perhaps best of all is that, by purchasing this set, you are supporting a military family-run business with a focus on just a few extremely high quality yet incredibly affordable products for crafters. It's clear the seller cares deeply about her products and her customers. You simply can't go wrong with this lovely set of crochet hooks from Athena's Elements. Treat yourself!

rating 4.0. Great for Beginners I really like these hooks, fairly comfortable for the most part. I really like these as an alternative just wish they were a bit thicker but just can't beat it for the price!  Recommend if you're looking to get your first set of hooks.

When using these hooks, they just don’t slide smoothly enough off the hook, and they are just a little too aggravating for me to use. Anyone out there who crochets a lot knows what I mean.

rating 1.0. Flimsy, poor quality wheel with muddy colors I purchased the JimKing color wheel "for amateur and professional artists." The very first thing that struck me was how flimsy the wheel is. It's constructed with three circles of low-grade, glossy cardstock that are riveted together in the center. This paper bends very easily and must be handled with extreme care to avoid damaging it. Greeting cards are sturdier than this product!

Ironically, the cardboard envelope in which the product arrived was of much better quality than the color wheel itself! The color wheel is not hardboard, as implied by the product location.

I've had color wheels in the past. They were much sturdier than the JimKing one, and their colors were far more accurate on the mixing side.

rating 5.0. Perfect for Food Photography , Graphics, and Baking This color wheel is easy to use, affordable and shipped quickly. Side Two is a "Guide for Color Mixing" that will be helpful for Baking. The wheel arrived in perfect condition (covered by a plastic sleeve, inserted into a thick cardboard envelope, then shipped in a plastic bag). As many others stated, the cardboard wheel is flexible and printed on thin cardstock. However, as a graphic designer, I can tell you that if a higher quality paper stock had been used, the price would have had to reflect that because paper stock is expensive. So for me, the trade-off is a fair one, and I'm happy with the purchase.

input: rating 5.0. Nice brush Ordered this to add to my ever growing hobby supply needs. Very nice brush and enjoy the shape of grip for prolonged painting times. Had a little issue with the shipping (the mail beat it up), but the seller was quick to replace my item and was very pleasant to deal it so I would strongly recommend buying items from them for anyone looking.

rating 5.0. Why doesn't anyone else make brushes shaped like this? I absolutely love the shape of this brushes handle. It is very comfortable and gives me a lot of control. This is very important because I have wrist issues and after a prolonged period of painting my wrist can start to shake. This brush allows me to paint longer without my wrist becoming fatigued.

Unfortunately this brush is very weak above the ferrule. While you would never put enough pressure on that area during actual use, it poses a problem during shipping. Making sure breakage is prevented is obviously up to the seller/shipper, but order this well before you need it just in case. ;)

The psycho's bristles weren't quite what I expected. I was looking for a brush that I could use to paint the eyes and teeth of my GW models. This brush does not hold enough paint at the tip to accomplish that. You have to hold this brush at a slant and drag the bristles in a line to make a mark. It is very useful for painting lines and edging. That just wasn't quite what I was looking for.

(If anyone knows which of The Army Painter's brushes would be good for painting eyes and teeth please leave a comment)

rating 5.0. "The Psycho" Aptly titled "The Psycho" brush is perfect for fine detailing in the most meticulous of details in many mediums including wargamer figurines, models of all types, dioramas and custom figurines using epoxy compounds and resin based paints.

rating 5.0. Great brush Really gets the fine details that I was looking for. Hard to blame crappy brush for bad pain jobs with this one. I also appreciate the triangle style handle. Makes it a bit more comfortable and precise.

rating 5.0. Great Brush This is a great brush. Period. I bought this for my son who is really into miniatures and it's great. it is super fine, and he is able to paint the pupils on a 28mm miniature. I would definitely recommend this to any miniature fanatic.

rating 5.0. A fantastic details brush. Can I tell you how awesome this brush is? I recently got into miniature painting, thanks to Kickstarting Arcadia Quest and finding myself surrounded by dozens of unpainted miniatures. Cool Mini Or Not's resident artist had painted a few of them and was posting them in the Kickstarter updates and I was amazed at how they looked. So I decided to try my hand at it. I've learned quite a few things in just the four weeks I've been painting and one of the biggest lessons (outside of using good paints and thinning them) is the importance of the correct brush.

Now that might seem obvious. And it is. But one area I kept falling woefully short in was the details. These miniatures are so small and the parts like the eyes, clean lines and small details require a fine touch and an even finer brush. Most of the brushes I found, even the ones marked "fine details" were too big for me and my clumsiness. You won't find me claiming to be an artist, but I want to try my best. This brush was a life saver. Now, I'm not as worried about eyes or tiny details. The tip is so fine that you can pickup just a small amount of paint and apply it...even if you have unsteady/untrained hands. It's a great learner's brush, too, I've found.

I would definitely recommend this brush for miniature painters who need a finer touch when taking care of fine details. It's probably my favorite of the brushes I own.

Note: I've read some of the reviews stating that the product arrived damaged. Mine did not, but it was ordered along with other items so it was in a box. The actual brush came wrapped in a bag and with one of those brush protectors over the tip.

rating 2.0. Not worth it. This brush frayed and became unusable immediately after using, I would not recommend this for miniature painting. A 6 dollar pack of brushes from walmart has lasted longer than this brush.

rating 3.0. Needs to be finer and keep a fine point Service was quick. I like the handle because of how it can't roll off a table and how it feels in the hand. The reason I only gave 3 stars is because although the brush looks tiny, the bristles splay out while using and can't make a super fine line like I want. It does not keep its point. I wish it did because I thought I'd found the answer to my painting needs but I find myself still reaching for an old 10/0 that I have cut most of the bristle off of to make a very fine line. Given the name of this brush, maybe that means I'm past the point of psycho? :)

rating 1.0. I'd be able to simple set a sturdy mag over my book and simply read it without ... This product failed to meed my expectations. My expectations were: I'd be able to simple set a sturdy mag over my book and simply read it without having to readjust it with each new paragraph. What I actually got: I got a large flexible, flimsy mag with multiple ridges that made reading quite challenging, despite the fact that the letters appeared larger. The mag's flimsy legs made it difficult to adjust to the book, which was more awkward because I had to readjust the position of the mag with each new paragraph. The flimsy string to adjust the mag around the neck did not simplify things either: the string is so soft and thin that it will not hold the mag into a firm, horizontal position. Shifting the position every five seconds is not quite a "hands free" experience, as the seller touts.

rating 4.0. They sent an e-mail displaying the unit like it is illustrated in the instruction pamphet I received my magnifier, I am 71 years old, so at first I could not understand the directions. I sent an e-mail to FiveJoy.  I told them my problem. They sent an e-mail displaying the unit like it is illustrated in the instruction pamphet.  I became impatient and did not take the time to do it right.  I finally got the lights to work and the unit is wonderful.  The lights are bright and the magnification of very good.  Thank you FiveJoy for putting up with me for a short period.  Forgive me for not being more patient when you tried to help..  Have a great day and LORD Bless You - Henry Pichon.

rating 2.0. Didn't meet my needs ... I am puzzled by the numerous highly positive reviews for this “Authentic FiveJoy product” which led to my purchase, perhaps I had unrealistic expectations for the ~$20 charged? The following bullets are my observations which may perhaps help other buyers considering this item. They are based on my initial evaluation after receiving the product.

1. It uses a plastic fresnel lens so perhaps one should expect some quality tradeoffs. However, the lens starts to distort the image sufficiently to cause blurring of text approximately 2” to either side of dead center. For reference the overall width of the lens is ~9.5” in “landscape mode”, i.e. with the longest dimension of the lens across the width of a letter size page and my eyes were about 7" above the screen and directly over it, perpendicular to the screen. By 3” from the center on either side there is color fringing aka chromatic aberration and bright purple edges to the sheet of paper are distracting beyond this distance. These measurements were taken using the lens with legs extended, so ~4” above the letter. Viewing a full width letter is not practical in my view without moving the lens back and forth across the page to maintain a sweet spot for focus and lack of distortion, which gets old really quickly.
2. There is significant distracting reflection from the top surface of the lens in a normally lit room. A slightly matt or “ground” type surface would probably eliminate or reduce this and improve the experience. My various attempts to shield the lens were not effective and made it awkward to use as well.
3. The legs are quite fragile and didn’t fully open on one side. They are adequate provided you treat them delicately. Leaning any weight on them won't end well!
4. The LEDs cast enough light in a dark room to read by but I did not evaluate for any length of time to see if it was sufficient to avoid eye strain as the light level was quite low. The battery compartment was fiddly to open and the use of springs on both ends of the battery compartment to retain and connect to the batteries were a substandard arrangement requiring a surprising amount of fiddling to fit the batteries. I could see the springs breaking or deforming quite easily from this.
5. Expect the unit including the lens to collect dust due to static. The dust isn’t easy to remove from the underside due to the corrugation of the Fresnel lens.

I am not sure what the difference is between this and other very similar products at close to half the price on Amazon but this product doesn’t meet my needs and wouldn’t work for me even at those lower prices. I intend to return this item and will continue to use an old but higher quality conventional glass 3.5” diameter magnifying glass for my purposes, and perhaps find something to clamp and hold that lens for hands free work. I hope this item works better for you if you choose to purchase since many other reviewers seem to find it satisfactory.

rating 5.0. Great beginner tools! This is a great starter kit for new poly clayers like myself. The black tools are especially helpful bc they have different textured ends so you can make all kinds of neat effects on your clay. The wood bark textured one is my favorite! The blue tools are well constructed, and the ones with the larger metal balls are a nice heavy weight, which really helps easily smooth rough or uneven edges. The white rubber ends of the blue tools are INVALUABLE! There are 4 different rubber ends and I guarantee there is one for every job! The acrylic roller works very well, although I wish it was a little thicker for my big hands lol Once I figured out what the piece of plexiglass was for (shaping) it worked great as well! ;)
Overall, I definitely recommend this set, it is a great buy for the price!

However, I still gave this product a 5 because for a beginner, the tools included are still worth the purchase. I mostly do my carving on the table covered in wax paper anyway, but I can use the little plate for the white clay to keep it from getting dirty. rating 5.0. Silicone tips are very soft The silicone tips are very soft, i wouldnt reccomend use on ceramics or terra cotta or FIRM polymer clay. I use very small amounts of rather soft clay, so they work like a dream for me. Dotting tools are different sizes and shapes, excellent scale range.

rating 5.0. Great quality tools and wonderful price for all of them! Because in the future i will most likely use them all :)
The only thing i would add, the roller wasnt totally perfect in my opinion - it would’ve been better if the “round edges” were sanded a bit or something, cause it looks like it’s been just cut and no “finishing” applied to it. If that makes sense.
The tools are in perfect condition; the round tips are smooth and look pretty high quality. I’m happy with my purchase.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Perfect for beginers and people getting back into sculpting This is a good set that gives you a good variety of tools. The roller is a little small and the glass slab is also small it's mean't for working on small things like flower petals in my opinion. I do also like how the case is big enough to fit everything even the roller and three bottles of sculpy finishes and bake n bond. The one thing you should be warned about is that the plastic tools are brittle and can easily be broken in the bag. I've already broken one that was my favorite it was a long narrow spatula tool with a ball tool on the other end. So keep that in mind and the plastic ball tools aren't completely smooth so they will leave texture . The metal and silicone tools are the best though.

rating 5.0. OMG where have you been all my life?! These pens rock! No more stupid tailors chalk, saved bits of soap, useless fabric marking pencils that barely show, washable crayons that lose their point leaving less than exact mark placement, transfer paper and wheel which you need to have a lamp as powerful as our sun to see… I’m done with all that. I only wish we could order refills in just the color we need most, which for me is white. I’m marking all my dark fabrics which I’m currently using (I sew apparel) with the white pen and it works brilliantly! I’m even marking the different pieces (like “A -Front, B Back, under collar, etc) so I never mistake wrong side for right side or front skirt piece from back piece. These pens are making my life as a sewist much easier and more enjoyable. Also I can sketch designs on muslin for hand embroidery (why I bought these pens in the first place.) Try these, you will never go back to your previous mark making tools again!

rating 4.0. Work well even after 3 gears These heat erase pens arrived intact, but no instructions as to how to use. The "pens" are in one bag, unassembled, empty. I work with vintage fabrics and hope these will erase without scorching.

Update: so thanks to helpful reviews on Amazon I finally figured out how to use these. They have been stored in my craft room for 3 years. Today I got them out to mark an embroidery design. The ink in the original pen was dried and not useful. But the refills worked fine. The seller says the ink is good for up to a year, so I was pleased after 3 years they are still useable.

rating 5.0. Leaves No Trace I have been using chalk and water erasable markers for years, but not happy with the results, especially on dark fabrics. I bought this heat erasable kit for a project I was doing on black wool. The white water erasable markers left residue and my embroidery took a beating from all of the rubbing. The hear erasable markers are a joy to use, they go on easily and cleanly. The removal is almost magical. I was skeptical, but my black wool was all black again and my embroidery was no worse for the process, French knots still rounded. I am currently using the product on a white background and I look forward to the easy removal process.

rating 5.0. I LOVE this wax! I LOVE this wax! I've made numerous candles using this 444 soy wax and I haven't been disappointed at all! The wax melts nicely in the candle container and because it has a lower melting point than 100% natural soy wax, I think the wax tunnels less when the candle is melting. This wax holds scents really well for both hot and cold throws. I use about 2 oz. of fragrance per pound of wax, which seems to work perfectly.

This wax does tunnel a bit after pouring (not nearly as much as 100% natural soy wax though), but it's really easy to fix with a quick blast from the heat gun. One thing I've noticed that helps reduce tunneling for this wax is to pour it into the containers at 160 degrees.

The wax came in perfect condition with out any other debris in the bag. The price listed is pretty decent. I highly recommend this wax.

rating 5.0. Easy melting, good value, perfect for candles and wax melts! This soy wax is such a good value! I would recommend buying wax flakes in lieu of the larger chunks because it's easier to melt.
Put the wax in a double broiler, over medium heat. For fragrance, I generally put 1 oz of fragrance oil in 1 lb of wax. It holds fragrance quite well, though the throw isn't as great as with a paraffin mix. However, for a cleaner, there's nothing better. (Perhaps beeswax, but that has a higher melting point.)

The wax burns well, and is long lasting! I love them for wax melts (and once the fragrance has gone out, I can once again reuse the wax too!)

rating 5.0. Good Bargain This wax was easy to melt in the microwave. It took about 1 minute per cup of wax. It melted clear and poured smooth. I used a glass measuring cup and it was easy to wash the wax from the glass while it was warm. This is a good bargain!

rating 5.0. Burns Nice & Slow This is some great wax!  It burns nice and slow.  I can't speak to its ability to hold oil, but my experience so far is that the smell is faint.  Maybe I am not adding enough oil.

rating 5.0. The candles turned out beautiful, I’ve used half the package and made 6 ... I used these wax flakes to make some candles with essential oils I also purchased from Amazon. rating 5.0. Great for emergency candles Power goes out frequently in the winter in the north east. I decided I wanted to make a few dozen emergency candles. This wax was super easy to work with. I put my Mason jars a large stock pot with water 3/4s the way up the jar filled with wax. The wax melted in no time but, you'll need to top off the jar a couple of times to fill it to just below the threads. I let the wax set a bit before I placed my pre-tabbed wicks so they wouldn't fall over. Once the wax was partially solidified on top I tossed in a book of matches and screwed the lid on. I now have virtually vacuum sealed emergency candles ready to go. 5 lbs will do 12 half pint jars to just below the threads with a sandwich bag of wax to spare.

I mix some oils with it to get a scent (but please do your research on this first to know which ones are ok/safe to use for this specific purpose...) Will certainly be buying this brand again!
rating 5.0. Awesome Great value, lots of wax! Still have half left over after making 11 teacup candles. Glad I didn't buy the 10 pound bag! Melts fast, dries beautifully. Will use this again!

rating 4.0. Perfect for beginners, so easy to work with. This was my first time making candles and this wax was so simple to use. My first candle was supposed to be a test candle and I was not expecting that I would be able to do much with it. But everything was so simple that no only did it come out colored but scented as well. The only issue I had was that when I added the color chips, I was excited about the color of the wax when I poured it, but as it cooled it got much lighter. That obviously had to do with the fact that this type of wax is naturally white, so it will lighten up any color. When I doubled the amount of color it was more vibrant, but still a bit too light for me. I'm still looking for a way to make darker more rich colors. But again, this was my very first batch of them. I would definitely get this same wax again!
rating 5.0. Burns clean Great price for such a huge bag of soy wax. It cools nice and slow leaving a nice smooth finish. Burns nice and clean too.

rating 5.0. making it easy to work with Convenient flake form. Melts at a low temperature, making it easy to work with. No scent to it, as specified, but it holds a scent very well when you add your own oil (I used high quality essential oils when making my candles.) All in all, I am very happy with this purchase.

rating 5.0. Works great! Melts easily and makes great candles. Note that with soy wax in general it is difficult to make it scented. Seems to not want to take no matter what I try.

input: rating 5.0. I'm In Love! OMG! This is, by-far, the best sewing machine that I have ever purchased! It is very easy to use, that's if you're a beginner! I am an advanced sewerer and it is absolutely awesome! Buttonholes are a breeze! Just set the desired buttonhole, put on buttonhole foot, and....walah!....machine practically makes the buttonholes for me while I sit there and eat popcorn! OMG! Can't say enough good things! It is quiet! Makes perfect stitches! OMG!...did I say how much I love this machine! ...and this is my FIRST computerized machine,,,,,but it will not be my last! OMG!

rating 5.0. Great machine for the price I have just sewed a few times on this machine and I have found it to be a smooth sewing machine.  I did try every stitch and it did great on each one.  I am anxious to get more sewing done on this to see how it handles thick material.  I especially liked the needle threader since that was what I was having a problem with on my older machine.

rating 3.0. LIKE  THE FEATURES, HATE THE CONSTRUCTION! THIS IS MY FIRST BROTHER MACHINE. I LOVE THE FEATURES, BUT HATE THE CONSTRUCTION. THE SOLID, PLASTIC  FRAME STRUGGLES ON SOME STICHES AND FABRIC.

rating 4.0. Beginner seamstress, loving my Brother *Update* I have been using this machine fairly regularly now and have learned that I have to take the bobbin casing out quite a bit to keep the stitches from bunching up or not showing up right. I tried a different needle, changing the tension, rethreading the machine (upper and lower), and everything else I could think of. Nothing worked but cleaning out the bobbin housing. While not really a problem, very annoying to have to do in the middle of the project. *Note, I am using Gutermann thread most the time.

This is my first well operating sewing machine, I used to have an antique one and another Brother (can't remember which one), but my husband was the only one brave enough to use either of them. I decided November 2013 to buy my own and use it at least semi-regularly, sewing clothes and doing projects for me and my family. I researched for months before deciding that this was the best option for me. I wanted a machine to be simple to use, and that could grow with me as I progressed from novice to intermediate,etc. This fit the bill. I have made pillows with this machine, and one dress for my daughter. I love how easy it is to use and how quick it is to complete a project (compared to hand sewing that I did before). I have no issues I wasn't able to easily resolve on my own. I am now looking forward to adding a serger to my sewing tools. I will update my review here if anything changes. :)

rating 5.0. Wow - Price - function Great I have an embroidery machine, so I don't use the letters, but the rest of the stitches are great. This is an easy to use machine. I did my very first dress (a colonial costume) and it was great. They speed, the stitch selection the Button Hole were so easy and really wonderful to have. I was able to do great hems, blind stitching, reinforced stitching.

The machine is light weight making it easy to move around, the threading is so nice for those of use who can't see the hole in the needle :-) and the bobbin loading is as easy as dropping the bobbin in. The LCD is great of selecting and adjusting the stitches, such as how tight or for some how wide the stitches are.

Unless you are a professional, this is a wonderful machine and great value for the price.
rating 1.0. Not Opened the box yet I've not opened the box yet!  Had no imediate use for it.  Was a good price.  I'll either sell it or get brave & try to sew one day!

rating 3.0. Making funny noise after 6 months I love Brother products. However this machine started making whirring sounds after 6 months of use. Letting it rest before using it again. Will write more after...but satisfied with the product

rating 4.0. nice machine at a great price I was surprised to find such a nice machine for about $200!  The machine arrived, ready to sew.  I pulled it out of the box and immediately started testing the stitches - there are so many it took awhile to go through them.  The best part of this machine, imo, is the choice of buttonholes... there are several and with the buttonhole foot (included), they are beautiful every time.  I am very pleased with this purchase.

rating 5.0. Great little machine! The machine came promptly and was exactly what I ordered. The functions it has are quite nice and I have already used it for several dresses!  It has been awhile since I have sewed, but one of the things I disliked the most was threading the machine- This machine, has a really wonderful system that practically threads itself and makes changing out your thread so much easier!  Happy sewing!

rating 4.0. Nice machine I like it a lot. It has so many functions. The only bad thing is that I can't find a tutorial to use it. The DVD doesn't give you a lot of info and I didn't find any useful videos or books in the internet. I wish I have more help to use it.

rating 5.0. Wonderful machine! As a sewing beginner, I was not sure I should get a Brother sewing machine. I had been using my mother's Singer (Athena) for the past few months and really liked it, but it was a little out of my price range. I decided to get this machine for some of the features and the excellent price. There are a few things that I like more than the Singer. First, the drop in bobbin. I was used to having to pull up the bobbin thread on the Singer and have quickly gotten used to skipping that step. Next, the speed adjustment switch. It keeps me from sewing too fast at times and I can set in on the lowest speed when my daughter is sewing. I love that the hand crank has a mark on it to show where the needle is in its highest position. But you don't actually need that since there is a needle up and down button. It was also new to me, but I have quickly learned to use it and appreciate it. I find the light is very good, the machine is fast and quiet. It was very easy to set up and begin to use. I chose it over a few of the slightly lower end Brothers for the hard case and the table, which are both useful. I think the features I mentioned earlier were even more beneficial to me. Finally, there are more than enough types of stitches. The monogramming feature works easily. The feet are easy to change, and the digital display tells you which foot to use.

Finally, I will say that the needle threader on the Singer is unique--you can do it with one hand. This machine's mechanism is slightly more difficult, but now that I've mastered it I've not threaded the needle by hand even once.

This machine is fantastic! It has renewed by interest in sewing and seems to be a quality product that will last for years. I will update if I have any significant issues with the machine.

rating 5.0. Recommend Sews beautifully.  Instructions easy to follow. I have used it to make pillow cases so far. Have many more projects to sew. It is lightweight, comes with its own cover, and many accessories. Great buy for the money. I love it so far.

rating 5.0. Great sewing machine! I've never worked with a computerized machine and I was a little skeptical, but so far I absolutely love this machine. Very easy to use, and although I have used a sewing machine before, this was very easy to learn how to use. Some people complained abut the automatic threader but it has worked for me every time. The only complaint I have is that with some of the stitches it doesn't do great with thicker fabrics, but overall i'm very happy with my purchase.

rating 5.0. Love this machine!!! I have diabetic nerve damage and do not have good control with my fingers and have a hard time seeing. I'm new to sewing and my biggest fear is threading the needle. This machine is PERFECT!!!  Load the bobbin AND the upper thread is so simple. I'm learning to sew and this machine is great!!  If you are a beginner like me I would recommend this machine. The added benefit it has drawings on it to remind you how it works! I've been using it about a month and NO problems. If you are a BEGINNER to sewing this is the machine for you!!  Good luck!!

rating 4.0. Brother Sewing Machine I'm not a knowledgeable sewer but was willing to try.  The video helped me get through some things and once the Holidays are over I can get back to learning more about it.

rating 5.0. Brother XR9500PRW works better than expected, easy to setup, easy to change presser feet, easy to thread top and bottom. The XR9500PRW has so many features for the cost. Sews exactly as the manual states with ease.

Highly recommend this machine.

rating 5.0. Great machine!! This is the first sewing machine I have ever purchased and I did buy it due to the good reviews and the lower cost compared to similar models. And I am not dissapointed! I've owned it for just over one month and so far I love it. Very easy to use once I got the hang of it, the manual and instructional dvd are both very easy to understand and very informative. I love all the different stitches and the monogramming (although very basic) is still very cool. And I haven't had any problems with the tension that apparently some reviewers have had. I haven't used it on any super heavy fabrics yet, but so far it works like a dream on light weight to medium fabrics. All in all, I'm so happy I purchased this machine and would definitley recommend it!!!

rating 5.0. Wife loves it! My with loves her Brother sewing machine.  She has never watched Project Runway but this one was a smidge cheaper and came with the extra stitches that she enjoys.  A great little machine.

rating 5.0. Easy to use, great first machine kids love it (4+8) and I get tons of use out of it. the threading was easy to learn, and the screen displays the foot and settings clearly. Also easy to adjust, very intuitive. I don't know how this will hold up against more advanced use, but for my occasional pillow cases, stuffed pet animals, blankets, quilting, shirt initials it's great!

rating 2.0. Not Good For Light Fabric I returned the first one of these machines I had bought because it was not doing the decorative stitching consistently and was leaving slight gaps in the stitch.  I returned the first for another one that was the same exact model. The second one did the decorative stitching a little better, especially when used on thick materials, and it seemed okay. However, after one and a half small projects of basic stitching, I'm still noticing gaps in the stitch. My thoughts are it is user friendly and has great options but I'm not sure performance is up to par with what I would like to use on a regular basis. It can sew through thick materials like a dream, but on light weight fabrics it just doesn't handle them well.

rating 5.0. Great machine Great machine. I love all the quilting settings and the quilting table. The machine allows me to customize any item I want with the lettering option. The different speeds selector keeps you at the speed you want. No more accidentally pushing the petal down too hard.

rating 5.0. Pleasantly surprised!! I've owned several sewing machines over the years, including a Viking machine which my daughter "borrowed."  I bought this machine to have one light enough for traveling, but with a good variety of stitches.  I've used it most recently making a handbag out of heavy duty denin and was pleasantly surprised how well it handled even several thicknesses of denin or strapping.  If I have any negative, it is that the machine is too light.  If it's not placed on a sturdy table or cabinet, it tends to bounce.  My daughter and I were sewing together on a folding table, but when she removed the much heavier Viking, the Brother bounced.  I finally had to put the Viking back on the table just to hold it down.

rating 4.0. I love this machine! I started sewing with a manual Singer Promise 1409. While that one is a strong machine and has served me well, it is very basic with just 9 stitches and is VERY loud. Goodness, my whole table shakes when I use it! While I originally wanted to stick with Singer, none of the machines in my price range, (under $200), could match the Brother xr9500PRW.

This Brother is an amazing, affordable upgrade! Quick set bobbin, needle threader, and needle down position are my favorite things. Not to mention the tons of decorative stitches! It is so quiet compared to my Singer. Also, the fact that the pedal is not needed for any if the intricate stitching is wonderful. It does get slightly louder when using the start/stop button, but is sill nothing compared to the Promise.
I made a list of all the things I wanted my machine to be capable of, including;
-adjustable needle position
-drop in bobbin
-wide selection of stitches
-quilting table included
-multiple presser feet included (with a free motion quilting foot)
...among other things I may not be thinking of at the moment. This machine covered them ALL.

Also, a HUGE plus is that the bobbin is not only drop in, but, it is a quick set bobbin...no holding the top thread and turning the hand wheel to pull it up! Simply drop it in, thread through tension spring and around plate. An attached cutter cuts to perfect length. Replace bobbin cover and begin sewing!

The only other thing that would make this machine even more perfect, is if it had an automatic thread cutter. Not a huge deal for me since I've always had the cutter on the side of my other one.

I truly feel I made the right choice by purchasing this machine.

UPDATE:5/19/2014
I am still in love with this machine, overall. However, the light on the machine is very weak, as someone else stated in their review as well. I have an Ottlite that I use, so it's really not a huge bother to me at home. If I take my machine some where to use that doesn't have good lighting, it could possibly be a strain to my eyes. I've tried out just about everything on the machine and it does very well! Still very happy with my purchase.

rating 4.0. Bought to use at quilting club. I like the machine for its lightweight making it good for taking to my quilt club. I was frusterated with the thread spool pin. My thread kept getting caught around the pin and causing the thread to break. After about 5 times in half an hour, I went into the sewing store where we were meeting and bought a table top spool holder with a thread guide uptake. Problem solved, but that was another $12. I have only used the machine twice because I have a Brother Innovis 4000D that I use at home.

rating 5.0. Got it for my 12-year old for Christmas! She loves it! I really can't say enough about this machine. For the money, it has a lot of bells and whistles, yet it's easy to use and not intimidating. Since Christmas (8 days ago), my daughter has made herself a dress and several outfits for her little sister's American Girl doll.

The feet change out easily, and it sews like butter. My daughter has tried out the zipper foot, the button hole foot, and the embroidery foot. They all work very well. She has also attached the quilting table, saying that it makes managing material while she's sewing easier.

The automatic threader was a big hit with her, too. Plus, she loves that it's computerized. For the money, it offers quite a few stitches. She has experimented with a few of the decorative stitches, and it has done well.

I got the Brother Enthusiast last year, and paid twice the money for it, but I wish that I had gotten this machine instead. It sews better. Plus, I love that you can store the accessories in the storage compartment on the front of the machine.

My daughter loves it so much that I now have to limit her time on the machine. It's all she wants to do now.

rating 5.0. Awesome Sewing Machine! I spent hours Reading the reviews and comparing this sewing machine to others. Finally settled on this one and am so glad I did! The features of this machine are awesome. Love that I can do decorative stitching without having to use the foot pedal. The needle up and down button is a great tool on this machine. Threading the machine and the needle couldn't be easier. The drop in bobbin is another great feature, Would highly recommend this machine to any beginneror moderate sewer or one who is using it for crafts.
rating 5.0. Love it Pretty good machine. I had help from a professional on a project and she was surprised of how good it handled heavy sewing. I love the fact that it's easy to use.

rating 5.0. Wonderful!! First of all, I am about an intermediate, to advanced seamstress. I have sewn many things over the years.

I was given the Brother CS-6000i for Christmas. It had defective parts. I contacted Brother and they were not much help. All I needed were replacement presser feet because what was included was defective. Brother was telling me all about thread tension and could not see that I knew what I was talking about. It was frustrating.

On the other hand, Amazon has been amazing! This was a gift and Amazon tried their best to make my experience with them the best it could be. Amazon did not fail!

After much browsing, I was able to settle on this one for its features and price. I have this as a replacement machine and I couldn't be happier. I started using it right away when it came home. Once I fixed the tension and made sure I have threaded properly, it ran very well. It is a quiet and fast machine. The LCD has a backlight that helps SO much! Also, the LED lighting makes things brighter, too. I hemmed jeans without difficulty. I sewed buttons and the back of them looked wonderfully clean without a lot of looping that I have seen from other machines. I sewed on some sheer fabrics as well. I was pleased with how well it fed through and did not damage my material. I even tried out the basic monogramming on a foam insulated lunch bag. No problems at all! No problems feeding fabrics at all. I am loving using it!

I am looking forward to completing more projects with this machine.

My machine came with an instructional DVD (this is better than the instructional manual) AND a see-through open toe quilting foot that were true bonuses because these were not listed in the original description. Once you watch the video, for the threading and start up, it is easier to refer to the written manual for future reference.

I am appreciating all the time that Amazon took to make sure I was satisfied with my gift.

rating 5.0. Awesome machine I blow up sewing machines every year. I was in the process of making a wedding dress when the last one blew. I panicked found this online. It shipped right away and I was back in business. It even did a better job than the other 10-15 I blew up before it. I also bought the extended warranty, JUST IN CASE.

rating 5.0. Nice and quite! I was surprised at how quiet this machine is! I'm used the the basic Singer machine, and that thing is obnoxiously loud and shaky, like a train coming. This machine is smooth running and quiet and I love it so far!

rating 5.0. Great sewing machine Bought this for my wife and she loves it. She has two much larger machines that do embrordery but this little machine is great in our camper. It's light weight and medium size makes it perfect to move around. It's well made and of quality material.

rating 5.0. I can't say I love it- but my wife does This was a gift for my wife.  She has never used a sewing machine in her life.  She read the instructions and watched the video- and started practicing.  Now she is starting to venture out and do some really cool things.  This was a good machine as it was very easy to understand and use.
rating 5.0. Brother sewing machine I have been pleastantly surprised by the ease of this machine. Its been quite a few years since I've sewed and its been fun!

rating 5.0. Great Machine!  Brother XR9500PRW limited Project Runway Sewing Machine Found the machine very easy to use.  Being a computer machine had to get use to the fact things are done a little different.  Followed the clear directions in the booklet and everything went smoothly.  I have been sewing for over 50 years, so this is not my first rodeo.  The price was great from Amazon, even though I did not need it, I couldn't pass up the great deal.  I have 5 other machines!  My deciding factor for purchasing it was the 100 different stitches, and all the extras. It was purchased as a used, in good condition, but I think it was a just a return, from someone who didn't dare to use it after it was bought!!!!!!!!  I could be wrong.  Have a great day, and Thank You Amazon!!!!

rating 5.0. Perfect for me! I purchased my [[ASIN:B005GXPOAM Brother XR9500PRW Sewing Machine]] last November when I decided I wanted to learn how to sew.  It was in the price range I was looking at so that if I decided sewing wasn't for me, I wouldn't feel terribly guilty for the purchase.  Thankfully, I am loving my new hobby.  My machine sees some use at least 3 days a week.  It definitely does everything a beginner needs, while having enough features to grow into for a good while.  I've completed several projects, some of which have been rather complicated, and this machine has been a trooper.  It's quick and easy to set up and easy to use and I am very pleased with my purchase.

rating 5.0. XR9500PRW I've been searching for a long time for just the right sewing machine and I finally found it!! Great Price! $89.00 less expensive than buying it directly from Brother!!! Free Shipping and Handling is a must! Love all the different stitches and the alphanumeric stitches as well! Performance is outstanding! Tried all sorts of different kinds of threads and it works beautifully!
I've been sewing for about 53 years and this machine is the "TOP DOG" and I worked with all the brands and Brother is the BEST!!!!

rating 5.0. Love this machine My quilting instructor has one and remarked about how light weight it was. I wanted a light weight machine to take to class, This machine meets all of those requirements. I can leave my larger more expensive machine in my sewing room, at home.

rating 1.0. Machine Lasted a Month This machine worked for about a month before the light flicked out and entire unit started to smoke. Sure, it's under warranty but there's no way a new machine like this should have started to smoke? The Brother service department had no interest in speaking with me and simply referred the problem to a local service center.The instruction manual is poor and the support on the Brother website is incomplete. The CD that came with the machine wouldn't play on the PC or DVD player.
rating 5.0. wife loves it whe won't write the review herself, but she is amazed at this machine. she used to sew on one 20 years ago, and really likes the ease of use and features of this one.

rating 5.0. Amateur Review This is my first attempt at using a machine. I am a 51 year old male and have no prior experience. I just thought I'd master this sewing thing before I die. That being said, I love the ease of this machine. I was sewing in minutes after watching the info CD. If you are a novice take some time to learn about the basic sewing tools, buy them before you get started to avoid frustration. Only a few bobbins are included. Buy more along with thread before starting. I bought all the basic thread colors to start with and made bobbins. I used almost all colors already. By a yard of muslin and spend some time trying all the stitches. I followed youTube vids to create different stitches while I was using the machine.
Overall I am satisfied. Since December I have made repairs to clothes and upholstery, and even altered some clothes.
rating 5.0. So far I love it. I've made several items with my new machine now and I love it.  It's so easy to use and fun to work with.
rating 5.0. Great machine I bought this item for my daughter. she ahsbeen sewing ever since. She has made christmas presents for her friends and co-workers. She loves it.

rating 5.0. Very nice product Love having a new sewing machine!  My old one is .. Well, "seasoned" at best...I got my Singer sewing machine about 35 years ago, and it finally quit moving forward....so time to either haul out my mothers 40 year old Singer or get a new one...I could not be happier!  I will admit that getting a machine that was "computer-oriented" intimidated me, but I have overcome that and realized it is a good thing. rating 5.0. Brother XR9500 is awsome!! After visiting my brother and sis-in-law last summer I knew that I needed a Brother. So far it is a dream.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Definitely featured packed for the money! I was using a bottom of the line Janome Mystyle that needs to go in repair. I decided to get this machine as a backup because it definitely has more features and was priced $100 less that my Janome that I got three years ago! I do a lot of free motion quilting.

I just got it today. A lot of things irked me at first, but with some minor problem solving everything must be just a learning curve thing. I thought the quilting table was a little unstable at first. Whenever I weighted my hand on the far left side it would tilt the machine... then I was more careful about seating the table at the machine and made sure that the legs were properly locked out and I didn't have a problem. The threading mechanism was definitely a good thing to have, but it's definitely another learning curve item. It took me several tries to do it successfully. I love the way the bobbin is filled and threaded. I like to use the Supreme Slider for free motion quilting but the throat space was a little small for it. Luckily I had an old one that I had accidently chewed up the edge, so I just sliced the chewed up edge and it fit in the throat space just fine. Love the needle position feature.

I usually set the stitch length to 0 to free motion quilt, but wasn't able to get it down to 0. I had to fiddle with the thread tension for awhile, but I even with fiddling I never got any severe loops or problems. In fact, it was more consistent with good stitch quality than the Janome. I changed out the foot to the included free motion quilt foot. I found I wasn't able to quilt as accurately than my Janome. The hopping mechanism of the foot would advance the quilt on it's own which means I had to use more muscle tension to keep handle the stitching. The LED light produced glare on white fabric, and the clear plastic on the foot made the needle position difficult to see. I found that I had to crane my neck more to be right on top of it to quilt accurately. Again, this is probably just a learning curve thing. I definitely like the incandescent light on the Janome better because the lighting is more diffuse and covers a larger area thus no glare on white fabric.

I logged onto Amazon today to print a copy of the invoice to keep with my warranty. I noticed that they had lowered the price $30. So I opened up a Help chat session and they proceeded to refund the $30 plus tax! I LOVE AMAZON!
