rating 5.0. The BEST healing mat I've used Olfa self healing mats for 25 years . . .and I still have and use one that I bought in 1989. I purchased a new cutting table last June and ordered three of these mats to use with it so I would have the option of removing mats when the leaves of the table were folded.

Some complain of a noxious odor . . .but it was gone from all three of my mats within 48 hours or so of removing them from the shipping carton.

They heal instantly and don't dull my rotary blades and the measurements are accurate and very functional . . .an excellent product and I know . . .just as my oldest mat is still in service, these will last me for several decades.

rating 3.0. A few complaints Any "self-healing" cutting mat gets 2 stars, and this gets an extra star for being reversible, but that's as far as it goes.

This mat is very thin. This is my third mat (last one purchased 12 years ago) and it is only half the thickness of my previous mats, which causes a real problem for me. Because the mat is so thin, the lip of my quilting ruler doesn't catch well and keeps slipping...do I need to point out how frustrating and how many yards of fabric ruined because of this? Because the mat is so thin and flexible it requires a really hard, smooth surface underneath or the mat curves and bows under pressure. This is an issue for me because I am short and like to do my cutting on the floor, and it is impossible to use this on a plush carpet or uneven floor or even an uneven table and get cuts straight and perfect (no problems with the other mats), and I had to cut a piece of plywood to put under it (which is a pain and extra expense). Because this mat is so thin, it really needs to be stored flat or it will develop a curl. When I noticed how quickly it curled resting on the floor against a cabinet, I drilled a couple of holes in it so it could hang on the wall as I just don't have another flat space to store it.

While all "self-healing" mats will eventually score, this has scored quickly. Using brand new blades, after several dozen strips being cut, I have permanent scores where I was cutting. Not only can I see them, I can feel them when cutting.

My final complaint is it stinks -- a skunky sort of smell. I can smell it when I walk into my sewing room, and I have to take frequent breaks from cutting as the smell gets to me.

rating 1.0. TOXIC WASTE????? Smells like it is made from TOXIC WASTE or decomposing mice trapped inside a wall after eating poison. It is flimsy as all get out and would have to be used on a solid surface such as a table or bench. Due to the smell I never put to the use it was intended for so I cannot comment on that aspect.

A better though out and made product would have been more money, had a more solid working platform and perhaps far less undesirable oder.

Thanks to Amazon's return/refund policies I'll be receiving a refund.

It only took a couple hours to completely air out and I didn't even bother leaving it outdoors. I couldn't be happier with my purcahse and I would definitely recommend this product and seller.

Original review:

First of all let me say, this mat has already made my life easier. I'm glad I got it. It's a good size and now I can properly use my rotary cutter. It fits on my table perfectly. My lines are nice and straight now. It's a great smooth work surface and an essential work tool.

When it first arrived, I let it offgass in the garage. That was a week ago. I brought it in the house to use it, but then I have to put it right outside again because it smells up the whole room. It smells like a skunk who has just come home from a long hard day working at the plastic factory. I sure hope it wears off soon!

I use a Olfa rotary cutter on it as recommended but when making a curved cut it still left a light slice in the mat that did not heal (hence four stars)... Needless to say I was really disappointed.  I thought I could continue to use these over and over again.  So do beware.  I give them a 3 star because they were great for my initial project, but because they dry up after the first use, they were no good to use again.

rating 4.0. Fun for Decorating Glass I saw this thing about decorating wine glasses on Pinterest and decided to do it for a bridal shower I was hosting. These made it really easy. The markers make it really easy to make designs and get creative. The colors are a little more neon than I expected. Also, occasionally they would go a little dry and so I would press hard to get the paint out, which then had it dribbling down and making a mess. I would wipe it off quick and no one knew the difference, but still a pain.

The glasses were a hit (I'll put a couple photos up in customer images). I did bake them in the oven for a short amount of time as instructed and they held up....to a few washings. The one I kept for myself is now getting a little...weird. It was a polka dot one, and the dots are starting to move and my husband is concerned that I am drinking the oil paint dots. His concern is probably valid, it has half the dots it used to. Oh, and that is with hand washing. Dishwashing these bad boys is a disaster...you lose half the paint and the rest is faint and water-spotted.

Anyway, I know it sounds like a lot of concerns, but it really was awesome and a big hit. These made it incredibly easy (each one done within 10 minutes) and I would happily do it again. (Oh, and I am sure these are good with other projects too.)

rating 2.0. Colors change on DIY sharpie Mugs I bought these for the DIY sharpie mugs seen all over pinterest.
They do stay on the mugs- but that is becuase i let them dry for 24 hrs, then put them in the oven and let the oven warm up to temp of 425F and then continued to bake for 30 mins.
However the colors do change!
The purple comes out pink
the pink is still pretty pinky
the green is like a seafoam green
the blue (do two coats) one coat comes out brown, 2 coats like a very pale blue, but looks almost identical to the green
the orange- is more of a rusty brown.

overall not impressed that the colors were so different from their cap. wish there was a warning

rating 5.0. Works on ceramic mugs if sealed in the oven afterwards. I was hesitant about buying these to decorate white ceramic mugs, because I wanted the design to last at least for some time. However, so far they seem fine (I'll update this review if it changes). Here is what I did: bought mugs at the dollar store (maybe the cheaper / thicker ceramic works better?); drew an intricate design on the mugs: the paint went on smoothly and once a surface was dry you could another color on top of it. I then put the mugs into a cold oven (I put them in before preheating) and turned it on to 350 for 30 mins. I then turned the oven off, and left them inside for several hours until the oven cooled off completely. I've since washed the mugs by hand - NOT dishwasher (nervous to do that) and the design is fine. Things to note: out of the oven the colors come out a bit darker/ not as bright as they look when just painted. As an experiment I put one of the mugs into the oven twice and that made the colors darker and more muted, but I wonder if it means that the paint will be more permanent.

rating 3.0. Not for Designing Coffee Cups, Apparently These are fine as long as you're not "baking" them on ceramic for permanancy, because the colors will warp and fade. Too bad, that's what I bought them for.

input: rating 5.0. Best White Glue on Planet I bought a small bottle of this glue while in Eastbourne after the shop owner told me she couldn't keep it in stock because customers loved it. The glue dries quickly, leaves no mess, doesn't over wet the card stock, no overly sticky fingers if you touch it and the black applicator doesn't get clogged like everything else I've used. I'm getting rid of at least 12 different glues I own because my search for the perfect glue is over. I'm hoping that the applicator on the large size is as perfect as the one on the small bottle, but even if it isn't, it's the glue I'm after!!!!!

rating 4.0. Darn Good Glue! I had this glue for 2 years prior to opening it. The tacky glue had dried up in the bottle and the blue glue stick wasn’t holding. I am so impressed with this glue. input: rating 3.0. It was easy to assemble and looks nice where do I start??  the metal legs were damaged when it arrived. all of the wheels were bent , the threads that the wheels screwed into were improperly welded so the table never stood up properly..  I removed the wheels and put felt pads on. the table top is not formica , so do not cut directly on the table with a roller cutter or blade. it will cut the surface. I intend to replace the top with formica . You can use blades and roller cutters directly on the surface with no damage.  It was easy to assemble and looks nice . the drawers are a plus. the table with the sides up can hold quite a bit of wieght.

rating 1.0. Nightmare to assemble. With drill took 5 hours, wheels do not roll. A nightmare to put together.  I took me 5 hours and that was with a drill. The wheels are cheap plastic and they did not roll either on my ceramic tile floor or on a rug. I called product number for assistance at 2:14 PM and I did not get a return call and it is now after 5:00 PM.  I was able to leave message but recording added, “if you need a part you can submit request in email.”Table has variable height but two people are needed to raise or lower it. The table is heavy and table top is very hard and will not easily scratch.  One screw on bottom shelf did not line up.  Once set up in place table is very functional. rating 4.0. Wonderful table in spite of assembly issues I love, love, love this table. It has everything I was looking for in a cutting table for my craft room; small footprint when not in use, storage, stability, movability and style.  However, it also has several things I don't like. Although assembly was rather easy, it was also difficult for several reasons. The wrench included did not fit the nuts included. Many of the screw heads for the hinges were crooked preventing them from being used (there were more screws than were needed so I did have enough). Three of the knobs for adjusting the hieght do not screw in all the way. It was totally missing the pins which lock the sides in place when raised. The casters are too small to make moving on carpet easy. Luckily I had help with assembly so these issues were not  roadblocks to a completed table. I needed to use this right away so a trip to the hardware store was necessary because I did not have the luxury of waiting for replacement parts.

I bought a large ironing mat also and it fits nicely on the table too. The next project I’ll use this table for will be a large diamond painting now that I have space to do it.

rating 4.0. The table is a wonderful addition to my sewing/crafting room The table is a wonderful addition to my sewing/crafting room. I like that the height is adjustable. Two of the legs did not have predrilled holes large enough for the knobs to fit into. The company immediately sent two more legs that work great!

rating 2.0. Screws missing-assembly diagram tiny/little text-no customer service The two-star rating reflects the problems of assembling the table AND the fact that important screws were missing. The table components are sturdy and should work well as long as the substitute screws I had to use don't come loose. Had everything been included, the assembly instructions easier to follow, and all the connection holes properly sized and aligned, this product would have gotten a five-star rating.
Assembling the table was more difficult because of the tiny diagrams (magnifying glass helped) and the misaligned connections. I am not a home handyperson, so would have appreciated larger diagrams with more text to describe direction of part placements. The assembly was "guess-and-go" or re-do at times. Several connections were difficult because the metal components didn't quite line up exactly.
One of the last steps is screwing the eight hinges to the drop leaf sides and the main table section. This could have been one of the easiest steps, but some of the blue screws were missing and one that was included was so small it fell through the plate holes. Because I needed to use the table immediately, I scoured the house to find some similar screws. Unfortunately, the screw heads should be larger, so I'm concerned about the strength of the finished product.
I didn't care to go shopping in-store for the correct screw during the pandemic, so I contacted the company via email and phone several times since February 6 (phone always goes to voice mail and the email was never answered). Since I was careful to describe what I needed, it would have been simple (and good customer service) to send me the missing screws. However, they have not.

rating 3.0. rating 5.0. Good deal on a versatile cutting/craft table. It took a while but was easy to assemble. The hardest part is the MDF top pieces due to the weight. The little wrench provided was the wrong size for the wheels, but the small end did work for the nuts on the extensions. I recommend a drill with the proper hex bit for the screws -- much easier than using the hex wrench. Also, a drill with the right size screwdriver bit is really needed for the hinges or you hands will hate you afterwards. I didn't like the provided wheels (I hate those split plastic things -- they actually damage my bamboo flooring). I replaced them with solid rubber wheels that you can get from Amazon or Home Depot. I did the top a bit differently than the instructions. I put the hinges on the center top and then turned the unit upside down and attached the framing to the center top. Then I laid each side, one at a time, on each side and screwed them on. It made it a lot easier to line up the 4 holes in the top where the top gets screwed to the frame (because I could walk all the way around it to get the holes lined up). If you do it the way the recommend you shouldn't walk on the extensions to get around the unit and line up the frame/top screw holes. I'm a 71 year-old female and if I can do it you can too. I live in Hawaii and most big/heavy stuff arrives damaged. The box looked pretty beat up on arrival but the interior packing was excellent. I didn't notice any shipping damage and was pretty surprised by that. There were a few factory defects such as flaws in the metal finish (like bubbles and rough areas under the paint. But all in all, it is a steal for the money. Other vendors sell similar units for about $500. The adjustable height system is a bit awkward, but once you get it where you want it just leave it. I was using my dining table and it was low enough to be a back killer. I'm thrilled!!! Thank you Amazon.
rating 5.0. Cutting table Absolutely love this table. Very easy to assemble, sturdy, love the adjustable height  and large surface for cutting fabric.

rating 5.0. Great Table. (Pls check out my assembly comment.) The product is perfect for my paper crafting needs. I love that the height is adjustable.

Assembly: I knew from other reviews that assembling it would take about an hour. It probably did. The instruction Require 2 people for a couple steps. I was stubborn and did it myself.

I was very impressed with labeling and packaging. Everything was labeled and the screws were shrink wrapped to a board. This was a huge help. The only small issue I had with the instruction was that the picture of assembling 9 to 4 shows 9 as being about the same length as 4. 9 is ~25% the length of 4. I tried to explain in an attached picture.

I still have it 5 stars for assembling. Why? Because it really wasn’t difficult. It was just time consuming.

Shipping: This was not good. When I arrived at home to get the package, I was very disapponted. I was shipped box 1 of 2 of a trampoline. (Now if it was an entire trampoline, now that would have been fun! Just kidding, I still would have returned it). The damn thing was so heavey that it stayed in the same spot where UPS left it. The poor UPS guy who came to get it almost didn’t take it, because it weighed so much. I hope that this was a singular mix up at the amazon warehouse and won’t happen to you. ;)

Overall, this table is perfect for my needs. I don’t have a separate crafting space. I use my living room. It allows me the freedom to transform my living room into my crafting room quickly and easily. When the family wants to using the living room, The sides easily fold down. It is stored in a closet.

rating 2.0. What you see is not what you get... This is not the heat gun I was expecting to receive. This one says Nicole on the actual gun tool and is an ugly lime green color. I'm pressed for time with a project so I can't mail it back and order a new one in time. Hopefully it works and functions well. I have a great heat gun set at home but was looking for something small like this while I'm traveling and the pretty teal one in the photo caught my eye. I wouldn't have ordered this one if I had looked at more of the reviews and photos.

UPDATE: I finally got to use it on a larger scale (20 pairs of resin earring studs) and didn't have an issue some people mentioned with the plastic melting at the tip. It does however smoke a lot from the holes at the bottom near the cord while you use it and for minutes after u turn it off smoke keeps coming out like something is burning on the inside. Also I used it to pop resin bubbles like my other heat guns and it doesn't do a good job of getting rid of the bubbles (that's literally the only reason I needed it). It gets rid of some bubbles if you hold it over them for a long time but you shouldn't have to hold it over your items that long and risk burning them while dealing with all the smoke and fumes coming from the vent. It then made a few pieces CREATE bubbles like when water boils and you see a ton of bubbles coming to the surface. More than half of the pieces I was making (made all at once because they are small) still had bubbles no matter how long I babysat them to pop new ones and that's never happened with any other heat gun (eventually there should be no more bubbles). So that's over $100 worth of product that literally is garbage now because the heat gun didn't do its job. You get what you pay for. Also I contacted the seller the same day I made this review and never got a response and that was weeks ago.

rating 5.0. Highly recommend for crafting needs!!! It melts the powder very quickly, however I turn it on and let it heat up for about 15-20 seconds before taking it to my embossing powders and they melt almost instantly and I rarely get warping. The trick is to keep the heat gun moving and not keep it in one place for too long. I love this product and would highly recommend for crafters. As far as other uses like electronics repair or anything else, I'm not sure if this would work for that. rating 5.0. Great little heat gun tool I bought two of these tools for my high school art classroom; my students have used them for curing embossing powder, heating/manipulating recycled plastic water bottles and shrink film, and for speed-drying paint and plaster.  These heat guns have a nice fit and feel in the hand, give off just enough heat to do the job but still very safe temperatures, and the outer casing is plastic so they stay relatively cool. My only small complaint is the little wire stand attachment does not work well; the stiff tension from the power cord makes the thing hard to balance on the wire stand.  Not a big deal, I have the students put the heat guns facing down in a coffee can so I don't use the wire stands anyway, but it's a design flaw.

current_product_reviews: rating 1.0. Beware of this tool For those who purchased this heat tool and for others considering purchasing it, I must tell my experience with this product. I recently purchased this heat gun tool as I use it while embossing some of my homemade greeting cards. I used it breifly shortly after I purchased it and did not notice any burning odor or smoke coming from the gun. Last night I was working and notice some smoke.  At first I thought the very light smoke was possibly common since the gun was new. I l turned the heat gun off and left it cool then again was using it for my craft project, before I knew it the gun was smoking greatly. I turned it off and again and as I did I notice  the same thing some of you users notice, the barrel was smoking. Almost immediately the gun caught fire. Flame came out of the the nozzle and continued to burn what seemed to be a few minutes. I can't say for sure as I was so startled by the situation I could only think about keeping the flame away from any flameable objects and to keep the flame away from me to prevent injury. I am grateful that the damage was contained only to the product itslf. I plan to write to the manufacturer about this problem before someone DOES get hurt.  I don't know the cause of the fire as the gun was too damaged upon inspection to determine whether the barrel was aligned improperly or if the gun had a defect which caused it to over heat. Reguardless of the cause, I want to warn anyone using the  piece of equipment to PLEASE be very careful!!!m

However, I did not test for durability in the dishwasher because it is sold as indoor and outdoor vinyl. Therefore, I am trusting that it will be okay. Crafters highly recommend this product.

input:
rating 5.0. I love to sew so I purchased this to help me ... I love to sew so I purchased this to help me with applique patterns. After checking out all the YouTube videos, I have found that the possibilities are endless. I have had this one day and I have so much fun with it, I don't know why I hadn't purchased one in the past.

With ScanNCutCanvas, which is free and, you can download your own images and it will convert your JPEG images into SVG so you can cut whatever you want and so far, everything has turned out perfect.

rating 3.0. now that I have figured it out I like it. I am using it to cut out ... It took a while to get it to do want I wanted. But, now that I have figured it out I like it. I am using it to cut out product labels from sticker paper. It kept spacing my items away from the border on the mat. Finally I was able to resolve the issue by using the online software and moving my designs closer to the edge of the mat. I then downloaded my layout and used the drawing feature to draw the template. I scanned this drawn image into my computer and then used Publisher to layout my labels on top of the photo I scanned in. After printing out my labels I loaded them into the scan n cut and used the cut feature to kiss cut them. Now I have custom sized product labels.

Could have been soooo much simpler if the scan function worked accurately. But, It had trouble finding the edge of my image even when I used a bold black line around them. When it did find the edges and I would save a template the cut was always about 1/4 of an inch off.

rating 5.0. I Love it!  Great Machine. Easy to use. If you read the instructions and take your time, you should not have a problem with this machine.  Watch the many videos on the internet for tips and ways to use and maintain your equipment.  One thing I learned recently....if you think your blade is dull poke it several times through a ball of  foil, increase the depth of the blade and/or check the "pressure" of the blade.  I had to do all three.  It just takes a little patience but it gives extra life to your blade.

rating 4.0. Really love the software (ScanNCut Canvas) - My favorite toy! There is a learning curve with the machine.  If you cut too small an image, the blade can wreck.  It's major fault is that it doesn't do the really small cuts well, but I do love my machine!  Really love the software (ScanNCut Canvas), and the other capabilities!  Great for designing with a secondary software.  Cuts cardstock best, not so good with too light a paper. The mat is not very sticky, they do not recommend that you use an adhesive spray, as it voids your warranty (see youtube:  [...]). If you do use sticky spray, you may have to clean the bottom scanning glass (the machine will not recognize the mat).  If you cut through the mat, use duct tape on the back.

rating 5.0. ... ScanNcut thoroughly before ordering it but to my deep disappointment it was not the much more expensive model that ... I thought I had researched the Brother ScanNcut thoroughly before ordering it but to my deep disappointment it was not the much more expensive model that can only be purchased through a dealer.  It is the one that reads embroidery files and cuts them to be sewn on with embroidery machine.  So if all you want it for is paper cutting svg files etc. you will be very happy.

rating 5.0. Great Machine I love everything about this machine! It works to increase creativity and productivity. It offers a professional industry level technology at the cost of a hobby makers price.

rating 1.0. Hate it.... I hate this machine. I thought it would convert pes applique files into cut files but so far no luck.. Yes I have watched youtube videos.. The rhinestone part is fairly easy, and later I may change my mind, but right now at this moment this machine was a total waste of my money... for me the screen prompts are not clear as to what to do, yes I just hate this machine.

rating 5.0. Love it! Love, love, love it. Takes a bit of getting used to but works great. Not a big fan of the scan n cut web site that lets you design your own stuff though. It is very limited in what it lets you do.

rating 3.0. Not as precise as I needed. I was planning to create some illustrations using paper. The workflow was easy going from Adobe illustrator to sag, even though you have to think of small details how it could cut better. But I was disappointed: I would cut better myself with a sharp X-acto. Maybe my expectations were too high? OK, I am Swiss and I like precision... I tried different stock paper, but it didn't cut better.
I wonder if Silhouette Cameo does a better job? Or a more professional machine. But for those who don't try to do "pro" work, I think it's an OK machine. Anyway I decided to return it.

rating 5.0. I'm one happy lady... I received my scanncut 2 CM350 yesterday afternoon and I have to say, I am overjoyed that I chose this machine. It is so simple to understand and use. I am very pleased. Since the day I ordered it I have been watching you tube videos and seeing all the wonderful things that can be done with the Scanncut. I have been using a Cricut for the last year and have never been satisfied with having to buy cartridges in order to get a font or design that I wanted to use. The scan n cut comes with so many designs, and so many ways to get what you want. I haven't purchased my activation card yet but have been busy working on the free scanncut canvas that is available on the Brother.com web site, loading my own designs onto a flash drive and seeing my own work cut. You will not be disappointed with this machine. I am not particularly computer savvy, but I must say working on this machine makes me feel like I am. I am looking forward to all the signs, quilts, cards and more that I am going to be able to do with the help of the Scanncut.

rating 4.0. love it, It helps me cut some shapes that ... love it, It helps me cut some shapes that I can't with x-acto knife but should be more specific about blades in the instruction
and the mat is too good, sometimes it left so many mark from the colors paper after cut

rating 1.0. One day shipping for the new machine would have been nice. Instead I will not get it for three ... My machine would not load the mat. I purchased additional mats at a local store. They also would not load (I got an error message saying the mats could not be recognized). I followed all the suggestions. The glass was clean. They were all scan n cut mats. They were loaded correctly (or tried to load correctly). There was no dust, wrinkles etc. I tried recalibration and any other suggestions. Nothing worked. One day shipping for the new machine would have been nice. Instead I will not get it for three days. Hoping the next one works or I will be purchasing a silhouette from a local dealer.

rating 5.0. Great machine! This was a huge investment for me. I have not used it much so far but find it easy to use over other machines that I have purchased in the past. Very light weight and easy to move around in my office.

rating 4.0. I love it! It just takes a lot of watching ... I love it! It just takes a lot of watching videos and asking for help from other users. I have made about 10 projects with it and overall, I am happy with my purchase

rating 5.0. LOVE it! Wish I had gotten it sooner! I've been thinking about getting a ScanNCut since I was introduced to it at my local authorized Brother dealer when it first came out. Finally I was ready to make the purchase when I received Amazon gift cards from my children this Christmas. I've had the machine a little over a week and have already used it to make appliques for several sewing projects. My first project was to cut a shape directly from a design that was already in the machine. Came out great and soooo easy for a first timer to use! My second project was to scan an image from a book into my computer, save it as a .jpeg and use the free cloud ScanNCut Canvas application to convert it to a format my machine would understand. No problem transferring the design to the ScanNCut and cut out the image. My third project was to try out the rhinestone starter kit that came with the machine. All in all, I think this is a fabulous buy for the money. I'm having a great time!! Since I already own the Brother Dream Machine sewing/embroidery/quilting/crafting machine and the terrific Brother 1034 D serger (which I bought here on Amazon), I'm in Brother heaven with my toys. Today I used the ScanNCut to cut out some heart shape appliques which I fused to fleece scarves I'm making for my twin granddaughters (age 6) For Valentine's Day. Prior to that I used my serger to finish the edges of the scarf and my embroidery machine to embroider adorable redwork bunnies with heart shaped noses and the girls' names on the piece. I can't imagine how I managed without a ScanNCut!

rating 5.0. Broken parts....Broken heart :( I wish I could write a good review on this product.

I couldn't wait to get my new cutting machine. When it finlly arrived I took it out of the box carefully and read the Quick Set up guide. I proceeded to open the machine and put the blade in when I noticed the rubber band thingy, that has grooves in it, to pull the carriage was very loose. At closer look I saw that it was broken. Uggghhh...I just spent $300.00 for a broken machine....what a let down. Of course I am returning it and will order another one in the hopes that it will be working properly and not with broken parts. You think an expensive machine and a manufacturer like "Brother" would have done a quality control check before they ship and sell their products.

When my new machine arrives I will be writing another review in hopes that it works and I can give a better rating. Keeping my fingers crossed that I won't be disapointed.

January 30 2016 Update with new machine.

Received my new one machine and I am very pleased! I actually bought the new one from a different seller. Package came intact with no broken parts. I haven't played with it much, just the scanning and cut feature. It does cut very precisely after scanning in so I am very happy about that as that is the reason why I wanted this in the first place.

Can't wait to explore more things I can do with this.

rating 1.0. If you are interested in the wireless connectivity you will have to pay and extra $50 which turns out to be a pretty worthless e Sadly, the direct cut function for stamping is still extremely weak.  I have tried several Julie Nutting Stamps and the ScanNCut feature barely recognizes the lines regardless of the settings and does not cut correctly.  If you are interested in the wireless connectivity you will have to pay and extra $50 which turns out to be a pretty worthless expense as for that price you can easily buy a larger USB stick or an external drive.  There also, unlike the Cricut is no cable to connect to your laptop.  A cable is much less expensive than $. Sadly, this machine still does not live up to its hype.  Pretty disappointing as I have purchased both machines and am not happy.  I am more happy with the Silhouette and while the cutting stamped images is much more cumbersome..it works.

rating 1.0. Just what I expected from Brother, arrived BROKEN! Just what I expected from Brother, arrived BROKEN! This is the 3rd ( and LAST) Brother product I have purchased that does not work..... Bad quality and poor customer service has turned me off altogether.

After being frustrated with the quality of the cuts and the giant clunkiness of that machine I purchased the ScanNCut2 for comparison. I am now a complete convert to this new machine. I love the background scan feature that allows me to choose the exact part of the patterned paper I want to cut. The wireless activation is well worth it.

rating 5.0. I am so happy with this cutter I am so happy with this cutter.  I have had Cricut and Cameo and this suits my needs for quilting much better than the others.  The 1/4" seam allowance is so important to quilters.  Makes life so much easier.

rating 4.0. I love my scan n cut but the directions I have ... I love my scan n cut but the directions I have don't seem to match the model I have. Quite confusing! I am seeking out a solution to make my mat sticky enough to hold the paper down but not so sticky it is hard to peel off.

With that being said I am a bit of a technophobe. I love that I don't need to use the computer fir this!!!! I have used it to make several stencils, die cut stamps, and have used a lot of the words it comes with on cards. My machine arrived on February 3rd. Today is April 7th. In just over two months I have used this to cut out over 100 images. This week I started downloading images from graphicstock and using them to make stencils from the backs of 12x12 scrapbook paper pads. Using them with acrylic paint makes them more durable.

This is my first electronic die cutter and I used it on the day it arrived. I am a stay at home mom. It came on my special needs daughter's birthday. I was thrilled with the results I got ! Some of you may understand that I opened it after she went to bed and that is NOT the time I am at my best.

rating 5.0. Buy this!! High quality, beautiful ink After purchasing a cheaper ink set to experiment with resin I quickly realized I was missing out on on truly Vibrant translucent color, so I purchased this set. It does not disappoint!!
First of all I love the design of the bottles, each ink is clearly and true to color labeled on the cap. The box has become ideal storage, keeping bottles snug with each other and upright. When used in resin for a Petri effect the ink moves and disperses beautifully. Also can be used to dye resin a solid, translucent hue easily without affecting curing. Colors are juicy and vibrant, very pigmented, and play well with each other without getting muddy.
Highly recommend anyone interested in a set of alcohol inks to purchase this set. Provides you with every basic color you can want and at such a good price. I really like that this set includes a clear blender, I’ve used it in Petries for an awesome dispersion effect. Also helpful for mixing my own tints, a cheaper option to buying other colors haha. I use a jacquard white ink with a lot of my projects, but if Trex made a white pigment I would buy it!!

rating 5.0. Excellent. This VersaMark Pigment Inkpad is perfect for embossing using detail powders. Images stamp perfectly every time. I always clean my stamp if using VersaMark Inkpad and color ink. Other option is to have 2 of VersaMark Inkpads - one for clear stamping and one for color stamping.

rating 5.0. Embossing must have. Decent price.  This stuff is so fun to use with glitter or metallic powder on cards.  A must have for papercrafters!  You will have to put in some extra effort to clean off your stamps after use, but so worth it.
rating 5.0. Best! I’ve tried an inexpensive brand and never got the results I’m now getting with Versamark. Now that my embossing is successful, I need more stamps and powder 😩

rating 4.0. Versamark Pigment inkpad This works very well for embossing.  I was also thankful that the name of the cleaner needed to clean my rubber stamp was mentioned as there appeared to be some problems if you did not use the right solution.

rating 5.0. Great for text stamps This is exactly what you need to use for heat embossing.  Believe me! I thought I did my research, and I bought white ink.  That worked okay, but I still wasn't getting the look I wanted.  I wanted a crisp image.  I was making wedding invitations, and I had a lot of text.  This ink did not bleed on my cardstock, and it had a satisfying sticky sound when doing the stamping. It made all the difference for the thermal embossing.

rating 5.0. Use for monotone color stamping colored cardstock or as a base for embossing Love this product to use for Project Life/scrapbooking/card making. Use to stamp on colored cardstock for a monochrome look, or as a base for embossing with your favorite colored embossing powders

rating 5.0. Another useful tool I have used VersaMark for several years.  When applied to a rubber stamp then stamped on paper it leaves a pale impression similar to a watermark.  Repeated on a sheet of paper, it can be used to create a subtle background when you want something that blends in rather than standing o.  I don't know of any similar products.  However It is necessary to clean the stamp as you would when using a dye or pigment ink.  It is one of my basic supplies.

rating 5.0. Must have for papercrafting Multiple uses: watermarking, use with embossing powders, pearlescent powders, and chalks. Excellent product.

TIP: Purchase a VersaMark pen and an inker refill. Possibilities are endless!

rating 5.0. Great embossing inkpad This inkpad is excellent, the versamark ink gripped my fine embossing powder and created such lovely detailed designs.  I sadly used up the ink in a matter of 3 days from making gift boxes, so stock up on the refill bottles because this inkpad dries very quickly.  Better to have plenty of versamark ink to grip the powder than leave faded embossing, it just looks terrible.

rating 5.0. Save your money and buy what works ! So happy with this watermark stamping pad. I went with another brand at first and the pad came dry as a bone ! Don’t waste your money on other ink pads , you can see for yourself the pad is full of moisture in the picture . Very satisfied.

rating 4.0. Great for crafters This inkpad was rated pretty high on Amazon, and it wasn't too expensive either.  I like that it works well for embossing projects, and that the ink pad is well saturated.  I think I'll get a lot of use from this ink pad and won't need to buy another for a while.  I've made several cards and embellishments for scrapbook layouts, and it's still has a lot of product left.

rating 3.0. It works... I have been taught to rub my stamp verses dabbing it and with this particular product the pad isn't very well fixed to the case. I don't know how to make it stick...maybe glue.

rating 5.0. Works great I've attached an image to show the potential this ink has when used right. Works great no complaints from me.

rating 4.0. VersaMark I saw this on several craft videos for making cards. It is a great item to add to any craft arsenal.

rating 4.0. Best embossing stamp to buy but please change the 2 ... Best embossing stamp to buy but please change the 2 set to one this only comes with ONE pad not TWO!

rating 5.0. Love this ink. I'm new to crafting so take this as you may. I recently got into resin crafting and now card making as a way to do something other than play video games when I'm bored. I decided that I wanted to do some embossing since I already had a heat gun for resin and went to Michael's and picked up their brand. It was horrible and really sticky. My brand new stamps were covered in goo that took forever to get off so I looked at the reviews for this and ordered the other day. This was not nearly as gooey as the Michael's brand and it came off wonderfully from my stamps under hot water. I can now actually see the details in my prints. Very happy with this product and will likely buy colored ink from them as well.

rating 5.0. A staple product This is a great watermark pad. I use mine mainly for embossing and metallic powder. It sticks really well and works wonders! I included a picture of my old one (which still works great after 100s of cards and a year od use).

rating 5.0. The Brand everyone told me to get Bought another brand for embossing and it did not work well. Got this brand as everyone in every video said to get and my embossing projects look so much better. Great fun!

rating 5.0. Great inking pad for embossing! This is a great inking pad for those that love heat embossing... simply completely press you stamp on the pad and stamp the surface you are embossing.  Immediately sprinkle embossing powder on the freshly stamped surface and treat it with a heat gun.  Even months after stamping, the embossed stamp will look new and as such, this product is highly recommended for its quality and ease of use!  Just make sure the properly close the pad after use or it will quickly dry out... trust me!
rating 4.0. A must for heat embossing It works great for heat embossing.  I'm sure there are other uses for it, but I've only used it for embossing and have been satisfied with it.

rating 5.0. The best stamp for embossing This is such a good stamp pad to make beautiful embossing! I bought this and the tsukinek embossing powder and made some gorgeous cards! great quality! the best for embossing!

rating 3.0. New item should be clean it looks like it has been used and expired My new ink pad for embossing just opened it and the pad has black something all over on it and the packaging for it has a has expire date says 11-12-18 help this is so this is so disappointing

rating 5.0. Fantastic Product This works fantastic.  Tried it out and worked great with my stamp and when I put the embossing powder on it and used the heat it turned out beautiful.  Will be buying more.  Loved the way it has changed - or added more beautiful options - to the way I make my cards.

rating 5.0. subtle & perfect LOVE this ink pad. Perfect for tone on tone stamping (subtle) or used with embossing powder for resist or other specialty techniques. I lost my last one and was going to get one from the local craft store but couldn't due to the pandemic. Enter Amazon...happy to have a VersaMark pad again.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. One of the best products out there! With the Corona Virus shutting down so many businesses, I have had to order this on line.  (Our local Art Supply store is shut down) Best product I have found to do embossing and chalk stamping.  Perfectly clear on it's own, so it Does not effect the color you are using.  Great for even very fine embossing powders.  Excellent detail.  I also stamp with the Versa Mark, and then use a cotton ball to rub chaulk dust into the image to get rainbow effects, and it works perfectly without smudging or blurring.  One very nice "trick" I have used is to press a dried leaf into the Versa Mark pad, and then sprinkle with gold embossing powder and then use a heat gun on it.  The Versa ?Mark holds the powder onto the leaf without having it blow off, and allows me to CAREFULLY melt the embossing powder onto the leaf.  Love this stuff!
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Really nice set The clear stamps make it easy to position the words right where you need them because you can see through the stamp to the underlying surface you're stamping on, especially if you use a Misti positioning tool or an acrylic stamping bloc.  The sentiments are very sweet and can be combined with other sets to make just the right sentiment for any occasion.

rating 5.0. Fantastic tool for card making!! Love this very easy to use tool!  Had not even heard about these until I watched a Jennifer Maker video that showed how the Misti made it possible to do multiple techniques on the same stamp without getting blurred edges or smudged prints!  It makes is possible to use one stamp over and over on multiple cards and get completely different effects.  And if you're doing a limited edition of the same image, this tool makes that flawless and extremely easy to have the image be in exactly the same place on each card, over and over again.  Can't say enough good things about this really useful tool!

rating 5.0. Going to fit right in with my card making supplies! Have only recently discovered the versatility of the "Clear Stampd" and how easy it is to position them on a card or other project.  When they come as a stamps and matching die cut, it's really fantastic!  The detail and clarity of the images is perfect and I'm sure I will be using them for many years come!

rating 5.0. Great selection for the money!! Love the included carrying canvas "bag."  It's actually NOT a bag, but a roll up holder that keeps all the brushes secure, protected and organized.  I now have a very easily portable selection of brushes for all my painting needs from oil to acrylic to watercolor!  The quality is very good, and I now have 36 brushes and a durable carrier for about the same price that I once paid for ONE Windsor Newton sable brush!!  I'm now looking forward to many hours of painting on various locations around the beautiful State of Oregon!

rating 4.0. Just regular floss, not the super shiny kind The kit was truly wonderful and there were literally HUNDREDS of skeins of floss, some great little "bobbins" that were really easy to wind as well as threaders and needles, so truly an all in one kit.  The only thing that I was only slightly disappointed in was that the floss looked like the super satiny, shiny thread in the photo image, but turned out to be just regular embroidery floss, but I don't think it was misrepresented, just a not very clear photo.  I am attempting to do a Brazilian Embroidery project that would look better if the floss were shinier, but I'm going to use it anyway and see how it comes out.

input:
rating 5.0. It surpasses all my expectations! I absolutely LOVE using this resin!
I recently compared it to two other brands, one was a very well known one.
This resin outshined them both in smell, ease of use, bubbles, working time, and mixing!
I use resin for a coat on paintings, and jewelry, and other projects.

rating 1.0. Awful Awful, I’ve used a lot of different brands and this one is so far the worst, it’s super watery and drips off the cup while on the turner and no matter what I do all there is after it’s dry are gigantic fisheyes

rating 5.0. Very happy with this resin. Set VERY quickly. Not much work time but great if you’re trying get a pyramid done in one day. Very few bubbles of stirred gently, and come out with heat application.

rating 5.0. Better than the cheaper stuff After buying another product that was cheaper. I learned my lesson. This resin is more clear and cures harder. I will be buying more as soon as I can afford it!

rating 5.0. Beautiful, clear resin I love this product. It mixes together so easily and they are not over stating when they say it is crystal clear. Very few bubbles and almost all dissipate on their own. I hit it with the torch a few times just in case but I don't really think it needed it. You have a long work time with this. I didn't have an issue with it starting to seize up too quickly like I have with other brands. Love it!

rating 1.0. Not self leveling; does not harden This is the third resin I’ve tried and easily the worst. Less expensive than Art Resin and I know why. Unlike Art Resin (which costs far more) this does NOT self level. I tried pouring it on ceramic tiles ON A FLAT SURFACE and to my astonishment there were areas where the mixture was lumpy and in some cases did not touch. I had to keep adding more of the (carefully mixed) resin. I used a metal cocktail stirrer to spread. I followed all directions, mixing 1:1 and after over 24 hours it’s a disgusting, lumpy mess. I’ve tried this brand twice with similar results. Going back to the Art Resin now.

rating 5.0. Great Resin I use this to encase rocks or shells in a tray for decorative use. It is great. Beautiful finish, easy to work with few or no bubbles (some heat will almost guarantee no bubbles). Very usable working time and is ready to display in less than 24 hours. It's also durable...I've used my projects as stands for condiments, plants, etc.
I've used other resins, and this is by far the best...no comparison.
One caution...make sure it is very well mixed! Don't rush it.

rating 5.0. Works for me! This is the only resin epoxy I have ever used besides some UV resin I bought off of Wish.
After about half an hour, it begins to stiffen, so it’s wise to have your design layed out ahead of time. I’ve always heard resin epoxy has noxious fumes but this has none.
rating 2.0. Disappointed Unfortunately the resin was dull and had no shine..silicone mold gave it the finished product a matte look. Disappointed
rating 5.0. Love it! By far my favorite resin I've tried, very clear, little to no bubbles, mixes easily, and very minimal odor.
rating 1.0. Can’t use Allergic reaction, severe rash on arms and neck. Even with gloves and mask
rating 1.0. Waste of Money Material did not harden even after following directions to the letter. A waste of money
rating 1.0. Would not recommend Resin did not absorb in the canvas on two of my glass design projects.  Weeped all over the plastic liner and my table is level.
rating 5.0. I had to add a bit more hardener I purchased this product to make jewelry and coasters ashtrays etc I absolutely love this epoxy high quality very crystal clear well use again.

rating 5.0. Crystal clear easy to work with slow dri It's really perfect for what it is. Just make sure you give it adequate time to cure. Directions to 24 hours, it's really closer to 48 before it's completely cured.
rating 5.0. Easy and great value! Easy to use and CLEAR

rating 5.0. Best of the Best I've used about 8 different brands and this is by far the BEST! It's perfectly clear, easy to use due to it being a thinner than others and hardens nicely! I will probably use another brand again! Just wish it was offered in a value size!!!!!

rating 5.0. Be careful to measure exact Hard finding a good measuring cup, but nonetheless this is great to use. Very minimal bubbles- only experienced one bubble through the whole use. Waited to review to test how it holds up and so far so good.
rating 5.0. Easy! This is so easy to use with easy clean up
rating 5.0. Good resin Very good resin. Very few micro bubbles. Always dried clear and smooth. I would get this product again
rating 5.0. Quality Product is easy to work with and produces amazing products

rating 5.0. Better than most I was able to use a torch to eliminate bubbles and the instructions say it sets after 30 mins. So I kept watching for bubbles for 30 minutes but then when I’d come back the next morning, there’s be bubbles.
rating 2.0. Look else where I use this for tumblers I bought it based on the reviews, it doesn't set correctly separates from the cup and leaves lots of wholes

rating 5.0. AMAZING!!! i loved it!! this is my first time working with resin and the outcome was amazing. Mine did not have a strong odor it was very faint, i do recommend using gloves and a safety mat as it advises because it can get very sticky! Cure time was quick it began hardening only after 2 hours then completely ready within 12 hours. i will definitely order again!
rating 5.0. Amazing product! Love it this resin! It works great and is crystal clear and hard when cured!

rating 1.0. Bummed - resin bottle clearly used before Im so bummed. I literally just opened my package (which was fine) as well as the Unicone Box and the bottles (no leaks or punctures). I opened the resin bottle and the seal on the resin was already cut.  It seems as though someone used the resin because the amount of resin in the bottle vs. the hardener amount there is a clear difference. Unfortunately this means at a 1:1 ratio I would run out of resin before hardener. So bummed as this is my first experience with this brand.

rating 5.0. I love this resin! I like this resin a lot.  I am casting some things and glazing others, and it works quite well for both uses.  I appreciate the way it mixes easily, it has less viscosity than art resin, and actually smells better (or I should say - this resin hasn't got an overpowering odor for that matter).  I have tried a lot of different types of resin and this is my current favorite.  Worth a try - but I would start small, as always, until you are sure.

rating 5.0. Super easy to use, very low odor I have tried every resin out there and this is by far my absolute favorite. It’s super easy to work with and doesn’t yellow. Hardens up very hard with no tack. I think my favorite thing though is that it is super low odor. I will continue to use this product over the others.

rating 3.0. The resin is good but the bottle broke The resin itself is really nice, very clear and barely any bubbles

However, I did have issues with the bottles themselves. The first time I ordered the resin had spilled all over the packaging. I was sent a replacement and it was fine. after the second use the neck of the hardener bottle broke off completely and I had to transfer it into a new bottle.

All in all, the resin itself is really nice. Maybe consider working on better bottles/ packaging
rating 4.0. Awesome! Awesome product. Clear and less bubbles!

rating 1.0. DONT. BUY. [[VIDEOID:8edd98fcf50a1b013079800f4a4a3ff1]] I’ve never seen any like this. I was mixing the 1 to 1 ratio and It started getting hotter and hotter and actually steaming! Then started turning to jello as you can see from the video. I don’t know what kind of chemical reaction that is but for $40 for this small amount... well that’s a bunch of crap. Totally unusable. I’d like a refund.
rating 5.0. Amazing product. This is by far the best resin fries fast and so clear.

rating 4.0. Good product great price I like this resin. It makes beautiful resin charms and is a great quality for the price.
My only complaint is when I add pigment to this resin it’s a slightly different shade than if I mix the same pigment with other resin, it’s not too noticeable and I do like it as it gives a slight two tone affect if I combine the pieces together.
rating 5.0. Best resin Best resin I found so far. It’s clear and bubbles are very minimum as long as you mix slowly. I wish they sold in a bigger size!

rating 5.0. NO air bubbles!! I'm still fairly new to the epoxy tumblers world and when I was ready to jump in, I had absolutely no idea which brand of resin epoxy to use. I do 95% of my shopping online so I went to amazon.com and started researching every brand of epoxy I could find. I stumbled onto this product and thought the little unicorns on the label were just adorable so I figured "why not". It arrived VERY quickly and in perfect condition. I very carefully measured the 2 parts, mixed it well for 3 minutes then applied it to my very first tumbler. That's all I did! No horrible fumes, no torching away air bubbles, nothing! Just measure, mix, and pour for a gorgeous glass-like finish! I've completed 6 tumblers so far and there has not been a single air bubble on any of them! This epoxy is AMAZING!!
rating 1.0. Questionable The label is missing of what I can assume is a bottle of part?? And it is looking a bit yellow, too.  Please help.
rating 5.0. just what i wanted. have only just made one small item at the moment and that came out awesome.

rating 5.0. Very good resin Very good resin just as described!!

It does however, work really well in a mold. Super clear and cures nicely. But don't use it for tumbler cups!!!!!! You will be very disappointed.

input: rating 5.0. Handy and Easy To Use I needed a Quilter's Cut'n Press as I had used my old one so much the fabric wore out from ironing so much.  It did't upset me that my old one wore out I had only had it for 10+ years.  If you are in a small sewing space these are wonderful.
rating 5.0. Great convenience! I take a quilting project with me when we travel.  This cutting/pressing mat is great for traveling, since it fits into my machine case perfectly!!

rating 4.0. good product This board is what any experienced sewer would expect. You can iron and pin on one side and use your rotary cutter on the other. Good for easy portability to a class or to use in a small Sewing area.

rating 5.0. Perfect I ordered this to use when we have the rv out and it works perfectly to iron my quilt blocks and fabric and I can flip it over and rotary cut on the other side. And it doesn't take. Up much room
rating 5.0. Very pleased. I love the size for when I'm working on small patchwork. Sits right beside my sewing machine. I may end up purchasing the rectangular one for larger projects.
rating 5.0. Very useful Works wonderfully for taking small projects away from home. It also allows me to do hand projects outside of my sewing room

rating 5.0. Such a handy thing to have !!! I make a lot of quilts, and often need to trim little pieces or use my mini iron to press out seams.  I keep this on a small table right by my sewing machine.  It is just the right size for it !!!

rating 4.0. Good Tool this is a good tool when traveling to my quilting bee group. table space is limited and cut and press gives me enough space to work comfortably, and fits in my weekender on wheels.also works well in my lap.
rating 5.0. Quilting project gift It is just what I wanted and needed for my quilting projects and the ease of it being portable for me.
rating 4.0. June Tailor's Quilter's Cut'n Press I have already used one of these.  The big problem is that if it gets wet, the board underneath the padding can stain the top.
rating 4.0. Practical Convenient to use when traveling.  Easy to pack, and will recommend to all my friends and family.  Good to have on hand.

rating 5.0. Easy, compact cutting and pressing surface. Perfect for pressing quilt seams as you go! The handle is a little sharp so I covered it with an empty toilet tissue roll with foam inside! Not really a problem since I'm not carrying it around, but just a 'heads up' for those who might be!!
It's also nice that both sides have clear measurements so you can see if your block is square! I recommend this product from this company.
rating 5.0. June Taylor quilter's cut n press Fantastic, easy to use, compact, and a welcome addition to my craft studio,as I can keep it next to my machine
rating 5.0. Great product Works for small projects, taking to a quilt retreat and eliminates having to bring the large and bulky stuff.  Easy to goat in your machine carrier.

rating 5.0. Love this tool for quilting I have a limited amount of space for my quilting, so I have to constantly go back and forth between setting up my cutting board, then taking it down to set up the sewing machine, then getting up to go to the ironing board.  I can set up the Cut'n Press next to my machine and have it right at hand when I need to quickly press a seam, or by flipping it over quickly make a cut.  I also purchased a travel steam iron to use with the Cut'n Press and it's a great set up.  I still use my full-size iron and ironing board when I have a big area to press, but this is a great space-saver and saves a lot of getting up and down.

rating 5.0. Awesome, versatile board- Cut and Press- just don't mix up the sides :) What's better than being able to cut and press with the same board? I have limited space for crafting, and this is a perfect tool for my card crafting and quilting projects.

rating 5.0. So Handy This is such a helpful device for quilters. One side is for cutting and one for ironing as it says. I put this on my lowered ironing board and can cut my blocks then iron them without leaving the sewing machine. It's light weight and seems very well made. I'm very pleased with this dual use item.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Handy Board I use this all the time between my cutting board and sewing machine. It's perfect for pressing as-you-go.

input: rating 5.0. Leverback earring wires I exchange the earring wires from previously purchased earrings that may cause an allergic reaction. Lets me enjoy a wider range of earrings.
rating 5.0. easy to use I have made alot of earrings with theses and love the ease of hooking them when you wear them love silver

rating 3.0. Good price and better than hooks, but has issues with the plating I ordered a pack of these to support dangle earrings to sell at a craft show. Most of them were completely plated, but a number of them had spots on the part that goes through the ear where the plating had been scratched/disrupted in some way. The opening/closing was also less than a smooth motion, but not really an issue for the price.

It is, however, difficult to find lever backs without some form of ornamentation, and the price is certainly good. Just might cause issues for someone with an allergy. Ended up using a higher quality version from an bulk retailer, but these should certainly be serviceable for anyone looking to replace the base of personal earrings for one reason or another. :)

rating 3.0. Not too good quality product but for the price it's fair For the price the seller offers for the product, as leverbacks are usually a lot more expensive than regular fish hook earrings. I appreciate the price, however, the silver coating is not shiny as I'd like for my jewelry to be, and even after wiping to clean the surface, it's not quite shiny, and the silver coating is not all the same quality. Even though the price is good, I will spend more for other leverbacks with even silver coating quality for the future purchase.

rating 5.0. Great looking leverback I found the leverback earring very good looking, works very well, strong and easy to use.  I will order many more.  Bright and shiny I believe you can use as an enhancer.

rating 4.0. Good product for the price Very cheaply made, but you get what you pay for and you can't beat this price. Some of them have issues with not being able to move the hinge smoothly, but they still work. I have made dozens of earrings with these and so far I have had just ONE break, so they are holding up fine.

rating 3.0. Okay, but don't last forever Out of the package, these are nice, shiny leverbacks. And for the price I can't really complain. However, a large number of them are very stiff when you go to open and close them--some are so stuck that I had to pitch them. Also, the hinge mechanism seems rather cheap in its alignment. When you go to reclose the back, it will often not meet up smoothly with the rest of the earring. I also find that these are noticeably tarnished after a couple weeks. Jewelry cleaner *might* take it off, but that's a bit of a hassle to do so frequently for already sub-par leverbacks. That being said, I don't want to be too hard on it; these are probably no better or worse than other affordable, silver plated leverbacks.

rating 1.0. JUNK! DO NOT BUY!!! Ok...so I never give bad reviews, however these are total CRAP!. First of all if I would have realized they shipped from China I would not have ordered them because they took forever to get. Second, they DON'T OPEN WITHOUT USING NEEDLE NOSE PLIERS!!!. I guess the saying you get what you pay for is in order here.

rating 5.0. Great quality [[VIDEOID:726681b7502c95eef11f11eecaf41d5e]] I made 5 letters out of two table spoons of the resin and mixed it with ultra sparkle glitter and a little bit of foil.  All the letters came out great and I have more than half the bottle left over.  I had no bubbles and it dried quickly and smoothly.  This was my first time using it so this kit is definitely beginner friendly and I will be buying more for other projects.  I used a blow dryer in between layers when pouring and for a few seconds when mixing and all the bubbles immediately disappeared.  I am a highly satisfied customer.

rating 5.0. Easy resin to use for beginners Very easy to use. Make sure you mix A &B thoroughly for 3 minutes in a well-ventilated area. You can tell this resin works fast but not too fast for the tiny molds I used. I forgot to wear gloves but used tons of rubbing alcohol, hand sanitizer and dish washing soap to remove the sticky resin. I only did 3 layers in my mini molds and it was fully cured and super shiny in 24 hours. Excellent price and value with all the accessories.

rating 4.0. Good resin, easy to mix, not great if you need more working time This is my first experience with resin, I like this resin but I do have a couple of reasons why I want to try other brands and come back to this when I have more experience working with resin. If your a beginner like me and need more working time this may not be the best resin for you. When it says fast curing it really means it.

Let me start with the Pros:
*It comes with very detailed instructions that are easy to understand
*It's really easy to mix 1:1 by volume
*It's clear and very glossy when dry
*It dries super fast and can be unmolded in 10 hours, but mine set up faster and I was able to demold at 8 hours.
*I had no problems with it being gummy

Here's my Cons:
*It thickens ultra fast, the alcohol ink I used didn't drop down very well because it was too thick
*It has a lot of bubbles despite the several different things I tried to prevent them

So overall a very good clear easy to mix resin but thickens so fast I couldn't get the desired results I was looking for. If you are more familiar with resin and work very quickly this resin is probably a great fit for you, it just doesn't fit my needs at this time. Once I'm more familiar with resin and casting it, I may come back and give this resin another go.

input: rating 4.0. Sharp, tight scissors. Every time I turn around, my son is "borrowing" my 40 year old Fiskars shears (which were still nice and sharp) for whatever terribly important things college guys do with scissors.  I decided that the thing to do was leave my much loved shears for my son to use, and purchased 2 pairs of these based upon the fact that they were Singer brand and they were pink.  I put one pair in each of the two drawers in my sewing table.  I've used them to cut small amounts of fabric, for example, cutting off the excess when hemming up a dress, and a great deal for trimming threads.  Both pairs of scissors were sharp (I did note that some reviewers said otherwise), and while they won't replace my larger shears for cutting out patterns, they are very good for light duty work.

Glad I bought a backup from a different brand to continue making mats for the animals cages at the Humane Society shelter-

It has a very sturdy design and didn't feel like it was about to fall apart. It was comfortable in my hands too. It also felt sharp in my hands. So I was excited to use it. However, it could barely cut fabric! It took so much energy just to cut a single layer of fabric. I pulled out my cheapo scissors, because I thought it was the fabric, and they breezed through! I tried cutting thread with it too, and it's not great with that either. I wonder if it even cuts paper....

I definitely don't recommend these scissors. I dunno if I got a dud, seeing as though it has so many great reviews, but I'd suggest getting a different pair.

rating 5.0. Wonderful sewing aid I have arthritis in my hands and had pretty much given up on sewing. I love to sew and have found a number of wonderful, helpful sewing aids that have given me the courage to get back into sewing. They allow me to stretch my limits and look forward to expressing my innate creativity with fabric once again. Joan Hawley's Press Perfect Hot Hemmer has given me yet another key to unlock more doors. Many thanks to Joan and Amazon Fulfillment Services for this wonderfully handy sewing aid!
rating 3.0. Great for small sewing tasks! The Hot Hemmer is especially handy for small craft items that need small corners! In particular I found it great in making bags.

rating 5.0. Grips fabric while ironing! For years, the only commercial solution has been the metal Dritz hemmer of similar design. Not only did it get HOT while ironing up the hem . . .but was slippery.

Not so with this handy gadget. It grips the fabric while pressing so fabric remains in place until it's set with the iron, freeing up your hand to clear the way for a hot iron. No more accidental burns!

Wish this had been available years ago . . .one of Clover's best inventions!

input: rating 5.0. What a pleasure! Absolutely gorgeous colors. Some granulate more than others. When mixed with other colors on my palette, they add beautiful interest; can be quite unexpected in a Christmas kind of way!  I will be exploring other Daniel Smith Primatek colors!

rating 5.0. absolutely beautiful and these colors are so dynamic I started with the d.smith primary set and realized I had to have the primatek set, absolutely beautiful and these colors are so dynamic. I'm normally drawn to much brighter tones but these colors are, I don't want to say muted, more like earth tones I guess being they are made from mineral stones but still very vibrant in color. I'm absolutely in love with the Amethyst, it actually glistens and shines with minuscule flecks of sparkle. The Jadeite is the best green watercolor I've ever owned. Normally I wouldn't want a blackish/paynes grey type of color in a set but the Hematite, the way it granulates and lays on the paper is gorgeous, and mixes awesome with other colors. The only color I was no so fond of was the Mayan Blue, at first it felt bit streaky right out of the tube but that can be worked with, I guess it just doesn't flow as well as the others but still a pretty color non the less. I'll be saving up for more D. Smith watercolor! Highly recommend this set, I feel like it will add diversity to my paintings. Also, each paint tube is truly filled unlike other brands I've experienced.

rating 5.0. First DS experience..... thumbs up!. This is my first experience with Daniel Smith paints. The Primatek line is very unique. Just for reference, I squeeze my paint into pans that I let dry. The mayan blue and hematite are a little harder to re-wet, but honestly the extra effort is so worth it. The colors all have different colored undertones (with the exception of rhodonite) and those undertones change intensity depending on how thick or thin your wash is. I also understand that some artists do not like granulation, but if you do then these are some of the best effects with any paint that I have seen. At least 3 of these will have a permanent place on my landscape pallet from now on. These may not be for everyone but DS gets a big thumbs up from me and I plan on purchasing more in the future.

rating 5.0. This was a happy draw for me Daniel Smith has so many beauriful colors it was hard for me to decide which to get, even with the dot chart. This was a happy draw for me. This gave me enough colors to work with. This really is a nice starter set.

rating 5.0. Beautiful organic graining I'm sorry for the 1 star reviewer for his experience - I can only guess that he accidentally got someone's returned items or not fulfilled through Amazon? For my purposes (costume design sketches) I will most likely stay away from the DS Primateks - though not because these are bad. This Primateks set were supposedly chosen because these give a wide range of mixability and are all relatively semi-transparent. These are great supplemental colors to add to a simple primary palette - Jadeite is like a Viridian (your cool green), Rhodonite your magenta/perm rose, Piemontite would be your brown madder, and Mayan blue is somewhere between Indigo and Prussian Blue. The Amethyst Genuine is a great violet and actually shimmers in the light because if the tiny crushed crystal in it, so just keep that in mind if you're thinking of using it as shadow for faces. Also know, the Hematite is much closer to neutral tint (in the packaging it looks much warmer but it is really gray). The color is incredibly lush and the granulating effects are very cool because of their particle makeup so I will keep these and even explore other Primatek colors for my personal art, or if I want to paint natural garment materials like leathers or raw natural woven fabrics - but just know that these are made to be granulating, so if you want smooth solid color you can control try the rest of the Daniel Smith line.
rating 5.0. Beautiful high quality watercolors! Love these very beautiful paints! They mix so well and are so easy to use!
rating 5.0. Great colors! I love these colors, they're natural and not fakey looking.  They granulate and have plenty of pigment.  Very happy painter.

rating 3.0. Stinky packaging. Packaging of the watercolors reeked of an unknown smell like a perfume.  Before even picking it up you got a very intense smell that pervaded everything.  Made the other items smell like it for a few hours afterwards.  Removed the directions and packaging, and 0laced inside plaatic, it was still strong!!!!.  The product itself is usable after airing outbut I no longer am able to keep it in the packaging which making storage an issue for the 6 very small items contained.  The product worked well but this really made me not a fully happy customer.

rating 5.0. Truly Extraordinary Watercolors Some of the very finest watercolor paints I have ever bought have been made by Daniel Smith Co. The color, tone, hue, delicateness are all fators that Daniel Smith paints incorporate in their product.

It seems that finally, people have started to reach for and pay for the highest quality paints. I have gone completely to high end paints. It is not that I am such a great artist, but in te event that something I do create becomes xcellent or highly appreciated, I don't want to take the chance that I could recreate it with goosd paints later.

So, since I am not nearly as good an artist as I wish I was and since. am 60 years old now, I only have so mmuch time in my life to paint my own "Mona Lisa" or "Stary Night.". I suspect that my fine motor skills will not take me there.

Nonetheless, you never know what your ability is until you give it a try. Never assum that you cannot do it, If you read some of th books I hav recommended by Billy Showell you will see a true master. Ms. Showell is truly an extraordinary painter and she has a number of excellent books, which are still in print, that I would recommend to any person interested in painting living things, but especially flowers, trrees, gladees, natue. Her work is truly an inspiration to all of us amateur artists.

After all, one never knows how good oe can be unless they give it a try. I hav e met people who were in the process of throwing thing in the garbage that I would have paid hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of dollars if I was buying it at an auction.

We all ave the right to dream. And we all have the riight to create. I would encourage you to try and do so. Maybe you will find you have a hidden talent that you were too timid to try out. Well, you never have to show your work to anyone if you on't want to show it. But my guess is, you will want to show it, especialy as you get better and better. Jump in with both fet and see how good you really are. Best of luck and remember to enjoy yourself.

rating 3.0. Haven't used these yet but be aware they are very ... Haven't used these yet but be aware they are very, very tiny tubes. Smaller than a travel toothpaste. My own fault, the picture fooled me into thinking they were larger.

rating 1.0. Missing color, no option to get the correct one sent. Missing a color, double of another color. Very disappointed. Was excited to use these today. Too expensive not to return and get all six listed colors somewhere else.
rating 3.0. Three Stars colors were not as bright as I expected.

rating 5.0. Loving them and having fun! I just received this paint set today. I am really having fun with them. The colors are romantic, strong but gentle at the same time. They granulate wonderfully with some interesting color play. For example the jadeite has a bit of black so the granulation varies from green to dark green or black. The amethyst sparkles, the ground crystal looks like extra fine glitter. Not overpowering but subtle. All the colors are wonderful. They dilute and mix easily. I played with them and painted the attached picture real quick. Not a masterpiece, just having fun. I just love the almost renisaunce romance of the colors. I will add to this set with some other colors in this line.

rating 3.0. nice colors, but i won't get much use out ... so far, I have only used the jadeite and mayan blue from this set. nice colors, but i won't get much use out of the sparkly amethyst, for example. I wish I had instead spent $5 first on the test paint dots and tried all the primatek to determine which ones I like, then just buying individual bottles before splurging on these.

rating 5.0. Wonderful paints! I absolutely love these colors! They flow beautifully due to their granulating properties. This means that you'll have specks of pigment in your finished product, but the results are gorgeous.

The amethyst is my favorite because it sparkles as if it has glitter! And the green is my pick for florals. Granulation adds such a beautiful texture to the piece that other brands look flat by comparison.

rating 5.0. Rock your Watercolors. These colors are made of real rocks. The Amethyst actually sparkles. Mayan Blue is like looking into the sky in Costa Rica (well, I've never actually been there, but hey, I have Mayan Blue.)  The Rhodocrosite is a nice, permanent pink, shading to burgundy. The Hematite is basically black,though the label is brown and I don't use it much, but it does granulate nicely. Piedmontite is a very grainy brown--like our local red mud. Jadeite is a very dark green, sort of like a bluer shade of Hooker's Green. If you like granulating pigments on rough paper, you need to try these.
rating 4.0. I'm glad I bought this set The colors are not as intense as I thought they would be but I'm still experimenting with them.  I'm glad I bought this set.
