rating 5.0. Granulating, High Quality Paints! I am in love with these watercolors. The granulation is so fun to play with, and you really get a good deal price-wise. Unique and quality.

rating 5.0. Love these I love semiprecious stones and watercolor. It was inevitable that I'd buy these. I'm still experimenting, but they're very fun to use, especially if you like granulating colors.

rating 4.0. Not my favorite.... These watercolors are not my preference. I don’t like the shimmer in them. It’s my fault I’m sure, but I was not expecting these to have shimmer in them. These are the first watercolors I’ve ever used that had shimmer in them. I’m sure some people would love them. The colors aside from the shimmer are pretty and easy to paint with. If you’re looking for sparkle/shimmer or just something different this is definitely a good price for what you’re getting.

rating 1.0. Missing Rhodonite, received a non-primatek paint in set Instead of Rhodonite, the set contained Quinacridone Rose, which is not a Primatek paint and not part of the advertised set.  I have contacted Daniel Smith three times in the past week regarding this issue, as it is a DS quality issue and not an Amazon issue since the set was new and unopened.  DS has not bothered to respond.

rating 4.0. Overall happy with this set, aside from the misleading hematite These are some fun watercolours if you want something different and love granulation. Most of the paints are usable, but the purple amethyst is very glittery and something I would only use for special occasions. The blue and green colours are my favourites in this set!

The one star removed is because from the packaging the hematite looks like a rich, dark, earthy brown, but in my tube it is black! From my research this rock comes in both brown and black, so it's possibly the black variety ended up here. Still, I can't complain too much about it because it is great for painting rocks and in general adding a grey, grainy layer to whatever you are painting. It has been about a year since I received my paint to my writing of this review, so it's possible the black paint has been remedied. Still, a good thing to be aware of just in case.

rating 5.0. Stunning and unique, top notch paints! Daniel Smith is hands down my favorite watercolor paint. The Primateks are fabulous -- I love the subtle shimmer in the Amethyst Genuine, the mixing abilities of Piemontite, the gorgeous granulation of Jadeite Genuine... Hematite is also one of my go-to paints to add shadows with granulation. The Mayan blue encouraged me to try other Mayan paints from the brand, and I haven't been disappointed. I also use Rhodonite to mix skin tones and it's nice to have a pink color in my mix of paints. Highly recommended.
rating 2.0. Dried out One of the paints inside was trash, so was the watercolor ground, completely dried out. Tried contacting company with no reply.

rating 5.0. Excellent set Wow! Just wow!
It’s my first DS, but I’ve been painting for a few years and Jadeite had finally hit home as my perfect green, Mayan Blue is excellent substitute to Indigo, Amethyst is obviously gorgeous, but so are others in this collection- just perfect mono pigmented paints that are excellent for those who paint nature.
If you love granulation - they are must have

rating 4.0. Different from Holbein... From this set, I really like the Mayan Blue, Jadeite and Amethyst, although Amethyst is very glittery but beautiful. Piemontite, Hematite and Jadeite are more granular than others.

The most misleading one is Hematite, it is really greyish black even though the label showed a rich brown texture. It is also very light, almost feel like lack of pigments, and very gel like when fresh squeezed out off the tube.

These feel very different from the Holbein set I have, but it is good to have some colors from a different manufacturer.

rating 5.0. great deal Great value. Would not recommend for beginners as these paints granulate and have different effects. But the colors are amazing and the paints are lovely. Would suggest to use from tube as some of the colors are difficult to rewet.
rating 5.0. Worth the money But this set, but only if you like granulation. Which there is a lot of. The colors are great. Daniel Smith is by far the best and American made.

rating 5.0. Beautiful and unique paint set! [[VIDEOID:36503d8a7b148bb5fc8307a3ba64c28e]] Absolutely gorgeous fine watercolors with unique granulation! I love these watercolors for the beautiful effects they cause in my paintings. I am attaching a swatch photo as well as some recent art I made using them. The price here on amazon is excellent for the quality and quantity of these Daniel Smith watercolors. I’d buy again in a heartbeat and probably will have to with as much as I’ve been using them. Highly recommend.

rating 2.0. Tiny tubes not accurate colors The colors are very dark even with alot of water; not primary colors like I had hoped for. The tubes are ultra tiny. These watercolors are way too expensive for such a small amount. Do not recommend buying these.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Unique and Beautiful Paints I’ve been working with watercolor for a year now. I’ve used several different student grade brands of watercolor, most of which are fairly  good in terms of color saturation and performance. Having tried a honey based pro brand and now Daniel Smith, I prefer the latter... and yes, pro vs student grade does make a difference with performance.  Generally, the DS colors are far more concentrated than the student grade paints, making a tiny amount go a loooong way. Also, the paper (100% cotton) absolutely loves them! That being said, the primatek colors do behave A bit differently than the regular DS paints. They granulate naturally. It’s as if the mineral base sits on top of the paper allowing for streakiness, though that may be from not using enough water in my wash. The colors are subtle, except for the rhodonite, which is a pretty vibrant pink. For beautifully subtle, granulating color, I would definitely recommend these. They do dry quicker than the regular paints. It’s as if they absorb the water differently. I also purchased the essentials set. I swatched and mixed the two sets together (photo attached), which resulted in some beautiful colors. The tubes are small, but not as tiny as other reviewers led me to believe. One of the 5ml tubes will fill a full pan with paint left in the tube. The INTERIOR of the  Meeden full pans I am using is 2.8mm L x 1.6 W x .08 H. N. When you break it down by cost, I paid less than $4 per full pan. That’s not bad for professional watercolors. The paints arrived on time and were hand delivered to me at my door. The boxes were taped closed and the tubes were full and unopened. All appropriate colors were in the boxes. I’m quite excited to do more painting with these paints!
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Sparkling Very pretty. Big holes.

rating 5.0. Quality pens. No bleed through! This is my first time using the pigma brush colored pens. I bought them to use in my artwork. I have previously used the black pen, and was happy with its performance.
I have attached a swatch picture. First I tested them on arteza’s gray sketch paper, then on white cotton watercolor paper. The colors are definitely brighter on the white cotton paper, vibrant and even. They aren’t as bright on the gray paper, but still not bad. As usual with pigma pens, there is no bleed through. I would recommend them.

rating 5.0. Even diluted, they’re very sparkly! [[VIDEOID:20fe611141d90f0aee303f3f79fa02ae]] I love this paint! It’s so pretty and super sparkly. The colors are very nice. I use metallics mostly for highlighting. These are so pretty that I’m inspired to try new things with them. The box came factory sealed in perfect condition. It came on time. I must admit I’m a fan of Japanese watercolors. They’re a bit different that other watercolors, and so much fun to work with. I love the big pans, and how quickly the water penetrates and saturates. Same as other brands of Japanese watercolors, a little goes a long way. Hope this is helpful.

rating 5.0. So fun to paint with! Absolutely love these paints. Love the way they behave. Love the way they look once dry. I was already a fan of the Kuretake set, and these perform very similar. Easy to move the pigment around. Lift easily (good and bad). The colors are great. Unlike the Kuretake, even the thicker areas dry to a mat finish. The elephants in my picture were done with the “purplish black” E07 color. A little bit goes a long way! Very saturated. They activate easily and instantly. I would definitely recommend.

rating 5.0. Interesting and Fun Medium I’ve been looking forward to trying water soluble graphite for a while. I am happy to say that I’m not disappointed. Derwent regular graphite pencils have been my favorite for drawing. This set, with its unique properties, may surpass them.
The pencils came pre-sharpened, 2 of each shade. I really like that the cores are so big. They go on the paper nice and smooth. They blend out well with water. Once dry, adding watercolor is no problem. I will say that when planning to use wet, remember that a little goes a long way. It doesn’t take much to make shading dark. The picture shows the graphite with watercolor. It’s a good combo. My only complaint is that the tin came with a small dent in the lid. It doesn’t affect their usefulness, soo still gave 5 stars.

rating 5.0. Impressed! Came on time. New, sharpened but not used, sealed, no broken points. Perfect condition (including the tin). The dry colors are okay, but where these really impress is when wet. Wow! What a difference. My advice would be to use sparingly. Very light pressure, unless you want lines or the pencil texture to show through. I uploaded a swatch picture. I used 100% cotton 140# cold pressed Bee paper, so it is possible the texture affected the results. The picture really doesn’t do justice to the vibrancy of the color. Just remember, once you wet them there is no “lifting” or erasing. What you get is what you’ve got. These are a great new art medium for me. I do NOT suggest these for adult coloring. As I said, where they really impress is wet, and the paper in most adult coloring books is not designed for watercolor. I bought the set of 12, which should be perfect for my needs. Amazing price. Michael’s charges almost 4times this price. Yikes! I would definitely recommend.

rating 5.0. Quality Case, Awesome Deal I really don’t have anything bad to say about this kit. It came exactly as advertised. Actually there was a bonus little single hole sharpener that I wasn’t expecting. The sharpeners all worked well. The case is well made, sturdy. I have every slot filled (see picture) and it still closes with no problems.
I swatched the pencils and compared them with my Derwent set. In the darker shades they are comparable in how they lay down on paper. The values are slightly darker than the Derwent across the board. I swapped out most of the pencils for my Derwent set, but will use them eventually. I keep my charcoal in a different case, but am happy to be able to add to that collection with items from this kit. I haven’t tried the charcoal yet, so I can’t comment on it’s quality. The white eraser worked very well, which I didn’t expect. The stump holder actually works. Each end will accommodate a different sized pencil.
Approximating cost for each item, if purchased individually, you will SAVE about $30 by purchasing this kit. It really is an awesome deal. I would definitely recommend it.

rating 5.0. Great brushes, incredible value! What a difference quality brushes make! I bought this set after reading so many positive reviews. I needed good round brushes for watercolor painting, but can’t afford to spend double the amount of this purchase (or more) for ONE decent brush. These brushes out perform any/all other brushes I own, including my one Princeton Elite ($20) and Princeton Select brushes. These brushes hold a lot of water/pigment. Each one comes to a beautiful point. They are soft with just enough spring-back. I received them yesterday, so I painted with them last night and this morning.
The packaging did give opportunity for damage, as the package was simply put in a padded envelope, but mine arrived undamaged. Each brush head was covered with a plastic protector. Some of the brushes were so stiff I wasn’t sure I’d be able to get them cleaned enough to use. I managed. It just took patience and perseverance, and they didn’t shed a single hair in the process (or when painting). All of that stiffening was good protection! The sizes are different than the other brushes I have, but I’ve decided that’s just the way it goes. Each brand is different. My Princeton select and elite brushes are sized differently as well.
I am very happy with my purchase. I would definitely recommend, and will purchase again when the need arises.

rating 5.0. Enjoyable painting experience I love these paints! The colors are vibrant and deep or soft pastel, depending on the amount of water you use. I’ve added a swatch photo. I tested transparency with a thick black line. For the most part the colors are transparent. The single swipe is straight from the pan to dry paper. The wider gradient was wet on wet. The benefit is you can control the transparency with the amount of water.  They are creamy, and easily wet. They bloom beautifully! The yellow ochre goes on a little grainy, but dries beautifully, as you can see from my unfinished painting. It is on Fluid-100, 300# cold press cotton rag paper. The swatches were done on 100% cotton 140# Bee paper.  The only problem (or benefit if you look at it differently) is that they lift VERY easily once dried. However, it is possible to get beautiful layers. With a little care lifting is not a problem. The issue of the shininess goes away if you layer instead of laying the paint on thick the first time. I would definitely buy again, but these huge pans look about half filled, so it may be a while before I will need to replace them. Hope this review is helpful. Have fun!

rating 5.0. Perfect This graphite paper was exactly what I needed for transferring my sketches onto watercolor paper for painting. It goes on pretty light, unless you press rather hard. It erases well with a kneaded eraser, once applied to paper. I would definitely recommend for use on paper.

rating 5.0. I’ve said it before, great, affordable alternative I love this paper. It is an affordable alternative to the more expensive 300 lb 100% cotton papers out there. I will say that it is a bit more delicate than other papers I have used. If you apply tape, make sure to use a hair dryer or something to loosen up the adhesive before trying to remove it. It is VERY thirsty paper. The ad states that it has sizing inside and out, but I don’t think as much surface sizing as some others. All things considered, I still love this paper and will continue to use it for my watercolor painting.

rating 5.0. An affordable quality paper I love this paper. It is an affordable alternative to the more expensive 300 lb 100% cotton papers out there. I will say that it is a bit more delicate than other papers I have used. If you apply tape, make sure to use a hair dryer or something to loosen up the adhesive before trying to remove it. It is VERY thirsty paper. The ad states that it has sizing inside and out, but I don’t think as much surface sizing as some others. All things considered, I still love this paper, and will continue to use it for my watercolor painting.

rating 5.0. Good Value I really like this little box!  I was just looking for a mixing pallet when I found this. I love not having to pull out a tube each time I want a new color. The box was in pristine condition when it arrived. It had 3 layers of padding for protection, as can be seen in the photo. The half pans click right in and don’t slide around. There is a little space between each one. I like that they’re not squeezed right up against each other. There is plenty of space between the rows for a brush, or a water brush if it’s not too fat. It’s not heavy, even filled with paint. I believe it will be quite convenient for travel. The only downside is that the white surface paint on the interior flap chipped where it contacts the edge when I closed it. That means eventual rust. The lid opens and closes easily. It seems to be pretty sturdy. Over all, it’s a really nice little pallet box. I would definitely recommend.

rating 5.0. Love this case! This is my second purchase of one of these cases. My first one was purchased for a set of VeriThin pencils. This one is for a set of fine liner pens. It is perfect for either application. I love the way each section opens separately. The cases are well made and sturdy. I would definitely recommend!

rating 5.0. Can't wait to use this stamp and die set. I like to get stamps that have dies with it.  Makes cutting them out so much easier and makes the stamp more versatile. rating 5.0. Stamp & die fit fine. The  strange little shapes in the center are word dies, not part of the flower The stamp & dies fit perfectly. There has been some confusion in reviews,  I think, about the strange “centers” of the flowers. In truth these are not “centers. They are dies to cut backing for the words “thanks” and “thank you”. They have to be cut off the main die, Companies often attach smaller dies to the main one. Once they are removed the flower die fits the stamp perfectly as do the word dies. Very happy with this purchase.

rating 5.0. I love Staroar’s Diamond Painting Kits I loved diamond painting this picture. The drills were well-made and the picture is beautiful. There were just the right amount of sparkly drills to enhance the poppies. I originally bought it for myself and the blue flower Staroar picture for a friend’s Christmas gift. My friend said her favorite color is red. I knew I had to give it to her instead of the blue flower picture.

Staroar is a quality diamond painting company. Their canvases are high quality, soft and with poured glue. Each of Staroar’s kits come with sufficient sheets to cover the painting while placing the drills.

rating 3.0. Slightly Disappointed The box of containers arrived slightly damaged. There was no outer box that enclosed the merchandise and the product’s box was coming open as the postman delivered it to my door. She said that it was lucky that the “stuff” inside didn’t fall out.

rating 4.0. Quality... Excellent! Selection... Meh 🤷🏻‍♀️ I purchased these charms to decorate the fairy doors and accessories that I make. The quality of the charms is EXCELLENT. It was exactly as I expected. They arrived in a see-thru plastic sealed bag that has a ziploc type of feature, which is nice for storage. (see pictures attached.)

The first package I received had a little less than 100 charms. I think I got 96 or 97? Not a big deal, but the selection that I got was just “okay” (in my opinion,) so I returned them with the option to get a new package sent.

Customer service was great. The replacement item was shipped right away, before I could get to UPS to return the first one.

The second package had the correct number, (a few extra, like 103 charms,) but the selection of charms wasn’t as good as the first package. I was hoping to get more of the charms I saw in the pictures (like the bees, butterflies, fairies, etc.) But I was kind of “deflated” by the assortment I received. (There were several duplicates of Christmas & Wedding themed charms that I had no use for.) I considered returning them again for a completely different themed set, but I was “over it” by then and just used the ones I could.

This is the type of purchase where “You get what you get, and you don’t get upset.” So if you’re good with the “Blind Box” or “Grab Bag” types of items, this is GREAT! But if you have your hopes on getting certain charms, I suggest finding charms that are packaged by the specific “THEME” you’re looking for... (like nature, magical, nautical, lunar, etc.)

input: rating 1.0. to tiny and fine ......not helpful with arthritic hands. Very disappointed.  I bought 2 sets at close to $20.  Terrible value for a product that requires good eye and hands to use.  Way prefer the plastic top pins.  If the tops were coated for handling, it would improve use.  So fine they are difficult to hang on too.  Expensive to ship back, so keeping.  May buy product to dip tops in vinyl, then they would be more useable.  As fine as a thin pin, but short like aa paperclip.  If they were longer, it would have helped.  I don't recommend buying sight unseen via internet.  As soon as I tried them, I knew they would not work for me.  Concept good, but product insufficient for use.  Wish I had bought the  Clover Quilting Pins-Fine, 100 Per Pack instead.  Much better value and the big heads make for easier use.

rating 5.0. Fork Pins These pins take a bit of adjustment as they are extremely sharp and a bit more attention is required when placing them in the fabric.  However, the bit of additional time is well worth it.  My quilt piecing quality has really improved as well as my seams.  Fabric does not creep or move when these pins are used.  But, beware of handling the fabric a lot or you are sure to stick yourself.  Still worth the trouble.

rating 5.0. get perfect matched seams on your quilt I am making a nine patch quilt using 1 inch finshed little patches & matching the seams was a nightmare until I ordered these fork pins. Now my seams all match & it is really a blessing to see my quilt turning out so crisp & professional, compared to my first nine patch blocks in the quilt.

Basically what you do is butt up the 2 seams, place a fork pin so that the seam is held together on each side by the 2 prongs of the pin. SLOWLY crawl over these pins with your needle, do each stitch turning your machine wheel until you have safely crawled over the pins. I was always afraid to sew over pins, but if you absolutely go slowly, you will not break a needle & you will end up with a perfectly matched set of seams using the fork pins.

Don't get in a hurry to get over those pins, you will save tons of time in the long run by not having to stop & rip out a seam that you thought should absolutely have matched up but did not. Oh yes, they lay flat & are very easy to insert in fabric.

I found about about these pins by googling on the net for directions on how to get perfect matching seams & several people mentioned these Clover fork pins. I am going to order another box of pins as soons as I get together a 25.00 free shipping order. Wish I had ordered 2 boxes right off the bat.

I use 100 per cent cotton quilting fabric & have noticed these pins leave little holes, but after pressing my finished block, the holes have healed themselves & disappeared. I cannot say if these pins would work for other types of material. The little holes are absolutely no problem & I don't feel they damage the material in any way since they disappear. I have checked back at previous blocks to see if the holes reappeared, but I cannot find a single trace of where the pins were.

If you are a quilter, please treat yourselves to a wonderful tool. These are not some gimmick things that pop up on the quilting market to take your money & you end up tossing the gadget since it sorta kinda aorked but really wasn't worth the hassel of using.

Also, I ordered a large box of binding clips to hold my quilt binding onto a quilt. I have not tried them yet, but I can already see they are going to be a big advantage over the usual pinning method.[[ASIN:B004ZKPX8A Clover Wonder Clips, 50 Per Pack]]. Check out the reviews for this product.

You will take your quilting process to a higher level using the Clover fork pins & Wonder Clips. Trust me & happy quilting.

Other than that, they are a little difficult to seperate from each other in the tiny (albeit neat!) little box they come in. Glad I found a shipper with a great price (half of what most others charge!). Until I learn to use these correctly, not sure I can recommend these over regular straight pins, such as [[ASIN:B007CQR2PG Clover Glass Head Quilt Pins 1 7/8" (48mm) Super Fine 0.5mm Shaft with Amber Head - 100/box]].

April 29th, Update :
... Regarding that how-to-YouTube video .. no luck finding anything. I searched for 'fork pins' and got all kinds of weird stuff, including ONE video which seemed to start about half way with a lady sitting at a table with a quilt and some fork pins and she was talking to somebody other than the camera person, and then it went off. rating 5.0. Susan Khalje is never wrong. These really work. Couture sewing guru, Susan Khalje, demonstrated on a video how these little gems help you sew plaids, stripes and seams so that they match perfectly. And if you sew, you know who who Susan Khalje is--or you should. You know how you have these lined up perfectly, but when you've finished sewing them, they're off just a little because the feed dogs feed the bottom layer of fabric faster than the top one? These little pins have two prongs, which you put on either side of the seam, stripe, etc.and they keep the fabric from shifting. They really work. Strangers on the street will actually stop you to comment how everything on your garment matches perfectly! Or maybe not.They're really a quilting notion, but we garment sewers won't tell. Plus, you know that anything made by Clover can't be all bad.

rating 5.0. Finally my seams line up! I love these pins. At first I had problems figuring out how to use these, but once I figured it out they worked great.

To use these pins for quilting, I pressed my seams open and lined up a seam and held it in place with my fingers. At 1/4 inch from the raw edge where I would sew my seam allowance, I put in the pin so the forks go on either side of the seam with the rounded edge of the pin lining up with the raw edge. I leave the pin in and SLOWLY sew over the pins by turning the handwheel on my machine.

I can't tell you how happy I am with these pins!
rating 5.0. Clover Fork Pins As a quilter, I would NEVER be without these pins.  They ensure fabric stays in place for exact, matching seams.

rating 5.0. How am I just now finding these?? Some quilters don't pin.  I am a pinner.  Especially at the money seams where points need to be sharp, and line continuity must be accurate.  These double fork pins are wonderful for making sure that those critical seams remain locked and loaded.  The pin straddles the line between the two conjoining seams, keeping everything steady.  These pins are also great for keeping the seam allowances all going in the right direction - especially the ones you can't see on the bottom side that seem to get turned around, making it difficult to press your seams in one direction.  I bought one pack of 35, and it's plenty for me to use even on a large quilt.  I love the snap-lidded box to store them in, keeping everything tidy and easy to locate.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. A great invention I saw these in use on a YouTube video and HAD to get them right away to solve the problem of perfectly aligning intersecting seams.  They are so slender that you can safely machine stitch right over them, ensuring that the alignment stays just as you pinned it.  The directions on the package don't mention it, but the YouTube video said to insert the pin from left to right, i.e., toward the edge of the garment, not toward the body of the garment as one does with an ordinary straight pin, with the bent end pointing up.  They are terrifically sharp and hold tightly.  I find that prior to inserting the fork pins, accurate alignment of the intersecting seams or plaids is made certain by vertically inserting a straight pin through both layers' seams and then using the fork pin to capture the alignment securely.  Excellent quality and performance!
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Saves time and pricked fingers! I love how this caddy aligns the pins and holds them just firmly enough.  I don't have to take the time to hold them "just so" to insert them into a pin cushion, or fish around in a tray of sharp loose pins to try and pick up just one to use it.  When removing pins from along a seam while sewing, I can just flip the pin in the vicinity of the caddy and the magnet grabs and aligns the pins safely and ready for the next use.  The groove helps grab a single pin.  Why didn't I have one of these ears ago?!?

rating 5.0. Superb tool for cutting out sewing patterns These scissors are what everyone who sews needs!  Pattern cutting is made so easy and accurate with the lower blade that rides level along the cutting table, barely disturbing the fabric as it cuts.  Requires far fewer pins to secure the pattern.  Razor sharp, making cutting a dream.  The only negative, and it is minor, is that the knuckles of my cutting hand lightly bump the inside of the lower handle loop.  Not really painful, just noticeable toward the end of cutting out a dress.  I highly recommend these scissors.  You will quickly wonder how you did without them!

rating 3.0. Maybe it was a bad batch I have used Siser HTV many many times, and wouldn't consider using any other brand, for ease of use and durability.  But this roll of grey was either not Siser brand, as promised, or it was a defective batch.  It cut the same as other Siser HTV that I have used, but it was VERY difficult to weed.  The carrier sheet simply didn't want to release the cuts.

rating 5.0. Sleek and unbelievably thin I use this Trace Light Pad (in silver) for weeding the vinyl and HTV and glitter output of my Silhouette Cameo.  Makes it all go much more quickly than trying to see the lines by angling another light source "just right".  This product arrived quickly and well packaged.  Right out of the box I was impressed with its fit and finish.  Very sleek and looks as though it should cost much more.  I have used it 4 or 5 times, for a total of about 8 hours, and it performs exactly as described.  I hope it continues to do so for a very long time.

rating 3.0. Learned this trick for a successful punch I bought this punch for using with laminated documents and card stock crafts.  The best result, when punching laminated materials, was to keep the lever down when removing the item that was punched.  This usually left a clean rounded corner, whereas if I let the lever return to its pre-punch position before removing the item, one side almost always was left with a small tag on one side, with the cut-off part still attached.  Other than that, I am pleased with the product, as well as the prompt delivery.

rating 5.0. and the thoughtfulness of the way it is marked is going to be very useful. I recommend it The mat I bought is very well made and looks as though it will last for a very long time. rating 5.0. My go to transfer paper I cut a lot of vinyl decals and the clear transfer paper really helps to line them up on the item you're transferring to. rating 5.0. I can see clearly now This is the second time that I have used this brand of transfer tape. I have to say it's much better quality than the traditional transfer tape that looks like masking tape. The grid lines help me distribute the tape evenly across all of the decals I make which helps reduce wasted product. With it being clear it's also much easier to see any imperfections that there might be in the final product along with making it easier to place the decals on whatever it is that you're applying it to. I've never used traditional transfer tape with my products but because of this tape, I'll for sure not plan on using any other tape any time soon.

rating 5.0. Read this before you leave Let me show you something before you leave here to go buy the Liquitex Basics Titanium White. This was a spur of the moment thing I did on Cardboard, this is the SAME amount of Paint. A is for Amsterdam, L is for Liquitex.

rating 2.0. Fork I don't know what fan brushes these guys are using but THESE brushed are crap. Dip them in water and you get a fork with the bristles sticking together. It's useless.

rating 3.0. Soft Body Craft Paint It's OK to have around as a backup but for main usage on Canvas it's a no-no. The Paint is quite thin and requires two coats sometimes. If you're making silhouette Grass, you can't go over that, it requires one stroke; with this paint you get nothing but transparent Grass. To their advantage, they advertise it as Artist Quality now apparently, but it's just Soft Body Craft paint.

For a few dollars more you can get a Heavy Body paint set from Amsterdam, Grumbacher or Liquitex.

**Update 03/06/18 **

They have changed the product since this review, I'm tempted to re-purchase it and try again just out of curiosity.

rating 5.0. Quality First things first, you guys are breaking your bottles because there is a thin film of plastic you need to remove from the lids that you are not removing. Nothing is wrong with this paint.

This is fantastic Paint! It's thick, heavily pigmented and fully retains peaks! As a beginner this was a great deal, although 2oz seemed quite small; a few big paintings and the black and white are gone. Colors mix and blend very well and drying time is normal, about 5-10 minutes.

I'd like to buy more of this paint individually in 4oz bottles. If you've been using liquitex, give this a shot because this is pro-grade stuff. If Color Technik is reading this, a larger package with common colors like White, Black, Phthalo Blue, Phthalo Green, Cobalt Blue, Ultramarine, Sap Green, Burnt Umber, Burnt Sienna, Cad Red, Yellow Ochre and Dioxazine Purple would be nice with at least 4oz of black and white.

Or let us buy 4oz bottles separately. Beginners need lots of paint. I highly recommend this paint.

rating 4.0. The best runner glue, until..... I was thrilled with the 50' of high quality glue. The first cartridge worked perfectly. The 2nd and 3rd stopped working while there was substantial glue left. I contacted Xyron who sent me a prepaid label to return 2 cartridges and the dispenser to be tested. I mailed it back today. They will send me new ones later. When it works, it's wonderful.

Just an update...Xyron did replace the dispenser and sent three refills. The way to use this item correctly so as no to gum up the works, is to press down firmly, and pull back slowly thus dispensing all of the glue. Pulling back too fast leaves less glue on the paper.

Every cardmaker and scrapbooker uses a variety of adhesives. This is a must-have.

rating 3.0. Good value for a begginer I'm a beginner and I really loved all the added accessories and the great selection of colors. I only have two isues. The floss doesn't have the color codes so if your using these for a project your going to have to guess and match the colors as best you can. I didn't mind since I'm just learning , allows you to use a little creativity and pick your own colors . The second issue I have is with the fabric or material your given to practice with. Its very hard to make a straight line and I feel makes it harder for a newbie like myself.. I much prefer using some cotton or linen scrapes. Other than those two things, great starter kit.

rating 1.0. Terrible for tumblers I ordered this because my local craft store was out of the brand I usually use. I had 6 tumblers to complete. The 3 that were done with my usual brand came out great. The first one I did with this brand was a disaster. Would not level out or dry. It was so bad that I thought maybe I mixed 2 cups of A or B, instead of combining them. For the 2nd cup, I made sure I was extra cautious. Used the scale to get exact weight for each, then mixed for several minutes. Same thing. Does not level. Looks like there isn't enough on there to cover. I am just hoping it will at least dry enough overnight to be able to sand it down. Otherwise, I'm out the cost for this box, the cost of 2 tumblers, and the work spent on the decals for the 2 that were ruined. So bummed. I may try it again for molds at some point, but I'm convinced this stuff is either diluted or otherwise defective.

rating 1.0. Most important to know is that it DOES NOT harden. I was using this product because I'm attempting to make resin art. Well, I tested it out with some small thing first, tiny Keychain pieces, and the art is still soft and malleable even two days after the curing period. I measured it exactly (1:1 ratio), stirred for three minutes as the box stated, and left it in a nice, room temperature area. I've attempted to add Keychain hooks, and it ripped through each piece I made.

This is NOT a good resin to use, use a name brand.

rating 1.0. Don't use unless you love bubbles! Disappointingly viscous resin will leave you with bubble filled castings that do not escape on their own due to the thick gooey consistency of the resin.  The resin needs to be vacuum degassed or pressure cast to achieve acceptable results, and there are many resins on the market that do a better job with a much lower viscosity.  I will stick with Smooth-on's Crystal Clear 202, which has given me good results so far.

rating 4.0. Best value for quality and price Colors were very nicely toned and accurate to the picture. Softer to the touch over the usual smaller brand cotton floss which was a nice perk. It snapped easily which  is annoying as I like detailed patterns with lots of outlining and shading, but I can easily substitute other colors from my stash so not the biggest issue. For the money it's VERY well worth it. 120 colors, in a wide variety and including many harder to find shades for a discount price with decent strength when using 2 strands or more. Will reorder to keep my palette stocked.

rating 5.0. Beautiful quality and coordinating colors I am very pleased with this selection of thread.  The quality is equal to DMC, and the colors are absolutely beautiful, with a great range. I am using my threads to make the little dolls from[[ASIN:1607058863 Felt Wee Folk - New Adventures: 120 Enchanting Dolls]], with the thread wrapped around a pipecleaner armature and embroidered clothes.  I love that all of the colors coordinate with each other, making it much easier for me to have fun mixing and matching colors.

The best violet in here is a very close match to DMCs 550. I don't have a lot of DMC colors, but if you want to do a lot of shading or huge ombres, get this kit. The ranges are far greater and cohesive than DMC. There were no wild colors that stood out from the rest.

rating 4.0. Affordable alternative to DMC for a beginner cross-stitcher Good overall!  I'll echo what others have said, though: there's no black and no white in this pack, so you'd have to buy them separately.  (Also not that there are two skeins per color.)  This isn't DMC floss, but for me, a beginning/intermediate cross-stitcher, it was perfectly acceptable.  There are no numbers to match to DMC floss colors, though, so you'll have to use your judgment in finding the closest match when using a pattern.  If an exact match is important to you, you should just buy genuine DMC floss...but otherwise, for someone at my level of expertise, this was a great, affordable alternative.

rating 5.0. Great stitch markers for crochet! I love to use these as stitch markers for my crochet. They open easily enough and are large enough to fit worsted weight (4 gauge) yarns. rating 3.0. Work well for mask lanyards but tarnish badly I was looking for lobster class to make mask lanyards that didn't cost a fortune, so that led me to these. They are a little bigger than I really wanted, but I couldn't find anything smaller at a reasonable price. Most of the smaller ones were sterling silver -- too expensive and too small.

These are very sturdy and I have no doubt they will hold up over time. The problem is, they tarnish very badly! I do not know if it's because I live near the ocean in South Florida? It's very frustrating because I've given the mask lanyards away as gifts, and after a few months they turn black as in the attached photo. I have no idea how to clean them or to stop them from tarnishing.

I'm still giving them three stars because of the pretty design, sturdy build, and overall value for the price. Just hoping that most people won't need their mask lanyards by the time the clasps turned black!

If anyone knows how to clean these, please let me know! Thank you!

input: rating 5.0. Great tape maker, easy to use I recently made eighteen yards of bias tape with this tape maker - it was a breeze.  It's easy to use and the finished result looks really good.  If your desired end result is double-fold bias tape, remember that you will need to fold it again after it's initial run through the tape maker and iron it at the center fold. The finished border on your garment or quilt will be one inch wide.  Clover is the only company I could find that manufactures this two inch size bias tape maker.

rating 5.0. Precise and Efficient I purchased this for quilts, but now that I've used it, I'll be looking for other reasons to pull out this great little tool. I couldn't believe how precise the results were, and how easy it is to use. If you don't want to spend the money on a bias tape maker machine, this is the next best thing at a fraction of the price.
rating 5.0. Clover Bias 2-Inch Tape Maker This is a great product.  I plan on using it in making rag rugs with more of a finished look.  Thank you for having this item available.

rating 5.0. Fantastic sewing gadget! I am so happy I have this!!  It makes it so simple to make bias tape!!  I was trying to do it on my own--burning my fingers, the tape not being straight/even... this is SUCH a time saver!

rating 5.0. Very helpful This size completes my set of all available sizes. I love to accentuate quilts and other sewn items with either matching or contrasting binding. Many times, the ready-made tapes don't match the color or the quality of the material, they are not wide or narrow enough and they are rather expensive. Making your own bias tape is also a great way of using scraps.
These tape makers are so easy to use and make bias tape out of the fabrics I WANT. Yes, one has to cut accurate strips to have exact tapes, but the effort is well worth it.

rating 5.0. pays for itself The 2 inch binding is what is used on all the nicer blankets. All you need to make your own is this, some material and a iron. You are guaranteed getting the exact color you are looking for. It is not impossible to make your own but this little gadget gives you a professional finish with straight razor sharp edges. It is not meant for the heavy fleece, or corduroy. It did handle the canvas I have been using on some diaper bags and totes. I have been using it to make some closure tabs and covers for cording on handles. While visiting, my granddaughters got into the pond and made quite a mess of themselves. While their mom was bathing them I grabbed a sheet from the linen closet and had 2 sundresses made before they got out of the tub. When their clothes came out of the dryer they said they would rather wear the dresses I made. I have the one inch also. It is useful for the purpose it is intended. The two inch seems to have so many more uses. I have been looking for this for many years. Thank you Amazon for always having what I need.

rating 5.0. Binder for bias tape This saved a whole lot of time for me when I did a large table runner with heat shield inside. Whipped the tape up in no time and went on smooth. Highly recommend this product and seller!!!!  Thanks

rating 5.0. Love Making Bias Tape Because of this Tool!! Thinking of buying this? Do it! rating 5.0. Great, honest 2" Bias Tape Maker! This is a review for the 2" Bias Tape Maker. Overall, I'm very happy with this bias tape maker! It's one of those rare tools that does just what it is advertized to do.

I bought this 2" bias tape maker so I could make straps for reusable grocery bags. (Because goodness knows no one but Trader Joes knows how to make a long lasting reusable shopping bag!) This tape maker works perfectly for that.

I have found that whether the fabric you are feeding into the tape maker is cut along the bias or not, this little guy performs well regardless! A good tip is to cut the beginning of the strip into an angle so it's easier to feed into the tape maker (this is true of any bias tape maker).

rating 2.0. it would not feed with nice folds without bunching up It did not work for my needs.  I searched online with no information as to what size strip needed to be, to make what size double fold bias tape.  Now I know, 2" tape maker should make double fold tape out of 4" cut strips.  I thought 2" tape make worked with cut 2" fabric strips.  Not the case.  Another problem I had, in experimenting with the sizes, even with 4" strips, it would not feed with nice folds without bunching up.  My fabric was regular to fairly thin thickness cotton.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Works good with some tweaks. I was super happy to find the 2” bias tape maker since I love my smaller ones, however this one is a bit finicky. The instructions said to cut your fabric 3-7/8” wide but this caused the tape maker to make a large wrinkle in the middle of the bias tape while ironing. I had to fix it by hand which was annoying. I reduced my width to 3-3/4” and it seems to have helped. I feel like the smaller tape makers are a bit easier to use, but this is still better than doing it all by hand.

rating 5.0. Sparkle, sparkle Lots of sparkle from these.  I replaced the pendants that came with my chandelier.  I don't know if they were plastic or not but they were starting to get cloudy and there were parts that looked poorly cut when the chandelier was lit.  The Yuetons are the same length and seemed to weigh the same.  Clinking them together produces the same sound as my other pendants.  These are identical on both sides where the other pendants had two somewhat different cuts producing a front and back that required hanging in a certain way.  Altogether not much difference.  I was beginning to think I wasted my money until the next sunny morning and wow.  These are much clearer and give off a lot of sparkle.  There are rings instead of hooks but they are easy to slip on and look better.  I can't believe someone would downgrade them for being on rings.  I also like that I can adjust the overall length by adding or removing the smaller crystals.  I contacted the seller and there is no lead.  The sparkle comes from using optical quality K-9 glass.  I am going to order one more set in case of an accident and also to replace a couple of crystals that are not cut as well as the rest.  The former pendants are going to the Christmas tree.

rating 5.0. Worked as advertised. These work just as I thought. We have some crystal ornaments on our Christmas tree and my husband liked the rainbows they made on the floors/walls. He wanted to hang some crystals in the window for my daughter (23 months) to enjoy. I went on a hunt across Amazon and found these. I was unsure at first because of some of the other reviews say they didn't make rainbows and they seemed cheap in price for real crystals. As soon as we got them we wanted to try them out, however, turns out we pretty much only have LED light sources in our house (no rainbows) and it was evening. The next day I tested them in the sun and BINGO rainbows all over. I have hung all 12 plus 12 ball crystals I also found on Amazon in the window and they make a whole room of rainbows in my house. The husband and daughter LOVE them! Would definitely buy again. There were a couple of crystals that were chipped (the small circle pieces above the tear drop only) but they weren't noticeable to me. Worth the money!!! Good deal.

On a side note they are a bit heavy and I had a hard time keeping them taped to the window frame. Just FYI.

rating 5.0. Suncatcher Supreme [[VIDEOID:e9aaab8dda64fb46556130fca7761845]] I bought these to use for suncatchers in my windows that get morning and afternoon sunshine. Boy, am I amazed at how good these work! They are easily the match of some larger name brand ones that I thought were the best. These cost a fraction of the price and throw just as many rainbows, just as big, and just as far. In fact, yesterday afternoon the sun was shining very brightly as the air was super clear and there was this huge rainbow near the ceiling in my bedroom. Except my bedroom doesn't have any suncatchers. One of these guys was throwing a rainbow easily 6" x 18" on the wall from 35' away in the dining room!

Play around with positioning before you mount or hang them permanently and see which locations and orientations work best. Personally, I like it when they are close to the window without touching it and somewhat in the breeze path of an a/c vent so they move around and you get rainbows dancing all over the place. It's not like a disco since the brighter the sun, the brighter the rainbows, so they stay pleasantly appropriate to lighting, but still really cool. If you have cats, they will have a good time. Enjoy. These are the right ones, just buy it. I've done the shopping for you and this is the deal. Real crystal and less than a buck a piece. Let the sun shine in!

ETA: I saw the rainbow in the video right after I posted this, so I shot a few seconds to give an idea. This rainbow was from one of these pendants about 25' away. Imagine a dozen or so per pendant and you get the drift of what it's like when the sun is brightly shining on them.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Way better than what we paid for! I am giving these five stars simply because so many people are giving them one star because of their size! That is absolutely ridiculous and it makes me Furious! It's not the sellers fault if the buyers are too stupid to know what three mm is! I didn't know so I went and looked. All you have to do is ask Google. 3 mm is 1/8 of an inch! That's pretty tiny. These rhinestones are very sparkly and they're very tiny. It doesn't take a huge bag to put a thousand in there. I didn't count them and I'm not going to. I don't know if there's a gross of rhinestones in there. I paid $3.95 and no shipping. I'm sure I got plenty of rhinestones for my money! I'm not going to run out anytime soon. I got what I needed and I'm satisfied with the amount of money I paid, we all got a bargain yet people are complaining because they didn't know what they ordered and it became someone else's fault! So they're going to blame the sellar! That's what's wrong in this world. I'm not going to turn this into an ethical thing, but it is!! It's absolutely not being accountable for yourself and having to blame someone else for everything because you surely didn't do anything wrong! It was your money and they should have used measurements you understood, even if mm is the standard measurements for stones and jewels!

rating 4.0. This worked OK for what I needed it for...the process was worth it to me. This worked OK for what I needed it for. I have a black, GAP simple raincoat that is carcoat length and about 17 years old. Over the years I've washed it so many times that it began to fade around the sleeve and collar edges and along the front opening edges. The coat is in otherwise perfect condition, and I didn't want to let it go. Since this is a coat and not a t-shirt I was not able to dye it in a pot on the stove. I ended up boiling many pots of water and dumping them into my washing machine with the dye and a little bit of Ivory dish soap. I set more water to boil while I stirred and swirled and was able to keep the water very hot by adding more as the new pots began to boil. I stirred for 30+ minutes. Dying the coat this way turned the clock back about 10 years in appearance...it does not look new again, but it does look much better. This coat is 100% polyester. It did not absorb the dye as well as I thought it would, not even close. I washed and rinsed the coat several times and let it air dry. I had to bleach and scrub out my washing machine tub to remove the black/grey film. The process was worth it to me.

rating 3.0. Will not work on all upholstery fabric Following word for word on the Rit Dye tutorial for upholstered items, I set out to dye a small truck jump seat (tan to black.

Directions specify to use either a sprayer or in their case, a standard paint brush.

I am not sure if they added 1 or 60 bottles of dye to their witches brew, but I can tell you that 1 bottle to 3 gallons of hot water will turn a tan chair into a pretty horrific shade of purple!

Back up plan is to reupholster, so what the heck.

Next day,..... lets try a bottle of the standard Rit Dye, black..... but concentrated.

It goes on nicely!
Looks like it will do the job......
NOPE!

Anyway, I am now learning to reupholster :)

rating 5.0. Second times the charm. For the first dye bath I used two bottles of graphite for an article of clothing weighing less than 1/3 of a pound, which I thought the over kill would produce the darkest color. It ended up being more of a grey and the dye ran each time I hand washed it. So I decided to try again. This time I used two bottles of graphite, half a bottle of red, and a little bit of the blue. Same bath time and temp. Came out much darker. And this time I used the fixative and it has helped with bleeding, and subsequently the color. Do it right the first time. Follow the color formulas on the Rit Dye website, and use the fixative no matter what.

rating 5.0. High temp method really works for synthetics!!!! I will be honest, I have not read a single review of this product. I simply bought 2 bottles for $10 to try to dye my light color synthetic motorcycle pants, which I wanted dark grey or black. Dark grey in this case, was perfect. Using the stove top (high temp water) and 2 bottles in a large 5 gallon bucket, I could happier. My light color motorcycle parts are now a PERFECT dark grey, saving me over $200 for new pants.

My picture shows a light color jacket, which was the EXACT color of the pants I dyed.

rating 5.0. Eyeglass Frames Darkened Perfectly! Worked perfectly!  Used to dye eyeglasses frames.  The tortoise shell was too light - almost a leopard spot look.  Used it about half-and-half with water.  Note:  Because I was worried about heating the frames too much, at first I tried it cold without much success.  Then placed the container with the dye and frames in a pot of water just boiled with the flame turned off.  It heated the dye just enough to react with the plastic and color the frames rapidly but not cause them lose their shape. The tortoise shell is still visible but much more subdued.  Excellent!  Thanks!

rating 3.0. Like all Rit products I've ever used... It's not horrible. Not terrible, but nothing spectacular.  I tried dying a pair of pants that are 65% polyester and 35% cotton.  The dye managed to do reasonably well on the larger panels of the garment but did zero on the thread and the poly cuff tabs it left green. Now, I WAS dying over a light green so I expected some of that color to still show. However when you are close enough to the fabric, you can see that the dye took to either the cotton or the polyester better so I have sort of a checkerboard pattern. It's not horrible but I wouldn't use it on a finished garment that you care about particularly.

rating 3.0. Poor instructions, unpredictable results This dye will "tint" whatever you are dying and some fabrics will take up the tint more than others. I had couch covers that had faded from the sunlight. I had to dye the tops and bottoms separate. Even though I did the same amount of time, same dye, and very similar volume and temperature, the bottom cushions came out slightly darker. And since it was merely tinting it, the fading could still be seen. It certainly made it harder to notice the fading, so it was still an improvement. The graphite also has a blue tone to it, so my graphite treated grey cushions turned to a dark grey-teal. It's a lovely color, but certainly not "graphite". Overall, the instructions are vague and the results can vary wildly. I wouldn't use this on anything you were already planning on throwing it.

rating 1.0. Defective - needles cut thread I finally got around to using these needles and could not figure out why the upper thread kept breaking. Since I hadn't used my sewing machine in over a year, I thought it needed an adjustment. Nothing worked, so I carefully observed where the thread was cutting, and it was at the needle. I tried several needles of different sizes in this batch, but they all did the same thing.

I wrote the seller to explain the problem, along with mentioning that other people had reported the same issue. The seller was aware of the problem, and even though the return window had expired, s/he offered to send a replacement. S/he said it will take about 20 days to receive it, so we shall see. If the new ones are any better, I will update this review.

rating 4.0. A nice little set This is a very affordable set of good-quality drawing pencils and charcoal. The pencils range in hardness from 4H all the way to 8B. There are three different types of charcoal as well at different hardnesses. I was a little disappointed that there was no F pencil for fine details, but everything else I need is there, so I'm not inclined to complain at this price.

If anything, the set contains several extra things that I don't need. It would be nice if I could find a set that just included pencils, as I need to restock every few months or so. I don't need a new pencil sharpener every time, and I rarely ever use charcoal. The included erasers are pretty tiny too, though they are somewhat useful. But even if you took out all of these "extras," the price still beats my local art store by a mile, so I'll take it, and I'd recommend this to any pencil artist.

The erasers are small and ineffective.

This is literally a waste of your money. Just spend a few dollars more on General, Staedtler, Derwent, or literally anything else.

rating 5.0. 5-Star, Would Buy Again and Again I have bought a total of three sets of these on two different occasions; I bought a double set for my own personal use to share with my sister, and another set recently for a friend. The pencils are tough and won't break easily, the sharpener is super sharp and can sharpen even the crappiest of cheap school-grade or annoying colored pencils, and the pencils write smoothly and don't catch on your paper. I could rave on for days about how glorious woodless pencils are. This one writes smoothly and is tough as well. The erasers included are both excellent as well. The graphite and charcoal sticks are nice but I personally keep them separated in plastic baggies so they don't get medium all over my kneaded erasers, other drawing utensils or pencil cases. The charcoal pencils are also very nice but sometimes pesky when sharpening. The soft ones' charcoal can break easily so you have to sharpen them carefully. This is an excellent set, especially for its price. Overall this is a five-star product that I love and would recommend.

rating 5.0. I found this easy to use and just got my first mold made I found this easy to use and just got my first mold made.

I make candles and have found some molds difficult to find. Or of a quality that I would sell the end product. So I decided to try making my own. Instructions were easy, I didn't have to learn a whole new hobby to get started and have a finished product.

Have made my first mold, an apple I bought from the store and then polyurethaned before trying to make the mold. Came out great, the candle looks exactly like the apple I made it from. Even to the point of the small bruised spot on the apple. Gives the candle a uniqueness.

I found some pitting in the candle when made, apparently from air bubbles in the mold. But I think the bubbles in the mold are due more to my inexperience when making the mold, than a product error.

You can do one small object with the pound of rubber. I plan on ordering more after writing this review. Very excited!

rating 1.0. Made two orders, one was a bust. I made my first order for this product from the Crafter's Companion seller, and then made another order about a month later when I realized I would need more for my project. I used the second item I received first and it worked well. The hardening agent had a pink tint which mixed well with the base component. However, when I used ~half of the item I received from Crafter's Companion, I saw that the hardening agent in that package was clear without any pink tint. I attempted to use it anyway and found the mixture turned gummy immediately and quickly turned into an unusable mess well before the listed working time was over. I tried using the remainder and worked quickly to pour the mix before 5 minutes had passed, but the mixture was unusable.

Overall not pleased with this product. I have contact Crafter's Companion about the issue and will see if they respond. But for nearly $40 there shouldn't be a 50/50 chance whether I the product works or not.

rating 5.0. Made a great, strong cast. READ ALL INSTRUCTIONS AT LEAST TWICE!! Don't forget gloves! The "base" comes in a wide mouth jar making it very difficult to pour - it's THICK! Like Elmer's glue thick! Runs down the side of the jar. Have on hand a tongue blade, plastic knife, something to scrape the runaway back into the jar. Why they didn"t put this stuff in a measured squeeze bottle, I'll never know. That would make it SO simple to use. Other than that I'd say buy 2, especially if you have a big project. Mine was a 10" round pattern which 1 jar barely covered. Directions say to cover it at least 1/4" deeper than your project. Good advice. Mine came out perfect after a full 24 hr dry time! I would recommend it. Be sure you use newspapers or a plastic bag under your project. Messy, but it works.

rating 5.0. There's different mixing ratios by weight or by volume Just pay attention to the mixing ratios, I did it by volume which took a long time with the provided scooper.  Next time I'll use a kitchen scale and mix by weight.  I used the kit to mold an intake port for a Honda cylinder head.  I used silicone spray lubricant as a mold release before pouring as well.  I let it set for 2 or 3 days before trying to remove it, this stuff is DURABLE, I separated the rubber from the metal as much as possible, and then just got a good grip on the big and and pulled until the mold came out.  Took some force, but it didn't even leave a mark on the mold.  Very happy with the results

Began hardening after just 3 minutes off stirring when I went to stir the silicone, i noticed that it was a different color than shown on the package (pink on package, white in person). i measured both components by weight and made sure to use a 10-1 (115g to 11.5g) ratio, but after just 3 minutes of stirring i noticed it had started to thicken. when i went to pour the silicone into my mold, it had thickened to the point that it didn't flow nicely into my mold. this meant that it trapped many bobbles on the surface despite my best efforts to tap them out, it also was not able to self level on the top surface of the silicone and cured will a bumpy non-level surface. i quickly returned what was left and bought some cheep pink stuff from another brand which worked perfectly first try.

rating 5.0. Amazing indeed! One of the very best mold materials for the home crafter! Really excellent product for capturing fine details! Please be advised that this is not appropriate for hard clays like sculpey etc. as the super flexibility may cause distortion if you are attempting to pack a hard material into it. That said it works wonderfully with liquid clays as you can bake right in the mold. If using a soft air dry clay please remember that you need to add extra days drying time as the only evaporative surfaces are the ones that are still exposed to the air directly. Improper measuring of the materials will give you a sticky result so it really does help to invest in a little digital scale that does fractional grams. Wear gloves and only mix in containers you consider disposable and please allow good ventilation when mixing as it has quite an odor until fully cured. To avoid air bubbles in your finished mold it really helps to have a little vibration from the bottom before you pour as well as after the piece has been poured piercing the bubbles that come to the surface with a pin. Be advised since this is not a platinum cured silicone is is not meants for contact with skin. Happy sculpting!

rating 3.0. BAD LOT? -- -- UPDATE! I took this situation to Alumilite Customer Service and was advised that Amazon is apparently not rotating the stock of 'High Strength 3' silicone, allowing batches to sit past their 6 month shelf life and effectively 'go bad'.

rating 1.0. NOT a 40 min pot life. More like 5 minutes tops. I love making dice molds. The very first one I ever made was with Alumilite Amazing Mold Rubber and it was fantastic. Naturally, I had high hopes for this product of theirs as well. I was...horribly disappointed. I figured this would be a better mold, as it was supposed to be a bit softer and more flexible. The end result was, in fact, softer and more flexible. However, the first batch I mixed didn’t even make it to the mold box. I followed the directions to the letter, then put the mixed rubber into my degassing chamber. The whole process took maybe 10-15 minutes. When I removed it from the chamber, it was already solidified. As the box clearly says “40 minute pot life”, I naturally assumed that perhaps I had mixed it incorrectly or something. So, frustrated but not deterred, I mixed another batch. I set a stopwatch starting from the time I combined the rubber and the catalyst in the cup to the time I went to pour it, including the time in the degassing chamber. All in all, it was just over 5 minutes. Upon removing it from the chamber, it was already thickening up. I thought “okay, I’ll pour quickly”. Still, it began to clump before I was finished. I’m not sure if maybe it was mislabeled (although the label clearly states that it’s High Strength 3), or if it was just a bad batch. But I will be returning the remnants for a refund.

Other than that, the shirt looks great and it would like 95% well. Functionally correct, but auxiliarily a few problems. Ohh and the package the paper was in was a bit damaged (dirty, cardboard was a bit bent, etc.) but nothing too major. Perhaps that could have caused the problems but I'm not sure. rating 1.0. Wouldn't work I have used these transfer sheets before, with great results. However, this was NOT the same. I printed the image on my printer, per the usual. I pre washed and pre ironed like the instructions say. I used a cotton pillow case and a dry, hard surface. i pressed with two hands on an iron on the cotton setting, without water.

As i said, I have used these before with ease and great end products.

This time, no matter what setting I set my printer to, the image was pale, dull in colour. Then when i went to iron it onto a 100% cotton, prewashed and ironed shirt, the paper refused to peel away from the transfer. My husband tried, I tried, we just tried anything we could think of. Nothing would make the paper release from the transfer. We had to scrub the paper off with a wet wash cloth. The transfer was dull, just bad colour quality.

Bottom line is that, I don't know what happened to this particular batch of iron on transfer sheets, but I will not buy Avery brand anymore.

rating 4.0. Works great when done right I bought these transfer sheets because I was going to a concert with my friend and we wanted to make our own t-shirts. I bought two 95% cotton 5% spandex shirts and we tried to make our shirts.Anyway, we did everything right, except washing the shirts, which i don't think made a big difference. We rounded the edges of our images, had a pillowcase set up and ironed the shirts more than enough, but both the shirts came out with ripped corners and a thin layer of the images. After washing them , not knowing they were already ruined, they got worse. They were cracked and just not wearable

About a week later I tried again with Russel exercise shirts I got at walmart. I believe they were 50% cotton and 50% polyester. I washed them before hand and cut them into the mussel t type shirt we wanted. THEY TURNED OUT GREAT. I really don't know what was different but each shirt had a vibrant, thick, sturdy image on it. I still have my t-shirt and I have washed and worn it numerous times and it still looks great

TIPS:
- I recommend washing your shirts. If not for the sheets not working better, than for sizing issues. I would recommend not using these sheets on skin tight shirts because it increases the chance of cracking and is just bad for the shirt.
- Also, if you think you have ironed enough, go back and do it again.
-Round the corners. This is very important
- I would recommend not using these sheets on skin tight shirts because it increases the chance of cracking and is just bad for the shirt.

rating 5.0. Anything below 5 stars is due to lack of experience. When I ordered these I was at first a bit worried, because the packaging was different than the picture posted on Amazon, but the production date printed on it was 2013 so Avery must have changed their packaging.

I've used HP iron on transfers before, except they required thin tracing paper placed over the transfer for them to work, which was a hassle because of the tracing paper slipped off the design and you hit the transfer it would melt/ruin it.These Avery ones eliminate that problem.

As for the quality of these, they worked great; much better than the HP ones I've used in the past.

I'd recommend them, and if it's your first time using them, be very careful with the ironing; follow the directions exactly as they are listed and you don't have any problems.

rating 4.0. 4.5 Stars--Overall, Great Product! I purchased this product in order to make shirts for my son's birthday party. Overall, the shirts came out well, however, I cannot say how they will hold up during a wash as I don't plan to wash them before giving them out.

It is obvious that the image is an iron on as you can see the white film if you print the full image (which is why I marked down---although I would rate it a 4.5 if I were able).

The directions were a little tricky as well, be sure to choose a mirror image unless you want to end up with a backwards print, of course I learned this the hard way (in haste). I just went to the Avery site and selected the type of paper and printed it that way as I don't have the mirror option on my printer.

Lastly, the directions recommend that you don't use an ironing board, I did so, however I put s folder under the shirt for a harder surface.

I do recommend the product.

-------Update------

I purchased this product for the second time in order to make shirts for my sons and once again I am pleased. This time I cut around the picture rather than leaving the picture rectangle shaped and the edges weren't as apparent as with my previous shirts. This is definitely still my go to Transfer. I can't account for whether or not the print will hold up in the wash considering the previous shirts that I made were party favors. This time around I will have the opportunity to wash and I will update my review at that time.

rating 4.0. Works pretty well for my purposes I purchased this product to make older book pages more able to handle the water used in watercolor. I have an older book I'm using as a journal. I usually work in acrylic paint, so I gesso the pages first. But I wanted to be able to write on the pages in ink or pencil and then watercolor over the writing. When I tried it without the ground, the watercolor soaked through even though I used the least amount of water possible. When I tried again on another page using the watercolor ground, the page was able to accept significantly more water without bleeding through. I don't know what others will use this product for, but for me, it makes thin, highly porous paper better able to withstand moderate amounts of water. If you want to do great art, use high-quality watercolor paper. If you have a journal and you need to make it a little more accepting of wet media, the watercolor ground will help. For me, I think it's superior to gesso when you intend to work in watercolor. If you are going to do mixed media, then use gesso.

rating 5.0. Perfect for coloring books! Fast and easy way to make cheap coloring book paper accept traditional watercolor or Inktense with a wet brush.  I wanted to use my play time to improve my beginner watercolor skills, instead of relying on colored pencils or markers.  But even the books designed for watercolors were pilling and blotching under my heavy handed treatment.  The DS transparent ground gives enough protection and a slightly toothy surface, plus allows more lifting, which has vastly improved my results.  Very pleased!  NB! I cheat on the application to make life easier (it’s play time, after all).  Spritz about a teaspoon of water into a recycled mayo jar lid, soak a 1 1/2” flat taklon brush and use it to scoop a good dollop (3-4 tablespoons) of the ground into the lid.  Using the brush, stir until the water is absorbed, scrape off the brush into the lid, and paint one thin layer onto as many pages as possible until you run out of ground.  Spread them out to dry, wash the brush and lid, and have a cup of coffee.  By the time the coffee is gone, something will be dry enough to paint on.  Put the other pages away to cure for later, but I haven’t had any problems just diving in, as soon as everything’s dry to the touch.  Obviously, this is not recommended to real art, but it works fine for casual play.

I was able to blend the colors showing the layers of gold coin in the hatchlings cavern, only because of the pre-preping. Granted, I have a lot to learn about using the markers to color a face, but all in all, I think the sorceress in the red cloak turned out pretty good. Again, both pictures were done on flimsy paper from budget-friendly color books, and show something I could never have accomplished without the pre-prep of the Daniel Smith Ground. It's a purchase I'm happy with and when I use it up, I will certainly be buying it again.

rating 5.0. Watercolor on anything!! It was fun to experiment with the transparent ground. I had cold-pressed ground but got this one to see the difference. I can watercolor on wood with special prep and have the wood grain show through. Instructions are clear and it's easy to use.

rating 5.0. I adore this stuff! I love this! It's completely changed the way I think about materials for watercolor. Wood? Canvas? Old magnets? All can now be used for watercolor! Slather it on with a flat brush, let it dry and sand it lightly to even it out and your previously unpaintable surface can now be decorated! The colors can bleed around the edges in more detailed pieces so you do need caution and to practice with it before jumping into something huge. And whatever brush texture you have on the wet ground will stay when it's dry. I'm really happy I gave this a try, I enjoy this product immensely

rating 5.0. Like clear, liquid paper! I paint large 3x4’ watercolors and wanted to try painting on something other than white paper. I primed a wood panel with clear gesso and then this transparent watercolor ground- I recommend  letting it cure for the full 48hrs as it absorbed the watercolor paint better than at the 24hr mark. Also play around with how you apply it- using a brush left behind the stroke lines and the roller gave it more toothy texture. It doesn’t have quite the same porosity as paper and feels a bit more like plastic (it is acrylic based), but it’s the best clear watercolor ground I’ve found so far.

rating 5.0. Excellent quality This is an excellent watercolor ground. Painting on this is much like painting on good, high quality watercolor paper, but you can put it on practically anything. I only wish the transparent came in larger sizes

rating 4.0. Worked--Needs Directions Though I bought this to paint my 3 year old's face for his Halloween ghost costume. I have never used face paint before, and I have to say, this was a challenge to use for the sheer fact that I could find no directions with it for how to use it. I tried just wiping it out of the tin and couldn't get it to spread. Only after googling it and going to the company website did I learn you need a damp cloth to apply it, and after that, it was easy (relatively--a three year-old does NOT like things painted on his face).

I didn't end up with a thick coat, but that was only because my son wouldn't let me do it. You can see the nice tear track cutting through it because he hated the whole process, but I think it just makes him look like a tragic spooky ghost. I would purchase again, but I wish it would come with better directions or some techniques.

rating 2.0. Really patchy and hard to use You really have to layer it on for it to look ok. It will look ok if you put work.

rating 1.0. You Get What You Pay For Never has this old saying been more true than in the case of this Snazaroo face paint. Price-wise it was cheap and the quality of the product was just as cheap. You'd need a jackhammer to get the stuff out of the shallow dish in which it is packaged. It was hard as a rock, totally unusable as-is. Dry as a bone. Had to resort to prying out cracked portions into a bowl and then adding water to obtain a spreadable consistency, but given just how dry and hard the product was, this was no easy task. End result was that a make-up job for my son's Halloween party that should have only taken an hour, took three since I had also ordered Snazaroo brand for the other colors and every one was in the same condition. Complete garbage product, will NEVER use this brand name again. Save your money and pony up to buy professional-grade theatrical make-up.

rating 2.0. Not Recommended I was looking for a cheaper alternative to Ben Nye and Graftobian Pro Paint, so I tried this. I feel like it's almost impossible to get full coverage from this face paint, it is extremely streaked and smears easily, and takes forever to dry. When it does dry it cakes into the skin and is very easy to crack-- I wasn't able to blend anything in afterwards because the face paint would just come off. However, the worst part about this for me was that after I used it and washed it off thoroughly, my skin has been out of control with breakouts.
