rating 3.0. Inconsistent quality control, lack of customization Personal opinion, a grid shouldn't be the only thing you depend on for accurate measurements. If you do like having grids, then these are mostly fine. If it doesn't line up on one edge, flip it around and it will probably line up another way.

However, as you can see from the picture, the quality control is really bad. Some of the mats have the grid and logo printed on very clearly, and then others are incredibly faded, fresh out of the box. Also, if you look at the joins in the picture, the corners have a different shape, meaning you can only make them fit together as a grid. If you were trying to block a shawl with a more irregular shape, you can't extend it by putting one square off center.

The foam is pretty thick, though, so this should still work out just fine for my immediate needs. The squares don't slip once they're put together, and ultimately that's what I need right now. As I progress through this craft I will need something with better quality control and versatility.

rating 3.0. Rusty Pins The blocking mats are great but the pins are NOT rust proof. I am a relatively new knitter so I did not know the importance of finding rust proof blocking pins. You don’t want to spend months on a project only to have it ruined by dozens of rust stains. The pins rusted after about the 4th use and left ugly orange stains all over my white cotton shawl.

rating 1.0. poor quality I have a set of Hephaestus blocking mats and I need more for a bigger project. rating 1.0. Avoid These are thick, allow blocked works to dry quickly, and so far the supplied needles haven’t rusted. UPDATE: needles left light rust spots on a 200-hour white piece...between that and the mismatched lines, I regret buying these.

rating 4.0. Difficult to configure with longer pieces These go together easily but if you want to configure anything other than a square the lines don’t match up no matter how you turn them. My project is 45” long so I thought ok 12x4 is 48 perfect! Nope. The squares are 12” but that includes the cut out portions to put them together which means 4 squares is 45”. As a result my project goes all the way to the ends and large parts of the edge pieces have noting holding them up. I’m hoping they dry evenly and if not I’m hoping if I just re-do the ends they will come out evenly. My project is also 14” wide so there were not two extra squares to make it longer. I wish it came with just one more square or was more honest about the size of the squares. I can’t speak to durability as I just got it today.

rating 3.0. Parts fit together to make a handy blocking board The various mat parts fit together like a jigsaw puzzle to make an easy to use and easy to pack away blocking board.  The mats are labeled to let you know which ones attach to each other, and can be configured as a wide rectangle or long and narrow one, making it versatile for longer scarves.  I purchased this set of blocking mats with 150 T pins and it was supposed to be a new set,  but it came obviously used. There were less than 130 pins and several of the remaining pins were severely bent.  I was able to use the set successfully for blocking two shawls and a scarf, but paying full price for an obviously used and incomplete set was annoying. I left feedback for the seller over a month ago and was never contacted. I would not be inclined to buy anything from this seller again.

rating 3.0. Terrible chemical smell and way overpriced for the quality Three star rating. The item is as discribed but docked two stars for the following reasons:
1. The smell is absolutely overwhelming. After unpackaging and popping together had a full blown headache. Absolutely noxious smell!
2. The “storage box” is nothing more than the crappy cardboard box it was received in. This is fine but the item alludes to more as typically storage boxes aren’t paper.

Lastly, if you need these for big projects do yourself a favor and order foam exercise floor mats. They are the same price, exactly the same thing but without lines, and 144 total square feet for the same money. They don’t stink like these, more durable, and you won’t need to play the game of whether the sets will
fit together like these. I wish I had realized this fact sooner.

rating 5.0. A dream come true for crochet projects Love these blocks. They are thick and fit together perfectly. Makes blocking my crochet projects easy. I spent many years using foamcore board and a ruler to block- never again.
Also, customer service is incredible! Thanks.

rating 2.0. Not the best These mats are not great. I purchased them while on vacation, and they simply don’t compare to my original blocking mats at home . Two major problems:

1) The grid doesn’t line up from mat to mat, getting straight lines for blocking is almost impossible.

2) I was excited to get 150 T-pins, but only receive 101.

When blocking long times, straight lines and enough pins are a must. These will definitely end up being used in the future for small, single mat blocking projects.

rating 2.0. ... one set and a small square these would be great. However my project requires multiple sets and I ... If I only needed one set and a small square these would be great. However my project requires multiple sets and I cannot get the grid lines to match up. Very disappointing

But airing out took care of the problem, and the seller was extremely responsive when I contacted them with my concern. I would buy these again if I need more for a larger project.

rating 3.0. The brushes are hot glued to the packaging, so ... The brushes are hot glued to the packaging, so it takes some patience to get them out. Otherwise, they are decent for the price you pay.

rating 4.0. They also do a good job as dust-removers and whisk brushes for use inside ... These artificial-fiber brushes aren't as supple as your better-quality art brushes that use natural animal fur. However they are dirt cheap and as such, that makes them suited for a lot of jobs you wouldn't want to trash your fancy sable brushes on. In my case I use them for applying synthetic grease to metal parts that need careful application. They also do a good job as dust-removers and whisk brushes for use inside of complex mechanisms.

If I was still building model airplanes that required a fine level of detail and control, I wouldn't want to use these brushes - but for general use they are an excellent value.

rating 4.0. Begginer Water-Color Artist I purchased these for my beginning water-color college class. I paint with acrylic and this was my first time using this medium so I had very little knowledge on any good brands for brushes. I did not want to invest in expensive water-color brushes because I was uncertain if I would pursue this medium outside of this course so I went for these after reading the reviews. Fortunately I was very satisfied with this purchase. The brushes are short-handled which was great; this is my personal preference over long-handled brushes. None of the bristles fell out so I didn't have to worry about my work getting covered with the hair. The brushes worked well with the water-color paint and I had no problem working on any work. Although the brushes do come in a variety of sizes, as seen in the photo, I would recommend buyers to purchase more washes and larger round brushes. Aside from the lack of larger round brushes, which was much needed for my use, and beside the fact that I've yet to compare these to any other popular brand water-color brushes, this is a great set for any beginner water-color artist.

rating 5.0. Definitely Recommend! (Wish It Included Bigger Brush Though) I honestly love these brushes.

I read one review that pointed out the fact that the bristles dye to the color you are using. I found this to be true, but it won't affect your work in any way. I find the emphasis of this point to be a little over the top honestly, given the great price for the set.

They spread wonderfully, and they feel and look high quality.

My ONLY complaint is that since I am using these with acrylics, I wish they included a bigger brush than what is included. I think the amount of small brushes is somewhat... redundant. I think they should have included a 1.5 inch or 2 inch, that would've made me love this set even more.

Even if you get the soap bar into the bag trying to seal it is so difficult We tried with 18 bars of soap and never even made it to trying to shrink the wrap. We might try it again but only when we are very well rested and have nothing else planned for the day.
other_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Bottles leaked while being shipped creating a mess. I have ordered a few of these sets and while in the sets the bottles are normally not sealed I have never had an issue until this set. Two of the bottles leaked while being shipped and while there is still oil left in both bottles it was a mess once the box was open. Other than that this set was not to our liking but it has strong scents that are easy to use in soap making and the scents do not dissipate as quickly as a few other brands we have tried.

rating 1.0. One mold not usable. Three out of the four molds seem fine but sadly the one that I bought the set for is not usable. The leaf stems are left open along the edges. When the molds are filled part of the soap runs out the not seal part of the mold. The other three sets of molds are fine but not what I needed.

rating 5.0. Pretty and what you expect. I bought these to add to soaps. The colors are very pretty and you get a very good verity in this set. The only issue we have is the bags are sometimes hard to open and zip shut but it will not stop us from buying these again, which we plan on doing.

input: rating 5.0. Wonderful, vibrant colors! I love these pens. I prefer a water based marker for my projects, mostly for painting on wood. But these pens can be used on many surfaces. The colors are vibrant and the ink comes to the tip very well! When I need more I will but the same pack! The only reason I didn’t mark the “longevity” is because I haven’t had them long enough.
rating 2.0. SmaLlllllllllllllll Way small like crayon size

rating 4.0. A few issues but overall good pens. First purchase of these Artistro pen set. I enjoy working with them however, They seem to need a lot more shaking with use and will stop putting out ink until shaken and the point pushed up and down.++111111+ The more they are used the more bits of paint, mostly dried, begins to stick. I found using a rubber glove and pulling while twisting works best. Color coverage is good. Will buy again.
rating 5.0. Perfect I used these pens to paint my wooden Catan pieces. I stained the wood, then used these pens to do the detail work. I was very happy.
rating 1.0. Terrible These are terrible you cannot prime them to get the paint to come down to the tip they are horrible to use don’t buy them do not waste your money

rating 5.0. Amazing paint pens! The paint is opaque and smooth on most surfaces I like these a LOT more than I thought I would. The color range is great, the liquid paint is very smooth and it's opaque enough to cover mistakes on my drawings. The fine tips are great for details and also feel very durable (like it can take a beating). The only downside is that priming all 30 paint pens takes a while and the caps need to push down very hard to "click" closed, which irritates the skin on my thumbs.

rating 5.0. Amazing! Wonderful! Perfect!!! These are the best paint pens I’ve ever used! They are so much better than oil based pens. Note: if you have started a project with oil-based paint pens, these will cover it, but it will take several coats. The oil based paint bleeds through. But if you are starting a new project, just skip the oil based pens and use these!!! You do need a spray coat to protect the art, or if you want a glossier finish. These have an eggshell-type finish when dry. Just a little more sheen than flat or matte paint.
Great GREAT PENS!

rating 5.0. How awesome are these?! The most surprising thing about these is that the tips of these markers are incredibly durable.
You do need to shake them quite vigorously when you first get them or after they have sat for a while. Markers that haven't been used yet require patience while you get the paint to the tip.
When I was first priming my markers I was concerned that pushing on the tips would make them soft and ruin the fine point that they had.
This couldn't be further from the truth. I was pleasantly pleased with how well they performed once primed.
The paint dries fairly well, not too fast not too slow. As long as you don't over prime them they don't run.
It would be nice if the markers were labeled with the intended color. The box claimed there was a crimson red and a wine red. They looked obvious by way of the colored caps, but the crimson red looked a little too light in my opinion for the color it was claimed to be.
Otherwise no complaints. Well worth the money!

rating 5.0. Great paint pens Great quality pens!  I use them in a variety of surfaces and haven’t found anything they don’t work on. Shake em up good, burp em & pump em slowly 8-10 times and they’re good to go! They’re great for detail work and have a greater variety of colors than most other paint pens.

rating 1.0. THEY LEAK!! I was super disappointed when i opened my pens and started to use them!!! The colors are so light and the pens leak paint everywhere out of the tip!!! rating 5.0. Perfect for times when a paintbrush is hard to use These pens make it really easy to apply paint in smaller areas where it is hard for me to use a paint brush. I love them! I have used them on rocks and canvas. They really add a great finishing touch.

rating 3.0. Some work, some don't I really want to like those paint pens. The ones working are great but I got only 6 out of 30 with a constant paint flow. Most of them you have to shake and prime constantly to get a couple lines before the paint flow stops and you have to start over. 4 pens barley work at all and painting on wooden blocks for my kid, I was ready to burn those pens or at least throw them out of a window.
I also tried them on paper with the same result.

rating 5.0. Best flowing painting pens ever! Really good paint pens. Dont mind the pumping to get the ink out..with patience comes perfection. Once these babies get flowing it's even and straight. No clumps..no runny paint  just pure drawing pleasure. Colors are vibrant. I bought these because of the variety of color. I had a blast. Dont let the negative reviews fool you. This is an excellent group of paint pens. I am doing pet rocks next!

rating 5.0. Clarification On Colors Between 42/30/12/15(special) Extra-Fine Tip Paint Pens So first off: Great Pens! A lot of fun and easy to use! But I'm not here to sell you these pens, instead if you are interested in buying these extra-fine tip paint pens like I was, you may be wondering 'what set should I get?' Well, let me help!

I will go over which extra-fine tip sets have which colors and their overlaps, as well as the odd choice Artistro has made in naming the same color twice for two different sets (maybe their hoping you'll buy the same product twice?).

Firstly, if you want all the colors (not including the extra metallic colors from the metallic set, and the glitter colors from the glitter set) then all you will need is the 42 box set! It's generally priced around a little more than a dollar a pen, which is a pretty good deal. I have shared a picture of the colors listed on the box for your reference as well as a picture of all the pens lined up in the same order.

Next, you may notice the third picture I've shared is also pen colors from the side of a box; it is the 30 box set. Strangely enough these would appear to be very different colors! Oh, but not so! Almost every single "additional" color here is just renamed (I happen to like the majority of these names better, by the way) without an actual new addition to be found. (Also I'm not sure if the print settings were off when the packaging was produced, but their color swatches are very different.) So below is a list of the 30 colors from the 30 box set and their corresponding twin from the 42 box set:

30 box set                   42 box set
1. Chartreuse           = Bud Green
2. Spring Lemon      = Yellow
3. Sunburst Yellow  = Orange Yellow
4. Coral Orange       = Orange
5. Scarlet Red          = Red
6. Wine Red             = Purple Red
7. Pale Pink             = Sakura Pink
8. Pastel Violet       = Light Purple
9. Light Violet         = Purple
10. Violet                 = Dark Purple
11. Indian Blue        = Blue
12. Sky Blue             = Sky Blue
13. Light Turquoise = Shallow Green
14. Spruce Green     = Dark Green
15. Walnut               = Brown
16. Putty                  = Tan
17. Light Cream      = Cream Yellow
18. Pale Yellow       = Khaki
19. Gold                  = Gold
20. Cool Gray          = Cold Grey
21. Warm Grey       = Dark Grey
22. Silver                = Silver
23. White               = White
24. Black                = Black
25. Turquoise         = Jewel Green
26. Emerald Green = Green
27. Vivid pink         = Pink
28. Baby Blue         = Brilliant Blue
29. Extra White      = Extra White
30. Extra Black       = Extra Black

So, if you are curious to know which other colors are actually in the 42 box set, but maybe don't want to do the work of searching and cross-checking the two pictures I provided, I will list them here:

31. Fluorescent Yellow (yup, that's what it is)
32. Emerald Green (it's brighter than the "Emerald" Green from the 30)
33. Melanie (it's a greenish blue-green color despite being named weird)
34. Sapphire (a little brighter than Sky Blue)
35. Purple Stars (blue-violet)
36. Pigeon Blue (a dark grey blue)
37. Tawny (a mix of orange and tan)
38. Camellia Red (a bright red, almost pink)
39. Dream Purple (a little warmer and darker than Light Purple)
40. Bright Pink (more natural in tone than Pink's neon look)
41. Run Red (it's a light orange cream color)
42. Pale Pinkish Grey (terrible name; it's very similar to Run Red, a little less bright)

So, if you got the 30 box set and you decided you wanted the colors you are missing out on from the 42 box set, is there a way to get them without buying duplicates of all your 30 colors? Yes! Almost. Minus one: Fluorescent Yellow. The 15 box set called "Special Colors" here on Amazon, but labeled "Trending Colors" on its package actually has all your remaining colors needed sans that super bright yellow. You'll also get repeats of Chartreuse and Sunburst Yellow, as well as the ever-present White and Black. So not a bad buy if you just want to expand from your 30 box set.

But maybe you're like, 'I just want some basic colors, why have you been wasting my time with all these fancy color names?' Well, you probably should just get the 12 box set then. It's very basic. It has Black, White, Gold, Silver, Pink, Dark Purple, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, Red, and Brown. Have fun!

rating 5.0. Precise and perfect! I’m using them to paint rocks that I place out in the world for people to find.
I like these pens because the nib is hard allowing for precision even if the surface is slightly textured. Some people mentioned frustration with getting the paint flow started so I thought I would share what worked for me—it was quick and easy. After unwrapping all my pens, I would take five at a time, uncap them and hold them altogether in my hand. I depressed the nib on paper 3-4 times, recapped them and then shook until I heard the bead inside the barrel move for 10-20 seconds and voila! It worked. So in a matter of a couple of minutes all my pens were flowing and ready to go! I hope this helps💞

rating 5.0. Best I've tried This is by far my favorite set of acrylic paint markers that I've gotten so far. I like to paint rocks and tag them on the back to Western WV rocks Facebook page and I hide them around the community in town for people to find post a picture on that site and rehide for others to find another day.

rating 5.0. Great fun for stone painting! I've been painting on stones for a long time (with paint) and these pens lived up to my hopes for them. The colors are nice and fairly opaque (some more than others). The gold and silver are a little lackluster, so I will be looking for a more shimmery and true version of those colors in another brand. Be sure to store pens on their sides and shake before each use. The pigment separates a bit when not in use, so you must prime to get to true color. Also, make sure rhe lids snap when you put them back on so they don't leak. I still give the pens 5 stars and will buy again!

rating 4.0. Good addition to craft cabinet So far we are enjoying the paint pens. It is easier to do that small detail work with the pen than with a brush. I noticed that it is hard to use on some ceramic pieces though.

rating 4.0. Color naming is very confusing. I’m a professional artist and I use these pens for some of my work. When I first started buying the pens, I was very confused about the color labeling. Each pack uses different and confusing color names for the same pens, and the colors on the boxes are off. I have made a basic grid to show the difference with some of the pens I purchased. I would really love to see artistro get some consistency in the naming of the colors.
In terms of performance, the product is fun to use because most colors are opaque in one or two passes, which can be hard to achieve with many acrylic paints that flow this smoothly.
The main disappointment I have is the complete lack of a warm dark green, like a forest green you would want to use for tree shadows. The dark green here is very blue and I usually need to switch to paints because it isn’t usable in landscapes.
The product “Paint Marker Pen” that uses a yellow box and yellow graphics in the pens has a thinner formula and the white is not opaque. But you can usually get coverage if you give it time to dry and add a little more.
rating 2.0. Can’t be used when put into a kiln. Colors great on glass, however it disappears when fired in the kiln. 😕

rating 1.0. Don't try to use for stained glass I bought these because a review said they were great for glass.  BUT, after I applied to the glass, allowed it to dry, then took my glass to the grinder, the water from the grinder washed off the paint completely.  Having to start all over again to get pattern on the glass, that presents time problems, duplicitous work, and frustration.  Won't be buying these again for this particular application.

rating 2.0. Meh? These markers were very fine tipped and not at all what one would imagine a paint marker to be. rating 3.0. Some Good and Some Bad Okay, so it takes a LONG Time to get this product to "Ship" to you.  It sits in the "label printed" for at least 2 weeks.  I'm guessing that it is being shipped from China during this time, but nobody confirms this at all.  They just say "So Sorry, COVID delays".  What a cop out.  Anyhow, once it's shipped, it takes about 3 days to arrive (typical).  It is well packaged and heavy, so that is good.  The instructions are garbage!  you get better instructions if you google YouTube videos for instructions for heat presses.  It works just fine - it seems to have even heating across the board on the main press.  I haven't tried the other accessories yet, but I don't have any reason to doubt they work fine as well.  Do like that this one came with two of the teflon protective sheets on it.  Seller (QHTEK) is a company in China, so don't let any references to a USA based shipper fool you.  If you can wait for it to get here, you are good to go with this one.  All in all, I am happy with it, but come on now, I'm not stupid enough to think there were COVID delays for shipping.  It's a drop ship import product from China.  Just tell us that up front.

input: rating 5.0. 80 distinct colors Glitter pens n so many colors! It seemed to have duplicates on the outside, but the actual colors are unique. You will need to swatch these because the caps do not necessarily reflect the actual ink color. They come in a nice plastic case that is lightweight to carry, thereby making these portable. There is a nice range of blues, greens, pinks, yellows and neutrals. The neons are not obnoxious and, overall, the collection is very nice. If you like glitter, glitter, glitter, this set is it!

#superdoodleglittergelpens #superdoodle

***Another update***
I decided to give them another try on a different page. Not sure if it is the pack that I got or what but this time I didnt run into any issues with the ink running out before I was able to complete my picture this time. rating 3.0. Amount of ink in tank I am only giving this 3 stars because when I colorered one page of a woman with ( I used the brown for her hair) the medium brown gel pen I all most ran out of ink. I lightened my hand on some parts of her hair to be sure I got full coverage and used two ther browns to fill in thoise spots. At that even with filling in those two pens I was shocked at how much ink has been used. I was under the impression these pens had more ink in them than the average gel pen.
On another picture I colored I used what I call Grayish green to color a tree full of apples and three basket full of the same apples, I used up the entire contents of this gel pen on this one page of apples.
I do love how the Super Doodles go on the paper and the ease of using them, the coverage is always nice and smoothe.
The only real complaint I have is not nearly enough ink for coloring more than one page that uses basicly the same color through out the whole page. I don't use a heavy hand when I color either that's why I really liked how these covered, with most pens I must go over the area I an coloring a couple of times to get an even fill.
Would I buy these again??? Well I ould have to consider just what I want to use them on a head of time.. As I wouldn't want to run out of the color I am using again..

rating 5.0. Totally LOVE my glitter pens! I got mine and spent the first night just looking at them, SO PRETTY! Whoever came up with that case design should get a giant cookie with her name on it! The only way they could be improved on would be to have ink shafts that hold twice as much ink. Oh, and one other thing, olive-drab green or OD green, is still an ugly color even if you put glitter in it. rating 5.0. Good tool I was considering buying some clover tape makers before I found this deal at a better price for 3.  I've messed around with them a bit so far and I am satisfied with them.  It was a bit tricky for me to understand as there are no directions and I did not watch any videos when I started.  I noticed the tape can get a bit bunched up with thin fabric and you have to take care about keeping it even when it enters the wide side of the tool but other than that I'm glad to have them over measuring and pressing tape by hand.

rating 5.0. It is so fast and easy now that I got the hang of it I will never buy store bought bias tape again.  This tool makes it simple to make your own bias tape.  It does make single fold tape, but it's simple to turn into double fold...after you make your single fold, simply fold the bias tape in half length wise and iron.  To do double fold, you need to use 1 size up tip.  For example for 1/2 inch double fold tape (what I most commonly use) I cut 2 inch strips (on the bias), use the 1 inch single fold bias tape maker, then fold in half and iron.  It is so fast and easy now that I got the hang of it. It really expands what you can use for bias tape.  And if you pre wash your fabric, no worry on shrinking or bleeding like some pre packaged tape.

rating 5.0. Great little glue gun!  I love mine! I have had this glue gun for almost two years now and, though I have a number of others, this one is my favorite.  I have used it for hours at a time on paper flower projects.  Yes, you'll use up batteries, but I view that as the reason I chose to use this glue gun in the first place.  If I wanted to have to deal with a cord, I wouldn't have purchased a "cordless" glue gun.  I just carry extra batteries with me when I plan to use it for several hours at a time.  Yes, it takes a little longer than a corded glue gun to heat up.  But that's not an issue for me either.  I am not going to do my crafts at the speed of light.  I am teaching classes now and am buying a few more of these.  I don't have to worry about electrical outlets, extension cords and tripping hazards.  It is a perfect gun for working with paper.  Still hot enough to burn the fingers if you're not careful and heats mini glue sticks to perfect temperature for quick drying action.

input:
rating 3.0. Shockingly low-quality item from a typically great brand. I was really, really disappointed in these... I've used Prismacolor products for years, and they've always been top of the line.

The problem I had with these was that they seemed too... dry. They require a lot of pressure, don't blend very seamlessly, and often leave scratches in the paper.

I understand that cross-spectrum blends aren't going to have a perfect gradation, but I was hoping to use these on large areas of a single color to smooth out and blur the strokes. This was not possible.

it's hard to describe, but using this blender is just ... rough.
There's a certain feel to a colored pencil that draws a smooth, soft line.
It's a very satisfying feeling and everyone who's used Prismacolor colored pencils knows it.
Compared to that feeling, these blenders gave the effect of nails scratching on a chalkboard.

I've never used any other blenders before, so I don't know if they're all like this, but honestly, after trying this, I couldn't help but wonder if this was a counterfeit -someone selling a lesser brand under Prismacolor's name- and part of me thinks maybe it was some fluke, there was an error in the factory the day this particular set was made, the chemicals were mixed wrong or something. It just boggles me that a brand I've come to consider top-notch has put out something this cheap.

Someone else said in a review they used a white colored pencil for blending before switching to this, and how it's an improvement because you don't have to worry about whitening your colors... I was hoping to make that switch too, but I'm gonna have to stick with the white. This blender is NOT smooth. I was hoping this would have the consistency/texture of the prismacolor premier colored pencils, but honestly this feels like it could have come from a pack at a dollar store.

*I'll add that I used these with Art Stix and Staedtler colored pencils, and maybe that's not what they are intended for, but art six were advertised as being made of the same substance inside prismacolor colored pencils, so one would think these would work well for that.

Then I blend with the blending pencil and the color spreads and gets darker. Now that I've started using these I can't imagine how I got along without them.

rating 5.0. One of my favorite helps for coloring I love these blenders. This is my fourth set. They do a lovely job on artist grade pencils of blending colors more smoothly together.

I showed a picture of the two sets that arrived. The sharpened pencils next to them are the reason I needed to order.

The picture is one I used these blender pencils on. It is from Magical Jungle by Joanna Basford. The pencils did a incredible job on all the blending and shading I do in my coloring. I even used them on the background. Many people use other mediums for blending, but this is the one that pleases me.

The other picture is out of a book called Colouring Greece by Konstantinos Liaramantzas. It made the picture so much better to use these.

I highly recommend these for adult colorists who are serious about their artwork, or the person that colors to relax. They will enhance your work. Would buy again! Love, love them.

rating 2.0. BE AWARE~ Not What It Seems! The Huge Issue with this elastic cord is that it is NOT Measured (the size of the thread is stated 1MM, but is Much Closer to 0.5MM, half the size which could be described as the circumference) in the apparent standard manner that other very popular brands are measured.  Beadalon & Powercord are sold at pretty much all jewelry supply sites, whereas I've never heard of Beadnova until now.  Sadly, the reviews on this particular item were too vague so I thought I'd take a chance and try it.  As Beadnova stands by their product, I am returning this thread and receiving a refund.  But I think it's important that anyone else looking into buying this product gets a proper perspective on exactly what they are receiving.  I think that the photo will show the huge difference between the sizes of these different elastic cords which matters greatly when you are using larger beads that are heavier and have larger holes. (1 pic of the cords shows side by side, the other shows a cross section.) Tons of beads come with a minimum size whole of 1mm, and some as you can see in the picture come with 2mm-3mm (not including "Pandora" style beads as that's a totally different idea), so a thread as thin as the Beadnova cannot support a large percentage of the beads that I use.  Even doubling the thread, as someone had suggested, will not work properly.  I sell my creations and it is Important to me to have the jewelry be as purrfect as I can make it and be aware of possible issues.  Please understand, I am not knocking this product, just saying that the way it is being advertised is very misleading.  Thank you!

rating 4.0. Worked great! I spent two weeks researching which heat press ... I have only used this heat press twice so far. As soon as it was delivered I plugged it in, let it heat up and did a test sample. Worked great! I spent two weeks researching which heat press to purchase, reading reviews and watching videos. It was so overwhelming making a choice. The reason I chose this press was the price. From my research I knew I wanted to learn on an inexpensive press. This press is very very small and light weight. I will be upgrading in the near future to a 15x15 but for now this will work for small projects. The small size will come in handy if you want to take it with you to a friends for craft night. I tried out the teflon sheet that came with the press. I must have used the wrong side because it stuck to the top of press. I am used to just using a piece of cottton material on top of HTV anyway so thats what I did when making two shirts with this press. I guess it's just up to personal preference. I have read some reviews of other heat presses catching on fire. I have had my iron do this so to prevent any possibility of this my husband ran a 110/30amp plug on its own breaker for me to plug my heat press in. I recommend this because the press gets very hot. It took 20 minutes to completely heat up. I used this time to prepare my HTV and shirt. I am including a picture of the first shirt I did to try out the press. Ignore the wrinkles, I was just practicing.

rating 2.0. Too bad this didn't live up to my expectations I was so excited to finally get a heat press. Too bad this didn't live up to my expectations. First of all, if you do adult t-shirts...go bigger! I know its more expensive, but its quite the hassle to have to press in stages. Second, the cord is so short that you don't have any freedom of moving too far from an outlet (basically right next to it) because the swing arm takes the cord with it as it rotates. Third, the handle is so stiff that i practically have to put all of my weight on it to get it to lock closed, so getting enough pressure is practically out of the question because by the time i get it locked, its time to lift again. I'm a young and fairly strong woman, this should not be so difficult. Oh, and if you don't push down enough, it won't go back up, so if you decide half way down that you need to adjust something, you're already screwed. (Maybe this is just a fault of my specific machine.) Fourth, it doesn't automatically go into the up position in lock. Meaning, you have to make sure you put the handle all of the way up to the lock position entirely, or it will fall right back down and potentially damage your shirt by putting heat on vinyl that isn't covered, or your hand if you're trying to move the shirt off the base. Fifth, heat is dangerous and this thing is supposed to get hot, I get that, but i can barely get the arm completely twisted out of the way in order to place a shirt down. Sixth, make sure you have ample space behind the press so that you can swing the arm all of the way out of the way. If you don't have the space, get a clam shell style (I'm wishing I had.) Seventh, the foam pad isn't securely glued down all of the way around, my shirts catch the edge and lift it further, I've used this machine for 1 day, I did 7 shirts in a row. At this rate, the pad will probably be completely off by the end of the year. Finally, eighth, there is absolutely no instructions on how to use this properly. Truly, I had to look it up on youtube, and watch someone else do it on a similar machine. And I still have questions about using it. It's like they assume you already know how to use a heat press. There is an instruction booklet that is 4 or 6 pages long, and there is more empty space than instructions within.

I have one pro to the heat press...it is generally faster than using an iron.

rating 5.0. So far so good.. So far so good... I have only had this for a few weeks but I love it so far! I use it to press vinyl onto onesies and shirts and it works like a charm! I was using an iron before and my vinyl wasn't staying on because it wasn't hot enough or it would put bubbles in my vinyl... This makes doing all my shirts super fast! Heats up very fast, only have to apply heat for about 15 seconds and I'm done! When I was using the iron it would take about 20 mins to get vinyl to stick and sometimes it would start to peel off later on. I was able to use this press to fix a few shirts that were starting to peel. Love it! Only thing is I wish it was a little bigger so I could do the whole design on an adult shirt at once.

rating 5.0. I love this set I love this set, it has everything I need plus it comes in a nice little case, perfect!! Great customer service as well. Castle Art Supplies has already emailed me to make sure I am happy with their product, I really liked that they took the time to do that. :)

rating 5.0. Great product for a tight budget Excellent quality for beginners and those on a tighter budget. Not the greatest pencils, but they are worth more than their value. Customer service is excellent. I received a personal email when I made the purchase, and another after it arrived. I informed them one of the charcoal pencils kept breaking and they sent me an entire new set package without any extra cost. I advised them to ship them more securely. The first one had arrived in a box with two other items and a couple bubble things to secure things, but everything got knocked around. This resulted in the cores breaking. The second arrived in a bubble envelope with no knocking around. I can sharpen everything without breaks.

The packaging is NOT storage. I can't believe how many people think it's storage. Honestly, it's just cute packaging. Nothing about it says it's a place to store anything. Get a proper pencil box.

It comes with a kneaded eraser, and it's of great quality. The pencil sharpeners are top-of-the-line, but they're good quality. If the blade is dull you can easily get a new sharpener or even just blades.

rating 4.0. Good for beginners planning to keep going... First let me tell you a little about the kind of person I am when I am serious about a new undertaking... I *HATE* note being good at something right away and I HATE not having everything possible to make me good fast..... For example, I learned to be a vinyl DJ with over 1000 recrods and a top of the line dj booth in my house in 6 months compared to the 2 years it takes most people because I GET AND USE EVERYTHING..... I do not limit myself when it comes to ANY item, informational resource, or accessory that MAY help me move along faster than expected....

AND THEN I REGISTERED FOR A DRAWING CLASS... and lemme tell you, I can't draw a friggin Balloon or a Red Ball, even though I know a lot of art THEORY and what one SHOULD do (though I can't....)

As a regular "art doer" but beginner as an artist (you know what I mean) I bought this for a 15 session x 3 hour Intro to Drawing class at an Art Center near Yale University in CT.... the course instructions said to buy "1 Soft Graphite Pencil, Vine Charcoal, Pencil Sharpener, Eraser and Clips" It also said Some Paper Will Be Provided". Below I'll list what I bought after a WHOLE DAY questioning artist friends, reviewing the H vs B pencil designations and so on.

This Castle Art Supplies Drawing And Sketching Pencil Art Set (26 Pieces) was what I decided to buy INSTEAD of buying 1 soft (b) pencil... since I plan to draw and draw every day until I am excellent, I know I'll eventually use all of these pencils and already, just drawing vanishing point lines lightly vs the lines of that DARN cube, I"m using several #s of B pencil. I'd say this is a GREAT SET for a beginner who is SURE they will go on with drawing past the very basics. '

[[ASIN:B00K7KT2E8 Castle Art Supplies Drawing and Sketching Pencil Art Set (26 Items)]]

There's a few things I'd like to see improved but I give it a for the range and quality of the Graphite Pencils, I"d go with a 4.5 if I could. I'd prefer it came in a rolling leather over a flimsy box that's not really reusable... if you're taking it on the road with you, you'll want to buy something else.. I'm using notebook pencil case inserts like there but I'll probably get a roll... https://www.amazon.com/Bazic-3-Ring-Pencil-Window-803/dp/B003BMX80S/ref=as_li_ss_tl?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_i=B003BMX80S&pd_rd_r=K81W2K83BHZ2HBWMV43D&pd_rd_w=7Sj3x&pd_rd_wg=e3fqw&psc=1&refRID=K81W2K83BHZ2HBWMV43D&linkCode=ll1&tag=jocelyn0ab-20&linkId=86f4d450cd357b4923390394714c1f56

I don't love the pencil and charcoal sharpener only because they are so little that they are hard to find and they don't catch the shavings.... Also, if you give me a white plastic eraser PLEASE give me a smudge proof brush! And finally, just a thought.. a t-square would be a nice addition to ANY artist set.

rating 5.0. Returning artist chooses these high quality pencils over other more colorful choices My husband made a career as an artist then suffered a debilitating accident. In an attempt to reintroduce art into his world I presented him with a variety of supplies from which to choose: sketch pads and books, colorful and interesting pencils, pens, watercolors, et cetera. At the last minute I impulsively ordered the 26 Piece Drawing and Sketching Pencil Art Set from Castle Art Supplies as it was in the "Others who bought..."  category. I was surprised to watch my husband's face as he looked at the plethora of art supplies spread before him. His head moved back and forth as his eyes took in everything, then he set his gaze on the pencil set, which in my non artist's view was the least attractive of the choices. For several days now he is choosing to sketch something using only the pencils from this set. The original product description had referred to "returning artists" and I must say that in this case the high quality of the pencils in this set is returning an artist to his former self. Thank you Castle Art Supplies for offering such a great product at a truly affordable price. We will be purchasing from you in the future.

rating 4.0. Charcoal pencil sharpener too small for the pencils In preparing to teach a landscape drawing class this summer to twenty students, I ordered and have now received five different drawing sets from Amazon, two of which are likely by the same or related companies because they are that similar.  The "26 Piece Premium Drawing Set" by Castle Art Supplies would likely be in the running except for the fact that the diameter of the charcoal pencils (sent unsharpened because otherwise they'd probably arrive with their sharpened points broken off) is too large to fit into the plastic sharpener's entry point.  I tried the metal sharpener and found it to be the same.  The other set which is identical but costs $3.00 less carries the same plastic sharpener or facsimile, and that set's sharpener did fit their charcoal pencils and did an excellent job of it.  The charcoal pencils in both sets seem to be the same, only with different company's names embossed on them, and appear to be good quality pencils.  I checked the ends of the pencils to look at the placement of the charcoals in their wooden casings and noted that they were perfectly centered in the middle -- always a good sign, and, when I did sharpen the other set, the charcoal held tight -- didn't move in any way as to suggest inner breakage or anything.  This set has passed my own rigid tests and has such a good variety of mediums as to warrant five stars from me.  You'll just need to have a larger sharpener nearby for the charcoal pencils.  Then again, I could have just gotten the bad-seed sharpener off the assembly line, and maybe other people will get all of what they paid for.  My only other issue with the set is the wording used to describe the woodless 6B graphite pencil.  Its description reads: "1 soft graphite sketching pencil."  Most people I've encountered have been surprised that a woodless pencil even exists, but, in my own work, I decided some time ago to use only woodless pencils.  They are wonderful to work with from the initial strokes on the paper all the way down to your signature line.  They hold well, save on excessive graphite shavings, and allow for broad marks as well as sharp pointed ones due to the lack of a wood casing.  So, this isn't just a "sketching" pencil; woodless pencils are for everything.

rating 5.0. Very nice. I'm a retired engineer Very nice. I'm a retired engineer. I know how to draw engineering drawings by hand from the old days as well as by CAD but decided to take a shot at art. This little set seems to be just the thing for someone like me. It comes with all the graphite pencils recommended by my course as well as charcoal and graphite sticks. It also has the proper sharpeners and erasers. The entire package is much less expensive than purchasing just the graphite pencils individually.

The graphite pencils seem to be of reasonable quality although I am just getting started. I really have nothing to compare to except No. 2 pencils.

I read some complaints about the case. True enough, it is more of a molded plastic shipping case to protect the pencils during transit than a daily use case. It would be nice if one is traveling about but probably not practical for daily use. The portable easel I use has a drawer for pencils so I don't need the case. Get a pencil box for a couple of bucks from the dollar store or Wal Mart, line it with foam or paper towels if you want something better. Forget the case, the kit is a bargain.

rating 5.0. Great for price and beginner levels [[VIDEOID:ac249a09d1d478c59246561ce3ff19ab]] I bought a multipack of canvases. If you’re just getting into it, it is a lot of fun. I recommend getting a cup. I use one that is about 6-8oz. This canvas was 8x8 for reference.
To do this I “stacked” the paint. (Fancy word for pouring the paint in a cup on top of each color)

It worked best when I tilted the cup so I could see the colors in a row, if may work better if you decide to do it a different way, I only say what worked for me.

I did it in a pattern like dark blue, silver, light blue, white, light blue, silver, dark blue white.

Do what works for you and what you want to do. I found layering with the white the colors didn’t bleed into eachother and look blotchy... then again, this is my third one, so I am still learning.
I haven’t experimented with allot of other paint company’s yet..
this one for the price is great and I felt like o got my money’s worth. These are two ounce bottles. You’d be surprised how little paint it takes.

rating 4.0. Ultra ShapeXpress This is very tricky to use. You have to use enough force but not too much. It is very easy to use free hand to make doodle like cuts. The problem comes when you're trying to use templates. I thought I was using moderate pressure but my cardstock kept tearing and ripping when I got to the corners. Then I used less pressure and it seemed to work but then sometimes it didn't cut through. Very frustrating!

So here is what you need to do:
Watch videos on YouTube which explain how to use it.

You need to be clear on how to open and place your blades and lock them into position.

You also need to remove the protective guard before you use it free style and then to remove the freehand guard when using a template. Furthermore you have to place your template with the right side facing up. Make sure you can properly read the word Fiskars.

Then don't use too much pressure and not too little.

Almost forgot you can control how deep the blade goes as well. There is a control above the blade locking mechanism for that.

So bottom line: Practice! Once you get the hang of it it will make using templates easy.

rating 4.0. I'm impressed -- but still getting used to it I just received the Ultra ShapeExpress today. I was a little nervous after reading the detailed negative reviews here and elsewhere.

I was pleasantly surprised! Right out of the box, I was able to cut out quite small circles (less than an inch across), hearts and floral shapes -- all freehand. I was even able to cut materials that a box knife tore to shreds: sticker paper and metallic Christmas ribbon.

I've noticed that some folks have had trouble getting the knife to align by dragging along the cutting mat, as the manufacturer directs. I think this is probably only a problem when cutting thicker paper, like cardstock. There's a spring in the handle that maintains the depth of the blade, and it presses down more tightly when set for thicker paper, which means there's less "give" for the blade to turn. If you twist the paper-thickness-setter-thingy back towards "thinner," it frees up the blade. You can drag to align the blade, reset for your thick paper, and resume cutting.

Two things I have noticed: It doesn't work very well for really sharp angles or very tight curves. You can do these in sections: Cut, begin your curve, withdraw and realign, resume.

I haven't yet been able to cut right-angles or sharp zigzags, but that may come. Anyway, that's why rulers and craft knives are still made :)

All in all, an inexpensive and satisfactory "fix" for those who need to talk themselves out of buying an expensive die-cutting machine.

rating 5.0. Great Ruler for Beginning Quilters This ruler is FANTASTIC. I just started quilting and had considered getting a smaller ruler, but this one is the one for newbies to get. rating 1.0. Not as pictured The item I received matched the item number that is shown - 12-87577097J - but it is NOT as pictured. The markings are pink and white (which makes it very hard to see through) and the label says "coastal colors". I've had a Fiskars clear 6 x 24 ruler that is exactly as pictured for about 20 years and recently broke a corner off when I dropped it, so I wanted to replace it with the exact same item. I'm returning the coastal colors ruler.
The Fiskars site shows the orange and black ruler as Item #: 187620-1001. I called their customer service to be sure I would receive the exact item I wanted, if I ordered it, and they actually offered to send me a correct replacement at no charge.

rating 1.0. Item came broken and bent. I waited all weekend ... Item came broken and bent. rating 4.0. Nicks and Divots Over Time I thought this ruler was a good value. The product was as advertised and lasted a long time. I say 'a long time' and not forever, despite the 'Lifetime Warranty.' I used this with a rotary cutter and added a suction-cup handle to it for cutting fabric. Worked great, but after several months the rotary blade made little 'micro-cuts' into the plastic of the ruler. Over time these cuts started to catch the rotary blade, which not only chipped out even bigger divots, but really started to shorten the life of those expensive rotary blades.

At first I thought the blades were just getting dull because I was cutting upholstery grade fabric, but then I realized that the fault was with the chinks in the ruler itself. I think this ruler is a good value, but if it's advertised with a 'Lifetime Warranty,' I'm not sure I should have to count on buying a new one every six months to preserve the sharpness of my rotary cutter blades.
rating 1.0. grid ruler It got pretty scratched up over time.  The corner broke off.  Fiskar does not seem to be in any hurry to honor the lifetime warranty on it.  Will not buy this brand again.

rating 5.0. I was super surprised to receive this sooo quickly I am loving this ruler, folks.  They shipped this fast.  I was super surprised to receive this sooo quickly. This ruler is of high quality.  It is 1/8th of an inch in thickness. This, along with the fact that it is slightly flexible, NOT FLIMSY/ FLOPPY, helps keep it from being easily broken. The weight of this ruler is excellent for those of us who have wrist problems or previous wrist injuries.  It came in a cardboard box that was secure.  So, you won't have to worry about damage to your ruler.  And the fact that it is delivered so fast, cuts down on the chances that something could happen to it in an extended situation.  The ruler does have hot pink to red numbers and line increments that are highlighted by white to make it a little bit better to see or to make it easier on the eyes.  I might add, this is , indeed, very effective too.  When marking according to a line increment, keep your eyes on the red line or hot pink line that is being highlighted because that is the actual increment---1. 1/8th, 1/4th, etc...., not the white highlight.  'Cause it's just the highlight, folks.  Now, the lines that are running vertical the full length of the ruler are not highlighted. And really, they don't need to be.  That would be overdoing it.  I hope this made sense and was helpful.

rating 1.0. You will not receive the item pictured. The ruler I received is almost impossible to use. The measurement lines are pink with thick white borders all the way around them. This border makes the pink lines essentially useless, removing the possibility of using this ruler for—you know—actual measuring. Including that, here are the features that are immediately, recognizably different from the advertised ruler:

-Numbers and measuring lines are all pink, not red and black.
-Numbers and lines are all surrounded by a thick white border.
-The hole above the logo at the top of the ruler is a single, obviously drilled, 1/4” circle, not a 1&1/2” triangle.
-The numbers for 30, 45 and 60 degree angles are surrounded by a white, filled in box, not a red circle.
-The depicted board says made in China, the received ruler does not say where it was made.

rating 5.0. So fast and easy and scary sharp There are some things I will never use scissors for again! This cuts through fabrics of all types (cotton, silk dupioni, polypropylene and heavy duty nylon webbing, fleece and faux furs) like butter. I automatically set it up for lefty-ness and it is so easy to use, especially along a ruler. I got a suction cup handle for hand safety and I'm glad I did. The blades it came with are super sharp, it comes with a nice little blade holder, and the blades I got for replacement after that are Famore 45mm Professional Tungsten Carbide Rotary Blades.

The hole on the back makes hanging it on my thread rack easy and the safety lock is nice. It's comfortable to use and the perfect size for not only straight cuts but gentle curves as well. I like it so much I'm buying another for work for staff to cut surgical drapes with (they could do 8 layers at a time and be done in a minute instead of hacking away at each one with dull scissors!)

rating 4.0. Fits Brother SA156 but foam inserts not snug in case I got this item and a two pack of the New Phase Bobbin boxes (https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001R4T4VE/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_search_asin_title?ie=UTF8&psc=1) which hold 56 total.

This item's case latches a little difficult but once latched it is secure. The case is fine and absolutely sturdy enough. There is a divider in the middle but the two foam sheets with holes do not fit securely/snugly and mine lift and move freely inside the case. The slots are a hair too wide for the height of the bobbins, they go in easily but aren't held in place snugly. The slots of this one are also longer so the edges don't actually touch.

This case holds the most I've seen but it's not snug or secure even with full bobbins and I find myself using/preferring the other type, whose case latches are easier too.
